emails.subject: ENC: LATAM Week Ahead: MPC Meetings in Mexico (-25bp to 6.75%) and Chile (Hold at 4.5%); MPC Minutes and MPR in Brazil; IPoM in Chile
emails.one_liner: LATAM Week Ahead: México -25bp (6.75%), Chile hold (4.5%). Brasil MPC Minutes/MPR.
emails.body:
________________________________

De: GS Macro Economics Research <macro@alerts.publishing.gs.com>
Enviado: sexta-feira, 20 de março de 2026 18:09:36 (UTC-03:00) Brasilia
Para: Sell Side <sellside@torkcapital.com.br>
Assunto: LATAM Week Ahead: MPC Meetings in Mexico (-25bp to 6.75%) and Chile (Hold at 4.5%); MPC Minutes and MPR in Brazil; IPoM in Chile


 	 
 	 
	 
 	 Print PDF <https://publishing.gs.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fpublishing.gs.com%2FtrustedLink%3Fopentoken=T1RLAQKsg6uNUfngDzRiwQ60yy6G65ID2hDCqVC4Hf7N2kp6IlXv6l_eAACwm1dWGwjbEAnLWYN69P5GsoGbAWH9E_tubaB_qTFAMO8zwJU-7T43vfSCJjxJ1VOMPzanZygxj4GBgtzEQH_C6CpoNOcdPZerWO0LIXz0BRgG5oP5ONlwXEuFx6pA_iz9nJIHvw_cdHqzDJr0TqkJ3GB605jyO09X_TZazsM-xCrFjCwYcuNVLH1dvQ9rjV8nUALYUz3tjbluPZ9_II4Vn-lbww3tLp6SRVkzzuKXmRA*%26id=91437492-896b-4616-b351-570655e6fbd5%26utm_medium=pa%26renditionType=pdf/1/0100019d0d150d3c-1a50b5da-ba3a-4784-b82f-d93f0d4b93b3-000000/A0sXFC0xV5QNbUKQ63eHMsW-b8OZbrEUDRU4LAW9T44=449>  	 	 | 	 	 Read <https://publishing.gs.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fpublishing.gs.com%2FtrustedLink%3Fopentoken=T1RLAQKsg6uNUfngDzRiwQ60yy6G65ID2hDCqVC4Hf7N2kp6IlXv6l_eAACwm1dWGwjbEAnLWYN69P5GsoGbAWH9E_tubaB_qTFAMO8zwJU-7T43vfSCJjxJ1VOMPzanZygxj4GBgtzEQH_C6CpoNOcdPZerWO0LIXz0BRgG5oP5ONlwXEuFx6pA_iz9nJIHvw_cdHqzDJr0TqkJ3GB605jyO09X_TZazsM-xCrFjCwYcuNVLH1dvQ9rjV8nUALYUz3tjbluPZ9_II4Vn-lbww3tLp6SRVkzzuKXmRA*%26id=91437492-896b-4616-b351-570655e6fbd5%26utm_medium=pa/1/0100019d0d150d3c-1a50b5da-ba3a-4784-b82f-d93f0d4b93b3-000000/VansUFPVEWkWnPXEx6PbK0mNXnInCB2FVTuixzdjvQQ=449>  	
 	 
	
 	 
LATAM Week Ahead: MPC Meetings in Mexico (-25bp to 6.75%) and Chile (Hold at 4.5%); MPC Minutes and MPR in Brazil; IPoM in Chile 
	
20 March 2026 | 5:04PM EDT | Research | Economics 	
	
	
[1] In Mexico, the central bank Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) will announce its rate decision on March 26 at 3:00pm EST. At the Feb 5 meeting the forward guidance turned clearly dovish, hinting that a rate cut would be considered at the March meeting (“looking ahead, the MPC will evaluate enacting additional rate cuts”). In the meantime, the central bank baseline scenario was upended by the conflict in the Middle East which led to a sharp increase in oil prices, strong USD, bear flattening of the US curve (with higher real rates reflecting a hawkish policy shock), rising risk-aversion, and tighter financial conditions. All amidst heightened global geopolitical risk.

A Hawkish Cut. We are leaning (barely so) towards a 25bp rate cut, to 6.75%, given the consistently dovish guidance and Banxico-speak, just mild MXN depreciation since the last meeting, significant tightening of domestic financial conditions, softening outlook for US and global demand, and the fact that, so far, the impact of the early excise tax and import tariff hikes seems to have been contained. Furthermore, the majority of the MPC directors do not seem overly concerned with the current inflation pressures and above-target inflation, choosing to focus instead on the soft growth outlook (large negative output gap) and the contribution to the inflation dynamics of a strong MXN. In addition, the risk of a major spike in fuel prices in the short term is contained by the fact that the government reached a voluntary 6-month agreement with gas stations to cap gasoline prices and has lowered the IEPS excise tax on fuel sales.

In case of a cut, we anticipate a split decision (with director Jonathan Heath voting for a hold) and the central bank raising the bar for another cut in May (hawkish cut).

Our degree of conviction that the central bank will cut is not high. In fact, we assess a high 40% probability of no cut in a nod to the ongoing oil price supply shock and heightened uncertainty. If the MXN/USD exchange rate keeps depreciating next week and Tuesday's first-half March core CPI is much higher than expected, the likelihood of holding a 7.00% rate will increase, eventually becoming the most probable outcome.

Since the last meeting MXN/USD weakened (to 17.96 from around 17.33 ahead of the Feb 5 meeting), oil prices (Brent) are up approximately 65% (Brent US$111.2 barrel), and 1- and 10-year UST rates moved by up by 39bp/+21bp to 3.80%/4.39%, and 5-year CDS spreads widened by 24bp to 109. Headline and core inflation expectations for 2026 deteriorated (+5/+6bp to 4.00%/4.17%) and the median expectation for growth in 20206 rose by 20bp to 1.50%.

The inflation backdrop remains challenging. Annual headline inflation is now tracking at 4.13%, above the upper-limit of the inflation target band. Tax and import tariff hikes pressured core-food in Jan, with the impact tapering during Feb. The normalization of non-core inflation from very low prints (perishable food prices in particular) is now driving headline inflation up; inflation among freely-determined prices accelerated to 4.60% yoy, with core inflation tracking at a high 4.48% yoy. In addition, high labor costs are likely to keep services inflation high in the 4%-handle

emails.subject: ENC: ...it's a wrap...the most contrarian trade...receive rates (long TLT) and short equities (SPX)...
emails.one_liner: Contrarian trade idea: long TLT, short SPX, based on Fed reactions to oil price shifts (upside ignored, downside spurs cuts).
emails.body:
________________________________

De: Franco, Claudio <claudio.franco@bofa.com>
Enviado: sexta-feira, 20 de março de 2026 17:43:06 (UTC-03:00) Brasilia
Para: Sell Side <sellside@torkcapital.com.br>
Assunto: ...it's a wrap...the most contrarian trade...receive rates (long TLT) and short equities (SPX)...



This is Sales Commentary Only, Not Research. 

 

Why long TLT (20+ Year Treasury Bond ETF ) vs short SPX can be one of the most contrarian trades today…

 

Something to reflect about…global markets are focused on upside inflation risk and generally dismissive of downside growth risks…

 

Mark Cabana <https://rsch.baml.com/r?q=XabmIyxEPs9Dll-Z2q7Xwg&e=claudio.franco%40bofa.com&h=mhTtQQ>  says something interesting…history says the Fed typically looks through upside oil shocks and responds to downside oil drops…

 

Upside oil shock = Fed dual mandate is in conflict via higher inflation and downside growth/labor risk…Fed typically does nothing and waits…

 

Downside oil drop = lower oil typically occurs with elevated growth concerns, which results in alignment of Fed dual mandate via lower inflation and downside growth / labor risk. Fed typically cuts quickly.

 

TLT vs SPX…a level to o keep an eye on…



 

 



 

 



 

 

CF

emails.subject: ENC: RADL/HYPE: GLP-1 Trends in Brazil - Feedback From Close-Up International Webinar
emails.one_liner: JPM analisa tendências GLP-1 no Brasil, com Tirzepatide (65% share) liderando rápido crescimento do mercado farmacêutico.
emails.body:
________________________________

De: Joseph Giordano <joseph.giordano@jpmresearchmail.com>
Enviado: sexta-feira, 20 de março de 2026 16:49:15 (UTC-03:00) Brasilia
Para: Sell Side <sellside@torkcapital.com.br>
Assunto: RADL/HYPE: GLP-1 Trends in Brazil - Feedback From Close-Up International Webinar


 	Latin America Equity Research 	

Hi Tork,

The Extel survey (formerly Institutional Investor) is open through March 20 and is VERY important to us. Please support the J.P. Morgan RETAIL & HEALTHCARE team by giving a 5-star rating. We are focused on the LATIN AMERICA survey (not the Brazil survey). To vote, please click the banner below.

Thanks!
Joe & team



Brazil Pharma & Drug Retail


GLP-1 Trends in Brazil - Feedback From Close-Up International Webinar

 	 
 <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12836626&ad=4074336563&h=bZBR4X0&sig=7daMp7fQoXanTQvEDyJYLUHceU0&v=2&url=https://www.extelinsights.com/voting> 	
By Joseph Giordano AC <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12836626&ad=4074336563&h=1oafVmh&sig=RKKPJ5GhQ5VMzqSEOIX5Hvjsrpn&v=2&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/analyst/R103209> , Guilherme A. Vilela, CFA <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12836626&ad=4074336563&h=O-axWR_&sig=YKJRKsWqgQG950o1oueIz5a8Xnd&v=2&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/analyst/R720687> , Nicolas Larrain <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12836626&ad=4074336563&h=5k1WB8K&sig=5mED4PHYGUeNJtLaUumXMWKPqSi&v=2&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/analyst/R596032> , Giovanni Vescovi <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12836626&ad=4074336563&h=cUz-wv5&sig=VY0uQXOnHjbKGpX8oKNklEJVdKw&v=2&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/analyst/O798110> , Froylan Mendez <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12836626&ad=4074336563&h=OJFKx_a&sig=U7erTzmb6n4o9aMhynEXR2PPS2k&v=2&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/analyst/V579633>  

Click here for the full document and disclaimers <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12836626&ad=4074336563&h=MFPQW8Y&sig=XHJEq08SoffN4gVm5LMlhvb0Cz5&v=2&isig=LqM14PTjccHt3BBc40IHcqKbqMT&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/email/scx/i1oor6td/GPS-5242193-0/b60dce05-dfeb-4476-9a47-8074689c01e4> 

Today, we hosted Mr. Carlos Aguiar (Head of Sales ) and Mr. Filipi Campos (Head of Market Insights) from Close-Up International in Brazil. The main takeaways from the call were: 1) the Brazilian pharma market remains structurally robust, growing double-digits over the past decade, with large chains representing ~50% of sales; 2) GLP-1 products should continue to print solid growth trends, with the expiration of the patent for semaglutide (today in Brazil) being a relevant trigger to significantly increase the TAM; and 3) Tirzepatide (Mounjaro) represents today ~65% of GLP-1 sales but its growth is constrained by supply. Please find the detailed notes from the webinar below. The presentation used by Close-Up International can be provided upon request. 

*	Bottom Line. Brazilian pharma retail remains structurally robust (~US$48bn market, double-digit growth), but GLP-1 therapies have become the central driver of incremental value creation. With ~4.7% of market value and growing rapidly, the category is expanding the market, reshaping prescription behavior, and increasing channel concentration. Supply constraints, high pricing, and limited distribution suggest the market is still underpenetrated. The next phase should be driven by broader access, increased competition, and price normalization, supporting continued strong growth while gradually expanding beyond the current high-income patient base. 
*	Brazil Pharma Retail: Category Structure and Demand Profile. The Brazilian pharma retail market figures among the top globally, and has delivered consistent low double-digit value growth over the past decade. This growth is notably price/mix-driven, with unit expansion running at only ~4–5% p.a., implying strong premiumization. The market remains heavily skewed toward branded products, with prescription drugs accounting for ~50% of total value (rising to >55% when including GLP-1s), non-medication categories at ~30%, and OTC at ~14%. The out-of-pocket nature of the system continues to shape purchasing behavior, reinforcing brand preference but also embedding price sensitivity at the margin. 
*	Channel Concentration and Route-to-Market Dynamics. Brazil has a highly fragmented physical footprint (~100k pharmacies), but revenue is concentrated: large chains account for ~48% of total sales while representing only ~15% of stores. This concentration drives superior sales productivity and makes these chains the primary access point for premium therapies, such as GLP-1. Independent pharmacies still represent >50k stores, but lack the infrastructure for higher-complexity products. Meanwhile, digital penetration has accelerated meaningfully post-COVID,

emails.subject: ENC: H&E Channel: YDUQS (New buyback program); (BTG Pactual's Alves/Resende)
emails.one_liner: YDUQS anuncia novo programa de recompra de R$100mn (~4% free float), avaliado positivamente pelo BTG Pactual.
emails.body:
________________________________

De: BTG Pactual Equities Research <sh-btgpactual-research@btgpactual.com>Em Nome deSamuel Alves <samuel.alves@btgpactual.com>
Enviado: sexta-feira, 20 de março de 2026 15:46:34 (UTC-03:00) Brasilia
Assunto: H&E Channel: YDUQS (New buyback program); (BTG Pactual's Alves/Resende)


 <https://salesforce-mailing-distribution-prod.s3.sa-east-1.amazonaws.com/Mailing%20Distribution/assets/2024-07-05/1720188520532.png> 

			
#868 | Mar 19th, 2026

	
 

 

 

 

Click here for the full report » <https://www.btgpactual.com/research/report-view/118877> 

   

YDUQS: New share buyback program announced

       

Earlier this morning, YDUQS announced a new R$100mn share buyback program (up to ~9.9mn shares, ~4% of free float) to be executed over the next 18 months. The company’s previous buyback program was concluded in February 2025, under which ~30.5mn shares were repurchased (~75% of the program), totaling roughly R$300mn. See below the currently active buyback programs across our coverage.

 

The announcement follows last week’s results (see our review <https://www.btgpactual.com/research/report-view/118016> ), which were somewhat weak, with free cash flow landing near the low end of guidance (~R$500mn). For 2026, management expects FCF to be broadly stable or slightly higher y/y, with capital allocation focused on selective M&A and deleveraging (targeting ~1.0x ND/EBITDA by end-2027). The possibility of a buyback had already been flagged, making the announcement broadly expected.

 

All-in, we view the program positively as it signals confidence in the medium-term cash generation outlook and adds flexibility to shareholder returns, alongside ongoing deleveraging.

 

We maintain our Buy rating, backed by a still compelling ~18% 2026 FCF yield, although risks to our estimates have increased as early 2026.1 intake trends point to a ~7% y/y decline, with limited visibility on a rebound.

emails.subject: ENC: Obrigado pela parceria feat não é pedido de voto
emails.one_liner: Agradecimento pelo voto Extel LatAm Agribusiness/Food & Beverage e pedido de feedback sobre análises e ideias.
emails.body:
________________________________

De: Guilherme Pedroni Palhares <gpalhares@santander.com.br>
Enviado: sexta-feira, 20 de março de 2026 15:09:44 (UTC-03:00) Brasilia
Para: Sell Side <sellside@torkcapital.com.br>
Assunto: Obrigado pela parceria feat não é pedido de voto



Boa tarde! tudo bem?

 

Prometemos que este é um dos últimos emails sobre Extel, pelo menos por um tempinho.

 

Passando aqui para agradecer de verdade pelo voto em Agribusiness e Food & Beverage na pesquisa Extel LatAm, pela paciência com a temporada de emails e, principalmente, pela parceria ao longo do ano. Nosso objetivo foi seguir entregando análises que realmente ajudassem no dia a dia, com boas ideias, calls fora de consenso, contato próximo com o setor e bastante pé na estrada. 

 

E queria aproveitar para pedir um feedback rápido: o que foi mais útil para vocês este ano, stock-picking, acesso ao management, eventos ou conteúdo setorial? E onde podemos melhorar? Se puderem, respondam este email com uma linha sobre o que funcionou melhor para vocês, ou sobre o que faria mais diferença no dia a dia. Vamos gostar muito de ouvir.

 

Obrigado novamente pela confiança e pela parceria. 

Seguimos trabalhando para continuar merecendo seu voto, e tentando não abusar demais da sua caixa de entrada.

 

Abrs

 



	Santander LatAm Agribusiness & Food team

Guilherme Palhares (+55 11 98375-4238)

Laura Hirata (+55 11 97409-3318)

emails.subject: ENC: Latin America Energy: Oil: What to Buy in a mid cycle oil price environment in the USD70's range? PBR, PRIO, Vista stand out
emails.one_liner: O que comprar em ambiente de petróleo a USD70? PBR, PRIO, Vista se destacam.
emails.body:
________________________________

De: Bruno Amorim, CFA <bruno.amorim@alerts.publishing.gs.com>
Enviado: sexta-feira, 20 de março de 2026 14:57:37 (UTC-03:00) Brasilia
Para: Sell Side <sellside@torkcapital.com.br>
Assunto: Latin America Energy: Oil: What to Buy in a mid cycle oil price environment in the USD70's range? PBR, PRIO, Vista stand out


 	 
 	 
	 
 	 Print PDF <https://publishing.gs.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fpublishing.gs.com%2FtrustedLink%3Fopentoken=T1RLAQIhNsL1HaeJNwEC2a_67EB7n86dthCJ9Wun9pvgbQ-ZSG1NA5XGAACw8Un_0-JNBMOQGdw2rqaE3Y2EZJxb2-kCWTo7xyINKmKZC4Q1mHuNGZMbGyPxtYHdzUN3aUIQ-hgeIfvEGE3w3R08TOEb50YQ-WeRJlF4A_vP_Kffekr7NR5jBcw34vlrgpU0SL9Y4k-PFMCA6BE5PDdspZU_r6u10IbrGlhTltb1ZBEUUE3sGvd7A4QpN-mIheF9RIxp2P1hA5aScdTZPwEO8QLWKxPQKxR959UG8wA*%26id=dacec09a-de43-48a7-81a2-4d80da74f339%26utm_medium=pa%26renditionType=pdf/1/0100019d0c654979-055d8fff-2dbf-42bb-bf54-b0ee8bdc2cc1-000000/v3QExI6h-DpMW_fcWwqDgIx_p7BkfS6O5BxDCZ98LbE=449>  	 	 | 	 	 Read <https://publishing.gs.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fpublishing.gs.com%2FtrustedLink%3Fopentoken=T1RLAQIhNsL1HaeJNwEC2a_67EB7n86dthCJ9Wun9pvgbQ-ZSG1NA5XGAACw8Un_0-JNBMOQGdw2rqaE3Y2EZJxb2-kCWTo7xyINKmKZC4Q1mHuNGZMbGyPxtYHdzUN3aUIQ-hgeIfvEGE3w3R08TOEb50YQ-WeRJlF4A_vP_Kffekr7NR5jBcw34vlrgpU0SL9Y4k-PFMCA6BE5PDdspZU_r6u10IbrGlhTltb1ZBEUUE3sGvd7A4QpN-mIheF9RIxp2P1hA5aScdTZPwEO8QLWKxPQKxR959UG8wA*%26id=dacec09a-de43-48a7-81a2-4d80da74f339%26utm_medium=pa/1/0100019d0c654979-055d8fff-2dbf-42bb-bf54-b0ee8bdc2cc1-000000/3jf3XB2DO66CO_8sMKDyLs5TAm6wcOn00LS3PlQ3Ioc=449>  	
 	 
	
 	 
Latin America Energy: Oil: What to Buy in a mid cycle oil price environment in the USD70's range? PBR, PRIO, Vista stand out 
	
20 March 2026 | 2:51PM BRT | Research | Equity 	
	
	
Oil prices soared to USD107/bbl mainly due to the ongoing conflict in the middle east. However, the market seems to be pricing in a scenario where the conflict is likely short-lived (2y forward Brent @ ~USD75/bbl, significantly below spot prices). 

Historically, equities are usually priced based on the market's view on mid-cycle oil prices. With this in mind, we present an assessment of what is likely priced in, as well as what could be the potential implications of the current environment on dividends and any boost to oil production across our coverage.

Dear investors!

 

Please find attached our Oil and Gas Cheat Sheet, bringing together key price movements and performance metrics. We also highlight our high-frequency monitor for PRIO, Brava, and Petrobras' offshore oil/gas production with intuitive graphs following the cheat sheet table.

emails.subject: ENC: B3: Turnover Velocity at a Glance – Benchmarking vs. Global Peers and Adjusting for New Products
emails.one_liner: B3 turnover velocity at 161% YTD (6th globally). Core velocity adjusted to ~70% indicates upside potential.
emails.body:
________________________________

De: Yuri R Fernandes <yuri.r.fernandes@jpmresearchmail.com>
Enviado: sexta-feira, 20 de março de 2026 14:01:43 (UTC-03:00) Brasilia
Para: Sell Side <sellside@torkcapital.com.br>
Assunto: B3: Turnover Velocity at a Glance – Benchmarking vs. Global Peers and Adjusting for New Products


 	Latin America Equity Research 	

B3


Turnover Velocity at a Glance – Benchmarking vs. Global Peers and Adjusting for New Products

 	 
By Yuri R Fernandes AC <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12835604&ad=4073424133&h=BhNZ9Ro&sig=7DHZRr4TuJZJ6h6YLnZxyw0QuNs&v=2&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/analyst/E689071> , Kenneth B. Worthington, CFA <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12835604&ad=4073424133&h=Iw8aO5L&sig=WsVunvSRuHB96iGxT4prWXbuXSy&v=2&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/analyst/N008826> , Guilherme Grespan <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12835604&ad=4073424133&h=OGu-s5p&sig=CWd1tZLnQYWhbgV5eu0KiYJ4sYh&v=2&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/analyst/R578266> , Fernanda Sayao <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12835604&ad=4073424133&h=sCLP3vv&sig=CiIbyiAz6jpFkeRrMqb8JtnOxPT&v=2&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/analyst/I807027> , Diego Marquez Antonio <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12835604&ad=4073424133&h=PmeoAkb&sig=LlZIgKWXhf5E5GR1izKtMxTFT9F&v=2&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/analyst/O817967>  

Click here for the full document and disclaimers <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12835604&ad=4073424133&h=GeZbpMJ&sig=H0y22UymobArRsL2NrZMpLgpSA2&v=2&isig=UZhK6JodAEr9ZFopeAhEjRaAkjz&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/email/scx/-susnlj4/GPS-5241811-0/3a6c769e-a2db-431f-a143-4b12d1afac40> 

Neutral	 
B3SA3.SA, B3SA3 BZ	 
Price (19 Mar 26):R$16.92	 
	

In light of investors’ excitement with B3 equities volumes, we add in this report more granularity on turnover velocity – how it stacks against global players and B3’s own historical average, when adjusting for new products and services launched over the last decade. And as we addressed in “B3 – World Cup Doldrums vs Elections Hype, <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12835604&ad=4073424133&h=KnmjnCm&sig=MNI5xJwHOzKgxXIbvaka0j99L8k&v=2&isig=A87KDFJh09Yy4dQTHnAQGamu1tm&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/email/scx/k16ligk9/GPS-4182385-0/3a6c769e-a2db-431f-a143-4b12d1afac40> ” we also discuss seasonality for the company below. Overall, our main findings were: (i) strong ADTV is a trend seen in most markets, although B3 seems to have benefited slightly more than the global average; (ii) B3’s turnover velocity is among the highest in the world at 161% YTD, ranking 6th among the top 30 exchanges covered by WFE; (3) while turnover looks high at first glance, we see upside in what we call “core turnover,” i.e., excluding HFT, REIT, BDR and ETFs – which we estimate stands today at ~70% and similar to levels seen in 2016 crisis. While today’s conversations are mostly focused on the departure of the CEO, Gilson Finkelsztain, to Santander Brasil (link <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12835604&ad=4073424133&h=E4UoVr4&sig=1RuUtKAA0yH9Wp9KXFWRLgiIPKW&v=2&url=https://api.mziq.com/mzfilemanager/v2/d/5fd7b7d8-54a1-472d-8426-eb896ad8a3c4/759c2db5-a70f-a200-c555-24cd183108ba?origin%3D2> ), we believe investors will soon focus on potential EPS upside risk from equities (and perhaps derivatives) for this year. We see B3 trading at ~14x P/E ’26E. 

*	Increase in equities volumes – not unique to B3, but benefiting more than average. Equities ADTV for B3 increased from R$27-28bn in late 4Q25 to ~R$35-40bn lately, and we see similar trends globally. Using data from the World Federation of Exchanges (WFE), we see turnover velocity YTD increasing for most exchanges globally, both in developed as well as emerging markets. For instance, Nasdaq velocity increased from 121% to 137%, Shanghai from 301% to 351%, India (National Exchange) from 55% to 57% and Korea from 159% to 184%, to name a few. Still, we see B3 more at the higher end of beneficiaries. This compares with B3 turnover increasing from 128% in 2025 to 161% YTD. On a separate note, we continue to see B3 ranking high in absolute turnover velocity as well – the sixth largest turnover velocity globally from the top 30 exchanges covered by WFE, behind Taipei, Shanghai, Instabul, Korea and NYSE. 

Figure 1: Turnover Velocity Comparison – Select Peers (WFE) 

Value Traded / Market Cap

	2019	 2020	 2021	 2022	 2023	 2024	 2025	 2026 YTD	 YTD vs 2025	 
Nasdaq - US	 136%	 165%	 127%	 143%	 116%	 107%	 121%	 137%	 16 pp 	
NYSE	 94%	 136%	 115%	 125%	 107%	 106%	 131%	 163%	 32 pp 	
Shanghai Stock Exchange	 167%	 214%	 235%	 206%	 183%	 224%	 301%	 351%	 50 pp 	
National Stock Exchange of India	 57%	 91%	 75%	 53%	 55%	 67%	 55%	 57%	 2 pp 	
Korea Exchange	 137%	 318

emails.body:
________________________________

De: BTG Pactual Equities Research <sh-btgpactual-research@btgpactual.com>Em Nome deRodrigo Almeida <rodrigo-r.almeida@btgpactual.com>
Enviado: sexta-feira, 20 de março de 2026 14:00:00 (UTC-03:00) Brasilia
Assunto: Extel/II 2026 ending today - We'd Appreciate Your Support! (LatAm + Brazil Oil & Gas)



Hi, I hope all is well!

 

I wanted to kindly ask for your support on the Extel survey (formerly II) – the voting period ends today.

 

The ranking is an important metric for us to evaluate and better assess our performance, so if you feel our work and interactions have been helpful, we would greatly appreciate your support with a 5-star vote in the following categories:

 

*	LatAm – Oil, Gas & Petrochemicals (Rodrigo Almeida, Daniel Guardiola, and Gustavo Cunha)
*	Brazil – Oil & Chemicals (Rodrigo Almeida and Gustavo Cunha)

 

 <https://salesforce-mailing-distribution-prod.s3.sa-east-1.amazonaws.com/Mailing%20Distribution/assets/2026-03-04/1772642123198.png> 

 

If possible, it would mean a lot if you could rate us in both categories. Link for voting here <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https:/www.extelinsights.com/voting/surveys__;!!JVh_Qfuk4otXm3Mt0g!4aenJ5X46Ab42PFR_opsVRSuB5ghjmW9pxUlF1kKMIT_BBJYYm05pG3HjHDCElzEQQATsI1Xc6BU4AzH1EHbuJCu9C77-XY34CX80iTf$> 

 

We truly appreciate your support and partnership, and please do count on us if we can ever be helpful.

 

Best regards,

 

Rodrigo Almeida – LatAm/Brazil Oil & Gas

Daniel Guardiola – LatAm Oil & Gas, Colombia Strategy

Gustavo Cunha – LatAm/Brazil Oil & Gas

Assunto: Last call for the Extel survey - Healthcare & Education



Dear clients,

 

As you probably know, today is the final day to participate in the Extel survey, and I imagine your inbox has already been filled with voting requests.

We have always tried to take a slightly different approach. Rather than explicitly asking for votes, we prefer to focus on delivering consistent, high-quality work and let the recognition come naturally.

 

So this is not a direct request for votes. Instead, we would simply encourage you to take part in the survey. If you feel that our work has added value to your investment process over the past year, we believe your vote will naturally reflect that.

 

We are running in the following categories:

 

*	Brazil – Healthcare (Samuel Alves and Maria Resende)
*	Brazil – Education (Samuel Alves and Maria Resende)

 

*	LatAm – Healthcare (Samuel Alves and Maria Resende)
*	LatAm – Education (Samuel Alves and Maria Resende)

 

Link for voting here <https://www.extelinsights.com/voting/surveys> 

 

Thank you, as always, for your continued trust and partnership.

 

Best regards,

 

Samuel

emails.subject: ENC: LatAm Equity Strategy: What are Investors Reading?
emails.one_liner: LatAm Equity Strategy: Destaques de fluxos (Brasil US$1bi pós-conflito), interesse em Argentina e vantagens geopolíticas de Brasil/México.
emails.body:
________________________________

De: Emy Shayo Cherman <emy.shayo@jpmresearchmail.com>
Enviado: sexta-feira, 20 de março de 2026 12:59:45 (UTC-03:00) Brasilia
Para: Sell Side <sellside@torkcapital.com.br>
Assunto: LatAm Equity Strategy: What are Investors Reading?


 	Global Markets Strategy 	
 <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12835060&ad=4073060869&h=bZBR4X0&sig=IlVRR5ArIm5u3SYJsV4ZteWNX6P&v=2&url=https://www.extelinsights.com/voting> 	

What Are Investors Reading? 


If you like our research, consider your 5 stars vote for J.P. Morgan on the LatAm II Extel survey. Our team is featured in the following categories: Equity Strategy, Brazil Research, Mexico Research, North Andean Research, Argentina Research, Chile Research, ESG, Thematic and Economics. 

Below are the most-read strategy reports of the past few months. There are two main interests: 

*	Flows: To us, it is the biggest story for EM and LatAm since the Fed started to cut interest rates in September 2025. The question we ask ourselves every single day is on whether EM flows are to resume, even if at a more discreet pace, once oil prices find a footing. EM saw US$8 billion of outflows since the conflict started. On the other hand, Brazil has seen inflows of US$1 billion since then, which is incredible from a historical point of view. 
*	Argentina: Interest in Argentina reached a new peak with the congressional elections last year and since then, thematic reports on the country have been top of mind. 

The last two reports that were the most read are about Brazil and Mexico: 

*	Brazil and oil: We think Brazil (and LatAm for that matter) are clear relative winners of the current geopolitical crisis. The region is away from the conflict, is a net oil exporter (ex Chile and Peru), and Brazil still has the ongoing easing cycle and the optionality offered by the October elections. 
*	Mexico and USMCA: The USMCA renegotiation is the most important trigger for Mexico this year and the early start of conversations has made us more hopeful of a positive outcome by mid-year. 

See below, read below and vote for 5 stars for us. 

Many thanks! 

Emy, Diego, Adrian, Cinthya and Felipe

emails.subject: ENC: Autos & Shared Mobility: Global Auto Monitor: Robo meets Macro
emails.one_liner: Autonomia (TSLA, RIVN, UBER) avança, mas demanda EV fraca e inflação commodity persistem. S&P rebaixa prod. global FY26 para -1,0% YoY.
emails.body:
________________________________

De: Andrew S Percoco <Andrew.Percoco@morganstanley.com>
Enviado: sexta-feira, 20 de março de 2026 12:58:47 (UTC-03:00) Brasilia
Para: Sell Side <sellside@torkcapital.com.br>
Assunto: Autos & Shared Mobility: Global Auto Monitor: Robo meets Macro



	 
	 
	 
Autos & Shared Mobility 	
Global Auto Monitor: Robo meets Macro	 
Read Full Report <https://ny.matrix.ms.com/eqr/article/webapp/9f6858ca-244a-11f1-99ea-46f6fece5bca?t=1774022272:5454:9027:95722cac585c&m=1&ch=CDF Research ISG Autoblast#e=c2VsbHNpZGVAdG9ya2NhcGl0YWwuY29tLmJy> 	 
(PDF: 15 Pages) 	
North America 	
Industry View   In-Line

Andrew S Percoco, Daniela M Haigian, Javier Martinez de Olcoz Cerdan, Shinji Kakiuchi, Binay Singh, Tim Hsiao & 7 more...	 
March 20, 2026

	
OEMs in the U.S. accelerate toward autonomy, with TSLA's robotaxi flywheel and RIVN x UBER deal signaling a push to unlock software and fleet-based monetization. However, weaker EV demand, lower global production forecasts, and rising commodity inflation continue to temper near-term fundamentals. 

What Others Are Reading - Our Most Read Reports

*	[GLOBAL] S&P LVP downgrade. FY26 -1.0% YoY (15 Mar 2026) <https://ny.matrix.ms.com/eqr/article/webapp/d1352636-1daa-11f1-881a-897d542fee46?ch=rp&sch=ar> S&P now expects FY26 production to reach 92.1mn units (from 92.8mn last month), implying -1.0% YoY. FY27's production forecast revised down to 93.6mn units (from 94.1mn), +1.5% YoY.
*	[GLOBAL] Global Auto Databook: Shifting EV Dynamics Reshape the Global Auto Landscape (18 Mar 2026) <https://ny.matrix.ms.com/eqr/article/webapp/services/published/rendition/pdf/ARBENNO20260318195900380.pdf?cobaltId=02a3-202db37d5d91-d69a579a38dc-1000&uuid=e858291c-2304-11f1-99ea-46f6fece5bca>  Global autos sentiment remains cautious, driven by cooling EV demand and affordability constraints. We monitor a growing regional divide, with US EV volumes softening amid OEM pullback (offset by ICE strength), a weak start to '26 in China, and Europe facing rising pressure from Chinese competition.

*	[NORTH AMERICA] Unlocking the Robotaxi Flywheel (18 Mar 2026) <https://ny.matrix.ms.com/eqr/article/webapp/02f68848-174f-11f1-9551-76e26cd02d8c?ch=rp&sch=ar> TSLA's ability to scale the unsupervised robotaxi fleet is the most important catalyst for the stock this year. Each incremental mile driven by the robotaxi fleet accelerates learning for personal FSD, which supports higher FSD attach rates and re-accelerates auto demand, improving FCF gen.

*	[NORTH AMERICA] Framing Tesla’s Semi Manufacturing Ambitions (17 Mar 2026) <https://ny.matrix.ms.com/eqr/article/webapp/002342b2-215b-11f1-99ea-46f6fece5bca?ch=rp&sch=ar>  With AI compute emerging as a potential bottleneck, we outline what internal capacity at Tesla could look like. While any effort would be highly capital-intensive and years away from commercialization, it provides a useful framework for thinking about Tesla’s longer-term AI ambitions.
*	[NORTH AMERICA] Autos and Energy Converge (20 Mar 2026) <https://ny.matrix.ms.com/eqr/article/webapp/f80fbd1e-23d8-11f1-99ea-46f6fece5bca?ch=rp&sch=ar> This week's announcements reflect efforts from automakers to diversify revenue streams from cyclical auto end markets tethered to the uncertain fate of the consumer within a volatile market and attach to industries experiencing secular growth – see TSLA, GM, F, BWA, etc.
*	[NORTH AMERICA] Rivian Enters the Robotaxi Race with Uber (19 Mar 2026) <https://ny.matrix.ms.com/eqr/article/webapp/70ed8718-238c-11f1-99ea-46f6fece5bca?ch=rp&sch=ar> Rivian and Uber (covered by Brian Nowak) announced a robotaxi partnership this morning, wherein Uber will invest up to $1.25bn in Rivian and deploy 50k fully autonomous R2 robotaxis.

*	[KOREA] S. Korea Autos, EV Batteries and Energy & Materials (20 Mar 2026) <https://ny.matrix.ms.com/eqr/article/webapp/0e69bde6-236e-11f1-99ea-46f6fece5bca?ch=rp&sch=ar> 

*	<https://ny.matrix.ms.com/eqr/article/webapp/8989d4d4-2c2a-11f0-85fb-ceffcb919c87?ch=rp&sch=mfr> <https://ny.matrix.ms.com/eqr/article/webapp/8bb37ae8-04df-11f0-a792-58de021e0d36?ch=rp&sch=mfr> <https://ny.matrix.ms.com/eqr/article/webapp/8ac28222-f4aa-11ef-ba72-8d77403bbb42?ch=rpint&sch=ar> <https://ny.matrix.ms.com/eqr/article/webapp/6a08118a-344e-11f0-8677-a5b406758939?ch=rp&sch=mfr> <https://ny.matrix.ms.com/eqr/article/webapp/62be1614-2c23-11f0-85fb-ceffcb919c87?ch=rp&sch=mfr> <https://ny.matrix.ms.com/eqr/article/webapp/ffc3129a-105b-11f0-a04e-48725a0d59a3?ch=rp&sch=mfr> <https://ny.matrix.ms.com/eqr/article/webapp/84f8a6c2-35c9-11f0-8677-a5b406758939?ch=rp&sch=mfr> [KOREA] Attractive Re-entry Point (17 Mar 2026) <https://ny.matrix.ms.com/eqr/article/webapp/c9db4d7e-1cd9-11f1-881a-897d542fee46?ch=rp&sch=ar> Despite the risk of near-term earnings volatility arising from geopolitical issues, our latest company visit reinforces the view that HT's fundamentals remain healthy while valuation remains too cheap to ignore.

emails.subject: ENC: JPM - Argentina 101: The 2026 Country Handbook
emails.one_liner: JPM Research destaca bancos argentinos Macro e Galicia (OW), negociando a 1.2-1.3x P/BV com potencial ROE de 18% e 2.0x book.
emails.body:
________________________________

De: Yuri R Fernandes <yuri.r.fernandes@jpmresearchmail.com>
Enviado: sexta-feira, 20 de março de 2026 12:55:07 (UTC-03:00) Brasilia
Para: Sell Side <sellside@torkcapital.com.br>
Assunto: JPM - Argentina 101: The 2026 Country Handbook


 	Latin America Equity Research 	

Hi Tork,

 <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12835026&ad=4073036062&h=bZBR4X0&sig=6p1jdWjoZh0flC0EGRp9oTQfbB8&v=2&url=https://www.extelinsights.com/voting> 	

Making sure you saw our Argentina 101 report. Starting on page 69, we provide color on Argentine banks, including: (1) credit penetration, (2) where we are in the credit cycle, (3) margin and ROE evolution, and (4) deposits, among other topics. Hope you find it useful.

We like banks in the country (Macro and Galicia both OW), as we call for a gradual ROE recovery. Argentine banks are now trading at 1.2-1.3x P/BV, which looks asymmetrically positive. Our bear case is those names could trade at 1x book value with limited growth and real ROE returning to low-teens, while our base case in the model call for mid-high teens ROE ~18% and loan growth reaccelerating in line with loan-to-GDP penetration story. In this scenario we could see valuations back to 2.0x book. We discuss this and other details in our note “Argentine Banks: Positioning for a Multi-Year Turnaround - The Case for Buying on Weakness <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12835026&ad=4073036062&h=Z_myaId&sig=JVnYdiRTkn9Zv56wK6w1jvMXTge&v=2&isig=MajkrNdRQbzv1mKxD5JzJ0bxOKt&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/email/scx/8ieene23/GPS-5171237-0/6f5da451-8737-4581-b339-4f49c64b6e48> ”. 

If our Argentina coverage has been helpful, please consider supporting J.P. Morgan in the Extel Survey under the Argentina Research category (link <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12835026&ad=4073036062&h=KGUV_13&sig=Y8h6IDs4tXvjnSkprnOeylWinHi&v=2&url=https://www.extelinsights.com/voting/surveys> ).

Dear clients,

 

We would kindly ask for your support as the Extel/II polls are closing soon. As the saying goes, “the game is only over when the referee blows the final whistle”.

 

Markets are very challenging, and there are probably many people you may not know asking for votes. We also understand that filling out the survey can be somewhat time-consuming. That said, the poll remains a very important tool in supporting our work.

 

If I, Alonso Aramburu, Ricardo Buchpiguel, Thiago Paura, or any of our colleagues have been helpful to you, we would greatly appreciate your support and your vote, ideally with a 5-star rating.

 

As a reminder, we cover both LatAm and Brazil, across Banks and Non-Bank Financials.

 

All the best, and please continue to count on us in the years to come. I’ve been here for 19 years and have no plans to change or go anywhere 😊

 

Rosman

TUPY3.SA, TUPY3 BZ	 
Price (19 Mar 26):R$11.50	 
	

Tupy hosted its 4Q25 conference call with top management this morning. We see TUPY trading at 4.3x EV/EBITDA 26e vs POMO at 4.5x and RAPT at 4.6x. 

*	2026 outlook. For commercial vehicles, lower tariffs and higher freight prices in the US reads positively for volumes. As for off-road, investments in non-resi and data centers in the US are fuelling demand for generators and related machinery. 
*	Automotive segment. Domestic market was down 40% yoy, while foreign revenues declined 5% yoy due to the high interest rate environment and lackluster agribusiness sector. However, 41% of this segment’s top-line is linked to machining and assembly which have higher margins. 
*	Aftermarket segment. Top-line growth of 15% yoy in 4Q25 domestically was due to an increase in the portfolio of products. 
*	Energy & Decarbonization. Higher sales of own motors and indirect exports fueled revenue growth domestically of 36% yoy. 
*	Higher order activity of heavy vehicles in the US. Tupy is seeing its clients placing more orders but the clients have also cut capacity in recent quarters. As such, these higher orders should only reflect in production volumes during 2H26 as OEMs resume capacity by May. Moreover, there is limited visibility at this point on the tailwind of EPA 2027, but several contracts Tupy signed in recent quarters should start being delivered in the 2H, being a tailwind of R$600mn in top-line this year. 
*	US trucks fleet age. Fleet is at its oldest point since 2017 at ~5.8 years and is set to reach 6 years by year-end and 6.2 years by 2028. This implies substantial pent-up demand once macroeconomic risks subside and clients begin placing orders. 
*	One-offs. Totaled R$544mn in the quarter related to asset impairments, loss of tax credits and provisioning. 
*	Capacity reduction. Goal is to reduce fixed costs in order to Tupy have a positive ROIC spread to WACC in the mid-term. 
*	Operational cash flow. Benefited from R$174mn in IPI tax credits sale. 
*	Domestic outlook. Diesel price hike is a headwind for volumes recovery. 
*	Capacity utilization. Tupy is still assessing capacity and its utilization rate has not changed meaningfully vs last year (around 65% vs goal of reaching 80%). 
*	Covenant waiver. There is no need to revisit the net debt/EBITDA covenant waiver of 5.5x as the US recovery, re-structuring and new products will lead to EBITDA to improve. Moreover, Tupy’s liquidity is solid. 
*	Capex. Was in-line with the company’s expectations and linked to the demobilization initiative. 
*	Margins. Should improve sequentially and reach Tupy’s planned levels (14-15%) during 2H26.

Overweight	 
CYRE3.SA, CYRE3 BZ	 
Price (19 Mar 26):R$27.11	 
	

Cyrela hosted today its 4Q25 conference call with top management. Please see below for the main highlights. We see CYRE trading at 6.3x P/E 26e and 1.2x P/BV vs. EZTC at 7.3x/0.76x. 

*	Outlook 1Q. Sales speed is accomodating being below previous years; however, it is still good enough for Cyrela to continue to launch and be profitable. At this point there is no need to revise launches downward. Too soon to comment on presales in 1Q, but the company had lower launches in Jan+Feb. vs 2025, but sales of inventories are growing yoy. 
*	Revenue recognition. There was no change in the percentage of completion rules; Cyrela is now evaluating every quarter which projects the company will not give up or not, allowing the company to start their revenues recognition and this will continue going forward. Cyrela revenues to be recognized are over R$11bn. Additionally, CYRE expects in 2026 ~R$5.5-6.0bn in revenues from construction evolution vs ~R$5.0bn in 2025. 
*	São Paulo permits. The resumption in permit issuance should happen soon. Cyrela’s base case is that the market will normalize soon and at this point there was no impact on the company’s figures. 
*	Margin breakdown by segment. Vivaz gross margin is improving, reaching 36% in 4Q vs 34% in the full year; therefore, margins should be closer to 36% in the future vs 34% today. Consolidated gross margin should continue to improve, being around 34%. 
*	Inventory levels in SP. Sales speed was better in the past, but it still healthy for good companies. 
*	Improvement in MCMV. Looking at this matter thru the sector association, and Cyrela expects improvements to be approved and have a positive impact for the company. Cyrela never assumes any improvements on its viability studies. 
*	Deliveries. 2027 expected deliveries are 86% sold as of 4Q and 34% collected. 
*	Low income exposure. Around 30% at the organic level and over 40% including JVs. The mix in the future will depend on how fast mid and high income grow. 
*	Land bank. Cyrela will remain selective in land bank acquisiton, focused on high quality land plots. 
*	Launches. Vivaz (low income brand) continues to grow since the company is still small in the São Paulo market. 
*	Raw material prices. Overall, it is too early to comment on the imapct of higher oil prices on raw materials. 
*	Strategic focus for 2026: There are several challenges ahead as in every year, and Cyrela will continue to be focused on being profitable with a high ROE above 20%. 
*	Change in working scale. End of 6x1 is not defined yet, but it is too soon to say what the impact would be. 
*	Vivaz growth vs FCF. Vivaz is growing and generating FCF. However, growth in the mid-income segment has consumed part of the cash. 
*	IVA impact. Negative for mid and high and positive for low income. It will be mandatory for 2029 launches onwards. 
*	Selling expenses. There was an expansion on a yoy basis growing above launches, impacted by stands and Cashme expenses. Increase in Vivaz sales also generat

emails.subject: ENC: Cyrela (Buy): 4Q25 conference call highlights (BTG Pactual's Cambauva/Fabris/Pascale)
emails.one_liner: Cyrela 4Q25 call: challenging 2025, strong ops, healthy financials. 2026 also tough but resilient. Maintain Buy, TP R$40.00, 5.5x P/E 2026E.
emails.body:
________________________________

De: BTG Pactual Equities Research <sh-btgpactual-research@btgpactual.com>Em Nome deGustavo Cambauva <gustavo.cambauva@btgpactual.com>
Enviado: sexta-feira, 20 de março de 2026 12:11:56 (UTC-03:00) Brasilia
Assunto: Cyrela (Buy): 4Q25 conference call highlights (BTG Pactual's Cambauva/Fabris/Pascale)



BTG Pactual | Equity Research 


Company Note - Brazil - Real Estate 


Cyrela (Buy Rating; TP: R$40.00)


20-Mar-2026


 

	4Q25 conference call highlights  

 

Cyrela’s conference call has just ended. Please find below the main highlights.

 

Overall, management highlighted that 2025 was a challenging year for the housing sector, mainly due to a tougher macro backdrop. Nevertheless, they reiterated that Cyrela delivered a strong (and resilient) operating performance throughout the year, while financials also remained healthy and indebtedness ended the year at a manageable level. They also noted that 2026 should be another challenging year, but Cyrela is ready to navigate market conditions and has a resilient platform. We remain BUYers of Cyrela, with the stock trading at 5.5x P/E 2026E.

 

Main highlights:

 

*	Inventory levels: The CEO mentioned that sales speed has indeed decreased in 2025 compared to 2023 and 2024 (which saw above-average performance). That said, Cyrela will not revise its launch pipeline for 2026, although it acknowledges that inventory levels are higher.

 

*	Cyrela’s stake in Cury: With Fábio Cury transitioning from the CEO role, Cyrela will leave its two seats on the Board. The CEO mentioned that Cyrela’s participation was not as accretive and that there could be conflicts of interest due to its exposure to Vivaz. Despite that, Cyrela does not plan to fully divest from Cury in the ST. There are also no specific plans regarding Lavvi and PLPL.

 

*	Revenue dynamics: The CFO explained the changes in Cyrela’s revenue recognition rule in Q4. He mentioned that Cyrela will now decide at the end of each quarter which projects it is certain will not be canceled and will therefore start recognizing these revenues. Looking ahead, there is still a gap to be closed in terms of accrued and deferred revenues.

 

*	SP Zoning Law: The city hall and the municipal council are attempting to challenge the measure with solid arguments and expect it to be suspended soon. They cannot provide a timeline, but they do not work with a scenario in which the measure remains in place for a longer time.

 

*	Gross margins at Vivaz: The CFO stated that they expect a recovery in gross margins for their low-income brand (Vivaz). When looking at launches, Vivaz’s margins are slightly above the 36% level, suggesting room for further improvement.

 

*	Deliveries in 2026: Cyrela expects deliveries to grow ~15% y/y in 2026, of which 85% are sold and 49% collected so far. For 2027, volumes should be similar, with 86% sold and 34% collected. Vintages are well sold and healthy in terms of LTV.

 

*	Changes in MCMV: Regarding potential changes in MCMV, the CFO said that the likelihood of approval is high and should be positive for the company.

 

*	Landbank: Cyrela remains highly selective in landbank acquisitions and avoids providing a specific budget for this line in 2026. Growth for the sake of growth is not the focus; if projects deliver strong ROEs, they will continue acquiring land. The CEO also noted that Vivaz is still growing and should continue to do so.

 

*	Inflation costs: It is still too early to assess potential construction cost increases related to geopolitical tensions in the Middle East, but so far the company has not felt any impact.

 

*	Priorities for 2026: The main challenge is to sustain ROE levels. The CEO highlighted that the year should be quite challenging, especially considering the growth in operations over recent years.

 

*	SG&A: Operations have expanded significantly, with larger launches in 2025 (e.g., higher marketing expenses), especially at CashMe (which saw strong origination growth during the year). In addition, higher sales at Vivaz also bring incremental costs, such as ITBI and registration expenses.

 

*	“Other results” line: The CFO noted that the main changes in this line came from the amortization of goodwill from the Eden project (acquired via quotas) and an impairment in an older SPE in São Paulo.

 

*	Changes to the workweek: The CFO said there is nothing concrete yet and that they would need to assess the exact terms of any legal changes to properly estimate the impact.

 

*	FCF in Vivaz: Management noted that Vivaz has been able to grow while generating cash, as sales speed at launches remains strong, and transfers to banks are also quick (supporting FCF generation). At the consolidated FCF level, growth in the mid/high-end segment weighed on performance this year, but Vivaz continues to perform well.

 

*	1Q26 performance: The CFO n

A year in motion - 2026 Extel Voting Season

Dear investor,

We are in the final days of the Extel (Institutional Investor) voting period. Thank you for your support!

In case you missed - some of our report pieces so far

As we reach the end of Q1, one key takeaway stands out: it has been a particularly busy start to the year. In January, we saw a strong wave of equity inflows into Brazil, largely passive in nature, which drove outperformance among large caps and blue chips, supported by their higher index weights and liquidity. In February, investor focus shifted to AI-related disruption themes across selected names. More recently, in March, markets have been impacted by escalating conflicts in the Middle East, alongside rising oil prices and a broad risk-off move across both equity and fixed income globally. Against this backdrop, we have published several reports spanning thematic and long-term views, as well as shorter-term trading calls and scenario-based first takes, which we compile below.

1) Starting Packs - a quick start summary for the year

At the very beginning of the year, we launched our “starting pack” series covering some of the most discussed names within our universe. In these reports, we aimed to summarize 2025 performance, outline our expectations for 2026, and highlight the key drivers and risks for each name. Additionally, we sought to quantify how each case was positioned in terms of growth, returns, earnings momentum, valuation, and market sentiment. Lastly, a key focus was investor positioning and sentiment, where we aimed to better capture prevailing market dynamics. Our "Starting Packs" series covered WEG (here <https://www.btgpactual.com/research/report-view/117209> ), Localiza (here <https://www.btgpactual.com/research/report-view/117224> ), Embraer (here <https://www.btgpactual.com/research/report-view/117245> ), Motiva (here <https://www.btgpactual.com/research/report-view/117265> ), Rumo (here <https://www.btgpactual.com/research/report-view/117300> ), LATAM Airlines (here <https://www.btgpactual.com/research/report-view/117322> ), GPS (here <https://www.btgpactual.com/research/report-view/117352> ), Marcopolo (here <https://www.btgpactual.com/research/report-view/117383> ), and finally our "Starting Pack - The PM Control Panel" (here <https://www.btgpactual.com/research/report-view/117417> ), in which we compilated our views for 2026.

________________________________

De: Diego Celedon <diego.celedon@jpmresearchmail.com>

Para: Sell Side <sellside@torkcapital.com.br>

 	Global Markets Strategy 	

Andean Flow of Funds


Continued Inflows Across the Andean Region

 	 
By Diego Celedon AC <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12833949&ad=4072430850&h=6J2Xfs_&sig=WMzGVU9dJshkNoRJkqMKlAZO8TH&v=2&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/analyst/F473430> , Emy Shayo Cherman <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12833949&ad=4072430850&h=JDaFSLL&sig=3d5kjWsgJw96171AhESx2oMJvuK&v=2&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/analyst/O089612> , Adrian E Huerta <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12833949&ad=4072430850&h=EUeO2BD&sig=RS02yESa1E4rP4y5ebdRISYRctL&v=2&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/analyst/U034572>  

Click here for the full document and disclaimers <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12833949&ad=4072430850&h=7btVLZX&sig=8tQCnIIEbJVC9dfgQGfiDLacShH&v=2&isig=RT1IG7EMTPE7tsbpWsLxObhRSHw&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/email/scx/tsov81qf/GPS-5239552-0/95dd48c4-8f54-4e78-9b87-68055a7cfac5> 

In February, Chilean institutional investors showed positive flows. With US$175.7 mn of inflows into Chilean stocks, pension funds extended the positive trend in place since August last year. Compared with the prior month, inflows increased; however, the allocation to local equities declined from 10.8% to 10.3% of total AUM.Latam Airlines emerged as the most purchased stock by pension funds this month, resulting in inflows of US$161.4 mn, followed by Falabella with US$18.0 mn and Banco de Chile with US$16.4 mn. On the divestment side, the largest sales were in Enel Americas (-US$24.0mn), Entel (-US$13.4 mn), and Enel Chile (-US$10.1 mn). Similarly, Chilean mutual funds experienced inflows of US$59.4 mn, while decreasing their exposure to local equities to 4.8% during February. These inflows were primarily driven by Enel Americas, the most purchased stock at US$54.0 mn, followed by Latam Airlines at US$38.1mn and Itau Chile at US$25.6 mn. Conversely, they were mostly net sellers of ILC (-US$48.1 mn), followed by MallPlaza (-US$36.6 mn) and CCU (-US$16.1 mn). In Colombia, pension funds were net buyers, with US$23.2mn of inflows in the second month of 2026. Meanwhile, the Colombian market had an ADTV of US$64.1 mn, almost double its 10Y historical average. Finally, Peruvian pension funds posted an AUM of US$35.2bn, from which 18.1% are local equities. 

*	Chilean pension funds began the year with net inflows, recording a positive net inflow of US$175.65mn in February. This figure continues the trend observed last month. The allocation to local equities decreased versus last month but remains elevated relative to prior years. The largest holdings in pension fund portfolios are LATAM Airlines (US$3.6bn), SQM‑B (US$2.8bn), and MallPlaza (US$2.5bn). 
*	Chilean mutual funds registered US$59.4mn of inflows into local equities in February, down from January’s US$360.6mn, but still consistent with a firm inflow trend this year. The allocation to equities edged to 4.8%, and year to date the pattern continues to point to renewed appetite for domestic stocks and solid institutional conviction. Buying was led by Enel Américas (US$54.0mn), with LATAM Airlines (US$38.1mn) and Itaú Chile (US$25.6mn) close behind. 
*	Colombian pension funds remain buyers. Pension funds registered net inflows of US$23.3 million in February, while their exposure to local equities decreased to 8.8% for the month. The Colombian market’s ADTV was US$64.1mn, nearly twice its 10‑year historical average.

This monthly report provides a comprehensive summary of themes, top picks, and key valuation metrics for the airlines covered by J.P. Morgan worldwide. This product encompasses more than 30 companies broken down into the following regions: LatAm, North America, Europe, CEEMEA, and Asia.

*	Latin America (Guilherme Mendes): The short-term outlook for airlines in Latin America remains closely tied to the evolution of jet fuel prices, which account for nearly 40% of cash costs. Given the fluid nature of the current environment, we suggest caution – please refer to our joint report <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12833905&ad=4072413686&h=auypl7p&sig=6LyTBcGrFNh1A7AiKW70SkU041U&v=2&isig=N8QlnDgu1aVFYeRsyxdU2M1oqvx&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/email/scx/jbobqvt5/GPS-5230955-0/56a71452-5e56-40a8-87e7-a62a06e73c47> with the Credit Research team, which includes a sensitivity analysis on jet fuel prices. For the companies under our coverage in the Latin America Airlines cluster, we favor LTM (OW) and CPA (OW) due to a combination of discounted valuation relative to historical levels, lighter balance sheets than peers, and consistent free cash flow generation and dividend payments. Both companies have consistently delivered robust operational results over the past few quarters. We recently initiated coverage on Aeromexico (OW), highlighting its positioning as the only full-service carrier in Mexico and leading carrier at Mexico City Airport (MEX), with higher exposure to international routes. However, we acknowledge that growth is likely to be more moderate than peers’, justifying a cheaper valuation. For VLRS (N), we acknowledge that the outlook for the aviation environment in Mexico should remain challenging in 2026. emails.subject: ENC: LatAm Oil & Gas: Daily Fuel | March 20th, 2026 (BTG Pactual's Almeida/Cunha/Guardiola/Muñoz/Leyva)
emails.one_liner: LatAm O&G Daily Fuel: Diesel subvention, ICMS exemption, BRKM REIQ sanction adds US$250-350mn EBITDA, ANP public hearing extended to April 3.
emails.body:
________________________________

De: BTG Pactual Equities Research <sh-btgpactual-research@btgpactual.com>Em Nome deRodrigo Almeida <rodrigo-r.almeida@btgpactual.com>
Enviado: sexta-feira, 20 de março de 2026 11:16:42 (UTC-03:00) Brasilia
Assunto: LatAm Oil & Gas: Daily Fuel | March 20th, 2026 (BTG Pactual's Almeida/Cunha/Guardiola/Muñoz/Leyva)



 


BTG Pactual | Equity Research 


Sector Note - LATAM - Oil & Gas 


LatAm Oil & Gas 


20-March-2026 


 


Daily Fuel | March 20th, 2026 


Read the full document here >> <https://www.btgpactual.com/research/report-view/118861>  

The Extel (Institutional Investor) voting period ends today. If our research helped you make better investment decisions, we would like to kindly ask for your support through a 5-star vote in the following categories: "Brazil - Oil & Chemicals" and "LatAm - Oil, Gas & Petrochemicals". Click here to support our team <https://www.extelinsights.com/> 

Diesel subvention & potential ICMS exemption; BRKM - REIQ sanction

Dear Investor, please find below the most relevant news for the LatAm Oil & Gas sector, with implications for the regional stocks. In this report, you can also find a comparables table for the stocks under our coverage.

 

Company News

 

FUEL DISTRIBUTION: Federal Government Introduces Guidelines for the Diesel Subvention Program. What´s new? Yesterday, the federal government announced the decree 12.883/2026 that basically establishes that Petrobras needs to sell diesel at the current price (R$3.65/liter) to gain access to the R$0.32/liter diesel subvention from the federal government. Therefore, the reference price for Petrobras is R$3.97/liter. For importers and private refineries, the reference prices will be based on an import parity price (methodology to be defined by ANP).

What are the implications?

For Petrobras, they will be getting the subvention as long as they keep diesel prices at the current R$3.65/liter to distributors. Petrobras will be therefore getting R$3.65 (selling price for the molecule) + R$0.32 (subvention), for each liter of diesel sold.

The company could still eventually raise prices. For example, if Petrobras raised prices by R$1.00/liter to narrow the gap vs. Import parity, they would no longer receive the subvention, but the R$4.65/liter would be much closer to IPP. The point here is that raising prices remains at the company’s discretion and the reference price does not freeze. See our Mar 20 report <https://www.btgpactual.com/research/report-view/118858> 

Figure 1: PBR vs. Import Parity Prices If the ICMS Tax is Removed for Imports (R$/liter) 

 <https://salesforce-mailing-distribution-prod.s3.sa-east-1.amazonaws.com/Mailing%20Distribution/assets/2026-03-20/1774016085692.png> 

Source: Bloomberg, Petrobras and BTG Pactual 

Figure 2: PBR vs. Import Parity Prices Without the ICMS Tax Removal on Imports (R$/liter) 

 <https://salesforce-mailing-distribution-prod.s3.sa-east-1.amazonaws.com/Mailing%20Distribution/assets/2026-03-20/1774016085698.png> 

Source: Bloomberg, Petrobras and BTG Pactual 

FUEL DISTRIBUTION: The Brazilian government is pushing for a temporary ICMS exemption on diesel imports, with President Lula urging states to join the measure and offering federal compensation for half of the revenue loss (estimated at ~R$3bn over two months). The proposal aims to curb rising fuel prices and mitigate supply risks amid global oil market disruptions, though implementation depends on state approval, which remains uncertain. ( Folha, Mar 20 <https://www1.folha.uol.com.br/mercado/2026/03/lula-pressiona-governadores-a-zerar-icms-do-diesel-para-conter-impacto-da-guerra.shtml> )

Our take: A full cut would represent approximately R$1.17/liter, compared to an estimated R$1.93/liter import parity gap, meaning the measure would not fully eliminate the differential versus international prices but would still provide meaningful relief - particularly when combined with the existing R$0.32/liter subsidy for importers. The proposal reinforces the government’s efforts to ease fuel price pressures and reduce strike risks, while avoiding direct interference in Petrobras’ pricing policy, preserving its independence.

Figure 3: Parity summary table 

 <https://salesforce-mailing-distribution-prod.s3.sa-east-1.amazonaws.com/Mailing%20Distribution/assets/2026-03-20/1774016085701.png> 

Source: Bloomberg, Petrobras, BTG Pactual 

(+) BRASKEM: Announced the sanction of a new law increasing REIQ Industrial tax credits from 0.73% to 5.8% on petrochemical feedstocks, with validity through December 2026 (capped at R$2.0bn). The measure also includes an additional 1.5% investment-linked credit ("REIQ Investimento"; capped at R$1.1bn), reinforcing support to the chemical sector and potentially easing margin pressures. (Braskem RI, Mar 20)

Our take: We estimate the program could add approximately US$250–350mn per year to Braskem’s EBI

 	Latin America Equity Research 	

Hi Tork,

The Extel survey (formerly Institutional Investor) is open through March 20 and is VERY important to us. Please support the J.P. Morgan RETAIL & HEALTHCARE team by giving a 5-star rating. We are focused on the LATIN AMERICA survey (not the Brazil survey). To vote, please click the banner below.

Brazil Pharma and Drug Retail


IQVIA Industry Sales – Likely Suggesting GLP-1 Undersupply

 	 
 <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12833639&ad=4072207483&h=bZBR4X0&sig=GnT7rKjM9xfAdsWPC02pCPwm51P&v=2&url=https://www.extelinsights.com/voting> 	
By Joseph Giordano AC <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12833639&ad=4072207483&h=1oafVmh&sig=GjlN2x85MQB82VTwxWNos4fOZ47&v=2&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/analyst/R103209> , Guilherme A. Vilela, CFA <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12833639&ad=4072207483&h=O-axWR_&sig=3aFjlASdgjytovbMsi1yL98PnaS&v=2&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/analyst/R720687> , Nicolas Larrain <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12833639&ad=4072207483&h=5k1WB8K&sig=7X5W0JoMfQInsd4GPRqhULVbDHL&v=2&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/analyst/R596032> , Giovanni Vescovi <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12833639&ad=4072207483&h=cUz-wv5&sig=GO5SdZEyk5l5jZMq5Sqk7PszbP2&v=2&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/analyst/O798110> , Froylan Mendez <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12833639&ad=4072207483&h=OJFKx_a&sig=JKjyy46eDCakR5ZHzXPJ8YHO8OH&v=2&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/analyst/V579633>  

Click here for the full document and disclaimers <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12833639&ad=4072207483&h=BAC-DBs&sig=JLrhHPOCkrZYoFg6TfPu22mUEZ7&v=2&isig=VHnv2W1FIWDpIBZQXLLT108HHzY&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/email/scx/-ecm14el/GPS-5241888-0/87713f50-a809-499b-ae60-d6e75ec4b6f2> 

IQVIA data for Feb’26, published by Sindusfarma (Pharmaceutical Industry Association), point to solid LTM industry trends despite a monthly slowdown. In nominal terms, February sell‑out rose 7.5% y/y, versus 10.7% in January and 19.4% in December. In our view, while we may have some calendar impacts from Carnival, this deceleration likely suggests an undersupply of GLP‑1 drugs in early ’26. The slowdown is largely driven by reference products growth that decelerated to 5% in January and 2% in February versus +15% in 2H25. We also would have expected some acceleration at this time of year ahead of annual drug price increases, which was not the case. Sell‑in increased 2.8% y/y, down from 5% in January. Volume growth was 1.3% y/y, from 1.9%. Still, on an LTM basis metrics remained strong, with sell‑out at 12%, sell‑in at 7.4%, and volume growth at 3.8%. 

*	Retail channel: Drugstore sell-out decelerated, likely reflecting GLP-1 undersupply. Sell-out for drug retail in Brazil slowed to 10.9% y/y, from 11.7% in January and 15.4 in December. On an LTM basis, drug retail sell-out growth remains solid at 12.8% y/y—implying continued positive top-line trends for RD Saúde (RADL; OW), which, in our view, is likely to outperform the market again in ’26 given its higher exposure to GLP-1 and recent channel-optimization initiatives. It is also important to flag that potential GLP-1 undersupply could have had a limited impact of RADL, as the company invested heavily in working capital in 4Q25 and likely holds higher category inventory vs peers. For Hypera (HYPE; OW), medium-term top-line trends are expected to remain softer than the market given its limited and delayed participation in the weight-loss segment. 
*	Institutional channel: Sell-in now decelerating m/m. Sell-in to the institutional channel contracted 10.4% y/y in February, vs. January -9%. On a LTM basis, institutional sell-in growth was 0.8% y/y as of February, likely reflecting limited purchases in the public segment.

Assunto: Cemig (CMIG4.SA): 4Q25 First Take: Better-than-expected


 	 
 	 
	 
 	 Print PDF <https://publishing.gs.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fpublishing.gs.com%2FtrustedLink%3Fopentoken=T1RLAQKb01q7JxCBk9ATZyAfwTsyP4XwjBCLGtOHk4_RNrdW7SBPJT-8AACwG_diLrP9TGUipLPyNBM4wO5N_4e3fvpQx3IX9o0VUQgNGgYlgRMjZmQW1DM2UiX9x839lBSsT8euwjFLwdFdH9AhRY_bEda--rRk0HxhvFLm_KTqW2H9Xw8NmDFlPBL3ASY2765GkJ_czl1lXhHG7LPazNeBZLnnfS5R6CR0hAkZUUiGpIssorm9JkReuuWai_w6VZy9G4WZq1lOb-p15DXgsQryVVFkrwj3hODfZFk*%26id=45a0f18f-247a-4006-b7f6-249b9a912da5%26utm_medium=pa%26renditionType=pdf/1/0100019d0b7a7001-cfd2e775-43eb-4b73-93f4-bd2c4ee9b71f-000000/V7S7xDowbrzwdL8bxSm8AXPvVuENA26mORqcDn5l7JE=449>  	 	 | 	 	 Read <https://publishing.gs.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fpublishing.gs.com%2FtrustedLink%3Fopentoken=T1RLAQKb01q7JxCBk9ATZyAfwTsyP4XwjBCLGtOHk4_RNrdW7SBPJT-8AACwG_diLrP9TGUipLPyNBM4wO5N_4e3fvpQx3IX9o0VUQgNGgYlgRMjZmQW1DM2UiX9x839lBSsT8euwjFLwdFdH9AhRY_bEda--rRk0HxhvFLm_KTqW2H9Xw8NmDFlPBL3ASY2765GkJ_czl1lXhHG7LPazNeBZLnnfS5R6CR0hAkZUUiGpIssorm9JkReuuWai_w6VZy9G4WZq1lOb-p15DXgsQryVVFkrwj3hODfZFk*%26id=45a0f18f-247a-4006-b7f6-249b9a912da5%26utm_medium=pa/1/0100019d0b7a7001-cfd2e775-43eb-4b73-93f4-bd2c4ee9b71f-000000/VvEfZIosqMk3PM9qcCytBvZtnK5ebOWF5QKjfcvKeDM=449>  	
 	 
	
 	 
Cemig (CMIG4.SA): 4Q25 First Take: Better-than-expected 
	
20 March 2026 | 10:38AM BRT | Research | Equity 	
	
	
On March 19th, after market closing, Cemig announced 4Q25 results with an adjusted EBITDA (ex-VNR, one-offs and equity income) of BRL 1.86bn (-3% YoY), a beat to both GSe (BRL 1.72bn) and Bloomberg consensus (BRL 1.67bn). Bottom line followed reaching BRL 1.9bn (vs consensus' BRL 1.1bn and our BRL 789mn), also positively impacted by post-employment obligations agreements amounting to BRL 788mn. Key highlights from the set of results follow:

●

Operational results. Distribution adjusted EBITDA (ex-VNR) reached BRL 958mn (+4% YoY), a beat to our BRL 834mn. Billed volumes' growth of negative 4% was offset by a B Parcel positive effect of BRL 138mn in the quarter. Generation and transmission (including trading) adjusted EBITDA reached BRL 660mn (-31% YoY), a miss to our BRL 750mn dragged by a negative result in the generation arm driven by lower GSF and worse exposure to higher power prices in the quarter. Additionally, Gas operations were mostly in line with our numbers while holding and others costs amounted to positive BRL 56mn (vs our negative BRL 53mn forecast). Overall, results were better than expected driven by solid results in the distribution arm. 

●

Leverage continued increasing reaching ~2.3x ND/EBITDA (vs our 2.2x forecast), which compares to ~1.8x last quarter and 1.3x 4Q24, while total investments in 2025 reached BRL 6.6bn, mostly in line with planned capex of BRL 6.4bn for the year.

We remain Sell-rated on Cemig. While we acknowledge that management has been implementing cost efficiency measures and selling non-core assets, to the extent that Cemig remains an SOE we see limited room for further relevant cost efficiency gains, we see the company trading at 6.8% real IRR in a context of likely declining DY.

Read Full Report <https://publishing.gs.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fpublishing.gs.com%2FtrustedLink%3Fopentoken=T1RLAQKb01q7JxCBk9ATZyAfwTsyP4XwjBCLGtOHk4_RNrdW7SBPJT-8AACwG_diLrP9TGUipLPyNBM4wO5N_4e3fvpQx3IX9o0VUQgNGgYlgRMjZmQW1DM2UiX9x839lBSsT8euwjFLwdFdH9AhRY_bEda--rRk0HxhvFLm_KTqW2H9Xw8NmDFlPBL3ASY2765GkJ_czl1lXhHG7LPazNeBZLnnfS5R6CR0hAkZUUiGpIssorm9JkReuuWai_w6VZy9G4WZq1lOb-p15DXgsQryVVFkrwj3hODfZFk*%26id=45a0f18f-247a-4006-b7f6-249b9a912da5%26utm_medium=pa/2/0100019d0b7a7001-cfd2e775-43eb-4b73-93f4-bd2c4ee9b71f-000000/0suKVzfFZ0yT0Jzgx-3NtCRL4SnRl1Aii1CxOZkE4QE=449>

emails.subject: ENC: Brazil Retail & Pharma: The weight of gold – Semaglutide patents drop in Brazil (a quick Q&A handbook) (BTG Pactual's Guanais/Cesquim/Cendon)
emails.one_liner: Semaglutide patent expires in Brazil; market entry and price drops will be gradual, first products expected mid-2026.
emails.body:
________________________________

De: BTG Pactual Equities Research <sh-btgpactual-research@btgpactual.com>Em Nome deLuiz Guanais <luiz.guanais@btgpactual.com>
Enviado: sexta-feira, 20 de março de 2026 10:36:41 (UTC-03:00) Brasilia
Assunto: Brazil Retail & Pharma: The weight of gold – Semaglutide patents drop in Brazil (a quick Q&A handbook) (BTG Pactual's Guanais/Cesquim/Cendon)



BTG Pactual | Equity Research 


Sector Note - Brazil - Retail 


Brazil Retail & Pharma 


20-March-2026 


 


The weight of gold – Semaglutide patents drop in Brazil (a quick Q&A handbook) 


Read the full document here >> <https://www.btgpactual.com/research/report-view/118870>  

The expiration of semaglutide’s patent in Brazil on March 20 marks a clear structural milestone for the domestic obesity and diabetes treatment market, but the commercial implications are likely to unfold gradually rather than abruptly. While the end of Novo Nordisk’s ~20-year exclusivity formally opens the market to competition, Brazil still has no approved national alternative available in pharmacies. In practice, this places the market in a transition phase rather than triggering immediate disruption. Below, we organize the key points in a Q&A format, with more developed answers to support investor discussions.

1. What exactly happened with the Ozempic patent, and why does it matter?

The patent for semaglutide—the active ingredient in Ozempic, Wegovy, and Rybelsus—expired on March 20, 2026, effectively ending Novo Nordisk’s exclusivity after roughly two decades. The company had attempted to extend this protection by an additional 12 years through legal avenues but was unsuccessful. This development is structurally important because it legally allows other pharmaceutical companies to enter the market and develop competing products. However, unlike traditional small-molecule drugs, this does not translate into an immediate economic “cliff.” The absence of approved alternatives means Novo Nordisk retains effective control of the market in the near term.

Key takeaway: The patent expiry is a legal inflection point, but not yet a commercial one—this will depend on regulatory approvals and manufacturing scale.

2. Why are there still no alternatives available in Brazil?

Despite the patent expiration, no domestic alternatives have been approved so far, and the main bottleneck is regulatory rather than legal. Semaglutide is a complex peptide that sits at the intersection of synthetic drugs and biologics, which significantly raises the bar for approval. Companies must provide extensive safety and efficacy data, demonstrate high levels of manufacturing consistency, and prove analytical precision at the molecular level. On top of that, Brazil does not yet have a fully standardized regulatory pathway for this type of “hybrid” molecule, forcing ANVISA to conduct case-by-case evaluations often based on international benchmarks. This combination of scientific complexity and regulatory uncertainty has slowed down approvals.

Key takeaway: Approval complexity—not lack of interest—is the primary factor delaying competition.

3. How many companies are trying to enter this market?

There are currently 15 national applications under review at ANVISA, with broader indications suggesting that the total number of requests may be closer to 17. Of these, two products—developed by EMS and Ávita Care—are in a more advanced stage known as “cumprimento de exigências,” meaning that ANVISA has already reviewed their dossiers and requested additional clarifications. Seven other applications are under active review and may receive initial feedback in the first half of 2026, while the remaining filings have yet to enter the formal analysis phase. This represents one of the most competitive pipelines in Brazilian pharma in recent years, highlighting both the attractiveness and scale of the opportunity.

Key takeaway: Competition is likely to be broad and multi-layered, but it will materialize sequentially rather than all at once.

4. When will the first competing products actually launch?

The earliest realistic timeline for the first competing products to reach the market is July/August 2026. The leading candidates from EMS and Ávita Care received requests for additional information from ANVISA in early March and have up to 120 days to respond, although they may do so earlier. Approval will ultimately depend on the adequacy and quality of these responses. This implies a gap of at least three months between patent expiry and the arrival of any competition, followed by a gradual rollout of products.

Key takeaway: Expect a short period of continued exclusivity, followed by a gradual market opening starting in mid-2026.

5. What are the main regulatory hurdles delaying approval?

ANVISA’s review is focused on three critical technical areas. First, immunogenicity, which assesses whether patients may develop antibodies against

AXIA Energia (AXIA3.SA): Conversion of ISA Energia ordinary shares + asset swap announced; Positive 
	
20 March 2026 | 10:30AM BRT | Research | Equity 	
	
	
On March 19, after market close, Axia announced the conversion of ISA common shares into preferred shares <https://publishing.gs.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fapi.mziq.com%2Fmzfilemanager%2Fv2%2Fd%2Fabb77a17-3348-4bc7-849a-154998e06ca3%2F5ed48276-c4b6-4802-e71b-1aa05a6895d1%3Forigin=2/1/0100019d0b73ba1d-a52f9070-03f4-41b2-aa31-b8026d385b5c-000000/D-7fLmXkf_9iTqlEKzsfRpBDwShKr917YlVcUNyEiZg=449> , as well as the unwinding of transmission equity interests <https://publishing.gs.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fapi.mziq.com%2Fmzfilemanager%2Fv2%2Fd%2Fabb77a17-3348-4bc7-849a-154998e06ca3%2F01bc0ba6-9f56-e77f-bf22-d31be0f43efc%3Forigin=2/1/0100019d0b73ba1d-a52f9070-03f4-41b2-aa31-b8026d385b5c-000000/d3KRaDYALIHQBNW1d8uT8ziGByVkb4n9d5mOKy_f0Eo=449>  with ISA Energia.

We see both announcements as positive for Axia. In our view, the 19.8mn ordinary shares conversion into preferred shares is another step towards its stated intention for a sale of the company's participation in ISA Energia (as ordinary shares are illiquid), which at today market close prices would amount to ~BRL 4bn in proceeds for Axia if sold (~2% of market cap for reference).

Regarding the unwinding of equity interests in the special purpose entities Madeira and Garanhuns transmission lines, we also view the move as another positive step towards corporate structure simplification and monetization of illiquid assets (consistent with the company's strategic plan). The eventual conclusion of the transaction will lead to a BRL 1.2bn receivable + the consolidation of the Garanhuns transmission asset, contributing an additional BRL 134mn to Axia's consolidated EBITDA and BRL 43mn to net debt.

All in all, we see both announcements as positive for Axia. We reiterate our Buy rating on the company, backed by its strong combination of a relatively uncontracted power generation portfolio in a context of rising power prices, further asset optimization initiatives (such as the ones discussed in this report) and a clear path to higher dividends ahead.

Read Full Report <https://publishing.gs.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fpublishing.gs.com%2FtrustedLink%3Fopentoken=T1RLAQIFOzwZ7oCXMp3Rc6sWsm3K56ykahDyEF_jLP3uTeo8gsdWRM06AACwkTDdmc4BoxLm5M2j1ZR2Z6a3Ef8z3MdvPptnxW-JC43nDktZdEcmCHSng4oggR-6HnoGfRcrXmO7s7Ft8dILyFb6EtUn73X8M_rRjAWHn8ru11yKgJ5R74z6HNXTf667cAsFwrkuNwsSboRh0y-Hqw2zw6szjbJqBA1HwPnFdMo-ggSKPxYltP9Tss7MCHZZ4QHMUV3sT_FtvU6_S0c7VvWiGr-goaxmYb3CEE-l-pU*%26id=b8a91335-4cd1-4e98-b34f-db2ff90bcbfb%26utm_medium=pa/2/0100019d0b73ba1d-a52f9070-03f4-41b2-aa31-b8026d385b5c-000000/2VmDRYjO4g5g-pnRn23sYVqEEIDp6lkq3GMNAl0bOm8=449>
