LATAM Today: March 20, 2026 
	
20 March 2026 | 9:25AM EDT | Research | Economics 	
	
	
 	 

ARGENTINA 

 	 

Softer Imports and Exports in February 

Bottom Line: The trade balance recorded a US$788mn surplus in February, larger than the US$275mn surplus recorded a year ago but down from the US$2,189mn surplus in the previous month.

Imports fell short of expectations for the fourth consecutive month, supporting the trade balance amid lower-than-expected exports in February. All in all, the cumulative trade balance over the last 12 months rose to a US$13.9bn surplus, approximately 2.0% of GDP, up from US$11.3bn (about 1.6% of GDP) by year-end 2025. Looking ahead, higher oil prices may boost Argentina's trade balance. The country is now a net oil exporter with a US$7.7bn energy surplus over the last twelve months.

DETAILS:

1. 

The trade balance recorded a US$788mn surplus in February, up from the US$275mn surplus recorded a year ago. The cumulative trade balance over the last 12 months is now at a US$13.9bn surplus, approximately 2.0% of GDP, up from the US$11.3bn surplus as of year-end 2025.

2. 

In February, the energy trade balance posted a US$486mn surplus, softer than the US$642mn surplus recorded in the previous year on the back of 27.6% lower energy exports (US$631mn vs US$872mn last year) as energy imports fell by 36.8% (US$145mn vs US$230mn last year).

3. 

Total trade flows (sum of exports and imports) declined by 7.2% yoy in February driven by lower exports which fell 2.9% yoy to US$5.96bn (below the US$6.82bn anticipated by economists surveyed by the central bank) and an 11.8% yoy decline in imports to US$5.17bn (similarly below the median estimate of economists surveyed by the BCRA at US$5.83bn).

4. 

The decline in exports was driven by a 7.1% yoy decline in quantities exported as export prices increased by 4.4% yoy. Exports of primary products rose by 8.2% yoy, but processed food exports declined by 10.1% yoy. Manufacturing exports, in turn, increased by 8.6% yoy and fuel and energy exports declined (-27.6% yoy). Sequentially, exports declined by 16.2% mom sa.

5. 

Lower imports, in turn, were driven by a 14.9% yoy decline in quantities as import prices rose by 3.7% yoy. The decline in imports was broad-based with capital goods imports down 7.6% yoy, and their respective parts and accessories declining 24.9% yoy. Fuel imports (-36.8% yoy), intermediate (-4.1% yoy) and consumption (-3.0% yoy) goods imports, as well as vehicle imports (-5.7% yoy), all declined from a year ago. Sequentially, imports increased 8.2% mom sa.

 	 

Data Today: 

Real GDP (4Q25); in line with the EMAE, monthly activity indicator, we forecast real GDP rose by 0.8% qoq sa in the fourth quarter of 2025. Our sequential forecast is consistent with activity rising 2.2% yoy in the quarter and 4.4% in all 2025.

SA

 	 

MEXICO 

 	 

Real GDP Firmed During 4Q2025 Driven by Domestic Demand; Negative Contribution from Net Exports 

Bottom Line: Real GDP growth accelerated to 0.86% qoq sa during 4Q2025, driven by robust household consumption (1.0% qoq sa), gross fixed investment (0.7% qoq sa) and also government consumption (0.8% qoq sa), mitigated by the negative contribution from net exports. The variation of inventories added a minor 10bp to quarterly growth, and the statistical discrepancy contributed with 1.8pt. emails.subject: ENC: RADL/HYPE: Readthrough from India’s Semaglutide Patent Expiry to Brazil
emails.one_liner: Expiração patente Semaglutida no Brasil hoje impacta RADL/HYPE. Redução custo 50-60% na Índia impulsiona demanda.
emails.body:
________________________________

De: Joseph Giordano <joseph.giordano@jpmresearchmail.com>
Enviado: sexta-feira, 20 de março de 2026 10:31:03 (UTC-03:00) Brasilia
Para: Sell Side <sellside@torkcapital.com.br>
Assunto: RADL/HYPE: Readthrough from India’s Semaglutide Patent Expiry to Brazil


 	Latin America Equity Research 	

Hi Tork,

 <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12833371&ad=4072023978&h=bZBR4X0&sig=74hW6u9BBhoqtS68lMHirv2o4x0&v=2&url=https://www.extelinsights.com/voting> 	

Readthrough from India’s Semaglutide Patent Expiry to Brazil 


We would like to flag the report from our colleagues based in India: Latika Chopra, CFA and Bansi Desai, CFA (link <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12833371&ad=4072023978&h=YSidsdS&sig=XGJI2aMfXMyzloqyLAxGviFWwdA&v=2&isig=OFtAk7Pycgu8JJB94uatgAGLrp7&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/email/scx/-emau7qo/GPS-5239429-0/5c21334f-6c41-4c28-a93c-4c4447925df1> ) highlighting the impacts of Semaglutide patent expiry on Indian healthcare and consumer sectors. In this context, it is worth flagging that the same patent expired today in Brazil but the local regulator, ANVISA, is yet to grant licenses for the registration of generic and branded generic alternatives. Below are the main highlights. 

*	Price is the throttle. In India, semaglutide going off‑patent is cutting monthly costs by roughly 50–60% as generics enter (from ~Rs10–20k//US$106-214 toward ~Rs3–5k//US$32-53), which materially improves access but still concentrates uptake among affluent, urban consumers given out‑of‑pocket burden. 
*	Demand curve: urban first, modest penetration. Even with lower prices, modeled compliance (lower for obesity than Type‑2 diabetes) implies low‑single‑digit penetration near term with negligible rural adoption—demand anchored in big cities and higher incomes; watch how payer coverage and retail discounts in LatAm could bend this curve faster or slower. 
*	Elasticity matters. India’s team models GLP‑1 users rising from ~120k (Feb’26) to ~2.5mn/5.3mn by 2030/2035 as prices fall—clear evidence that sustained price erosion unlocks step‑ups in demand; if LatAm sees similar generic dynamics and partial reimbursement, expect a non‑linear adoption ramp from a small base. 
*	Consumption signal resilience. Global category reads show smaller portions and a pivot from full‑sugar toward diet/hydration/protein; quantified guidance is modest—e.g., 0.5–1.5% 10‑yr volume drag for snacks—suggesting demand effects trail pricing‑led adoption and remain manageable at low penetration levels. 

  
	

LatAm Retail & Healthcare

emails.subject: ENC: Helbor (Buy): Big (positive) land sale to curb leverage (BTG Pactual's Cambauva/Fabris/Pascale)
emails.one_liner: Helbor sells stake/CEPACs to Cyrela, aiming to curb leverage; R$40mn upfront, R$250mn later. Buy, TP R$4.10.
emails.body:
________________________________

De: BTG Pactual Equities Research <sh-btgpactual-research@btgpactual.com>Em Nome deGustavo Cambauva <gustavo.cambauva@btgpactual.com>
Enviado: sexta-feira, 20 de março de 2026 10:26:30 (UTC-03:00) Brasilia
Assunto: Helbor (Buy): Big (positive) land sale to curb leverage (BTG Pactual's Cambauva/Fabris/Pascale)



BTG Pactual | Equity Research 


Company Note - Brazil - Real Estate 


Helbor (Buy Rating; TP: R$ 4.10) 


20-March-2026 


Big (positive) land sale to curb leverage 


Read the full document here >> <https://www.btgpactual.com/research/report-view/118865>  


Cyrela is buying a land play from Helbor


Helbor announced today an MoU with Cyrela for the acquisition of a stake in an SPE (HESA 159), aiming to develop a low-income project (likely under the Vivaz brand) in the “Semp Toshiba” land plot (owned by HESA 159). After the transaction, which is subject to precedent conditions, Helbor will hold a 30% stake in HESA 159 (it currently owns 55%). In addition, the company also announced that Cyrela is acquiring ~19k CEPACs (Certificados de Potencial Adicional de Construção), in cash, held by the SPE.


Positive move, aiming to curb leverage; Buy rated (0.4x P/TBV)


We believe the move sends a positive message: Helbor seems committed to taking steps to curb leverage and increase asset turnover. However, we highlight that the immediate deleverage effects from this transaction should be limited, since: (i) we estimate that the company will collect ~R$40mn in cash upon closing; plus (ii) another ~R$250mn during the project, which may be launched in 2027 (as Helbor will retain a financial stake in the project, becoming a partner of Cyrela). We see this deal as positive, since the cash collection represents 16% of Helbor’s net debt, implying a relevant deleveraging (which we see as crucial for a stock re-rating). We have recently upgraded Helbor to Buy (see the report here <https://www.btgpactual.com/research/report-view/109585> ), as the stock looks excessively discounted (0.4x P/TBV).

________________________________

De: Rodolfo Angele, CFA <rodolfo.r.angele@jpmresearchmail.com>
Enviado: sexta-feira, 20 de março de 2026 10:13:55 (UTC-03:00) Brasilia
Para: Sell Side <sellside@torkcapital.com.br>
Assunto: JPM | Petrobras: It’s all about Governance - Brazilian Government Sets Diesel Reference Price Framework (Angele/Carvalho/Cunha)


 	Latin America Equity Research 	

Petrobras


It’s All About Governance – Brazilian Government Sets Diesel Reference Price Framework

 	 
By Rodolfo Angele, CFA AC <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12833125&ad=4071871501&h=60UqH5T&sig=F7EOr7d8PVvmax3lvV0kfV02msg&v=2&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/analyst/U950947> , Milene Clifford Carvalho <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12833125&ad=4071871501&h=kT4VllG&sig=DrNGXTgZU8TIiF7NmmsRcIKNchS&v=2&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/analyst/N699794> , Henrique Cunha, CFA <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12833125&ad=4071871501&h=YWJaXQk&sig=Bsrrvc6poUZhHoW7zHtLoJvE1ZF&v=2&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/analyst/O753834>  

Click here for the full document and disclaimers <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12833125&ad=4071871501&h=KQ8EcaQ&sig=M04eLU6aiJJTFvcoCMChO37NZsi&v=2&isig=NOmBtqA4WBaUqUl4isKpE8Nh47f&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/email/scx/-84kp7gh/GPS-5241783-0/598504a5-cc1f-4124-98d4-c19aba483d33> 

PBR, PBR US	 
Overweight	 
Price (19 Mar 26):$19.78	 
Price Target (Dec-26):$16.50	 
	
PETR3.SA, PETR3 BZ	 
Overweight	 
Price (19 Mar 26):R$51.57	 
Price Target (Dec-26):R$46.50	 
	
PETR4.SA, PETR4 BZ	 
Overweight	 
Price (19 Mar 26):R$46.78	 
Price Target (Dec-26):R$44.00	 
	

Our take: the announced reference price still implies a 30% discount to parity, but the price increase is still a decision of Petrobras. Yesterday, the federal government published Decree 12,883/2026 (here <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12833125&ad=4071871501&h=anH9AUO&sig=My2fa9vTzvGvjJTgKONGqzHjH5A&v=2&url=https://www.planalto.gov.br/ccivil_03/_ato2023-2026/2026/decreto/D12883.htm> ) on the determination of the diesel reference price for subsidies in Brazil (see Diesel Relief Measures and 12% Export Tax <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12833125&ad=4071871501&h=5QOePKI&sig=NvhCfP9WIdDGAeDHpX4PEHpWiBR&v=2&isig=ExrIHRvCsLM3N6xI9Nm0e1g4GAT&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/email/scx/ap0iil56/GPS-5233665-0/598504a5-cc1f-4124-98d4-c19aba483d33>  for additional context). The decree assigns to the Ministry of Mines and Energy the responsibility for setting the current reference price, with ANP defining the methodology for subsequent updates. It also establishes differentiated methodologies that, in Petrobras’s case, set the current R$3.70/l price as the cap for receiving the R$0.32/l subsidy announced last week. We calculate that, at this reference price, even factoring in the subsidy, the discount to import parity remains approximately 30%. We note that Petrobras operates under a formal pricing policy and governance framework. While external pressures may persist, we expect the company to continue making decisions consistent with these guidelines. 

	*	Decree 12,883 designates the MME to set the reference price and introduces differentiated methodologies for importers and producers. Decree 12,883 assigns the definition of the diesel reference price to the Ministry of Mines and Energy, with updates in subsequent periods to be determined by ANP. The decree also establishes distinct methodologies for importers and producers: (a) for importers and for producers refining imported crude or domestically sourced crude acquired from third parties, the reference price will follow import‑parity; and (b) for producers refining their own domestic crude, the reference price is set at the producer’s realization price at the time of the decree (approximately R$3.70/l in the case of Petrobras) plus R$0.32/l. 
	*	At the current reference price, the discount to parity would remain at 30% for refiners. We calculate that, at this reference price, even taking the subvention into account, the discount would marginally improve from 36% to 30%, but still remain strongly below parity. 
	*	The adoption of the new price reference is a choice, and investor focus remains on Petrobras’s governance. Historically, under the new policy, Petrobras has avoided rapid adjustments to short‑term volatility, prioritizing stable, predictable prices over immediately narrowing discrepancies with import parity. That said, after roughly three weeks of rising prices and a widening gap to parity, Petrobras announced a R$0.38/l diesel adjustment last week, underscoring its commitment to governance and its pricing framework. If the reference price remains as announced, we would expect Petrobras to decline the subsidy and maintain adherence to its established pricing policy.

________________________________

De: BTG Pactual Equities Research <sh-btgpactual-research@btgpactual.com>Em Nome deMarylaila Quintiliano <marylaila.quintiliano@btgpactual.com>
Enviado: sexta-feira, 20 de março de 2026 10:10:33 (UTC-03:00) Brasilia
Assunto: RESUMIDO (CEMIG, CYRELA, TUPY, WIZ, PANVEL, PETRO & FUEL, AXIA & ISA ENERGIA, STEELS, RETAIL, LOG, ESG, LATAM, EVENTOS)



Caros (as), as votações do *Extel/Institutional Investor* encerram amanhã, 20 de março. Contamos com seu apoio nas categorias Sales Brasil & Latam, Research Brasil & Latam, Trading e Corporate Access em https://www.extelinsights.com/voting/surveys

 

 <https://salesforce-mailing-distribution-prod.s3.sa-east-1.amazonaws.com/Mailing%20Distribution/assets/2026-02-13/1770987774455.png> 

 <https://salesforce-mailing-distribution-prod.s3.sa-east-1.amazonaws.com/Mailing%20Distribution/assets/2026-02-13/1770987774459.png> 

BTG Pactual - RESUMIDO

** This is a sales call and should be read as such **

 

 

** BRASIL **

CEMIG (Junqueira/Gushiken/Schutz; NEUTRAL): Resultados bons no 4Q, com EBITDA ajustado de R$1.86bn (-3.5% y/y), 6% acima da nossa estimativa de R$1.75bn, refletindo melhor desempenho em distribuição e G&T + trading, parcialmente compensado por Gasmig mais fraca. O lucro líquido atingiu R$1.88bn (vs. BTGe R$1.93bn), beneficiado por menores despesas financeiras, maior equivalência patrimonial e impacto positivo de reversões de provisões. A companhia também anunciou R$676mn em dividendos no trimestre, elevando o total de 2025 para R$3.51bn (9% de yield). A alavancagem subiu para 2.3x ND/EBITDA (vs. 1.76x no 3Q25). Read the full document here >> <https://www.btgpactual.com/research/report-view/118839>  

 

CYRELA (Cambauva/Fabris/Pascale): Reportou #s bons e em linha, com FCF levemente acima. Rec R$3,23bn (+29% y/y), impulsionada também por mudança contábil no reconhecimento de receita, enquanto mg bruta ajust. ficou em 33,7% e EPS em R$1,55 (+41% y/y), em linha, com ROE anualizado forte de 27%. FCF foi de R$74mn, ajudado por ~R$240mn em dividendos antecipados das JVs, e ND/Equity ficou em 17%, acima da média histórica, mas ainda confortável. Apesar do ambiente mais desafiador no mid/high, Buy mantido, com ação negociando a 6x P/E 2026E. Read the full document here >> <https://www.btgpactual.com/research/report-view/118841>  

 

TUPY (Marquiori/Recchia/Alkmim; NEUTRAL): Reportou 4T25 ainda mais fraco do que já esperávamos, com receita abaixo do previsto, margens muito pressionadas e sem sinais claros de recuperação. Além dos efeitos não recorrentes, o resultado operacional também veio fraco, refletindo volumes mais baixos em várias frentes, especialmente em componentes estruturais no mercado doméstico. A alavancagem voltou a subir e a leitura para os próximos meses segue desafiadora, já que ainda faltam sinais mais consistentes de melhora em volumes, geração de caixa e resultados. Seguimos preferindo ficar de fora até haver mais visibilidade, sustentando visão de NEUTRAL. Read the full document here >> <https://www.btgpactual.com/research/report-view/118845>  

 

WIZ (Buchpiguel/Rosman/Paura; NEUTRAL): Reportou 4T25 com crescimento de receita mais fraco, mas rentabilidade recorrente melhor do que parecia no headline. Apesar de a linha final reportada ter vindo poluída por impairment sem efeito caixa e pelo impacto da venda da Wiz Concept, o lucro ajustado ficou acima das nossas estimativas, ajudado principalmente por menores despesas financeiras. A geração de caixa segue forte, a alavancagem está baixa e o aumento do payout para 50% reforça uma leitura mais positiva de capital allocation. Ainda assim, seguimos cautelosos por conta da menor visibilidade sobre o crescimento de BRB Seguros, que continua sendo o principal ponto de atenção da tese. NEUTRAL. Read the full document here >> <https://www.btgpactual.com/research/report-view/118846> 

 

PANVEL (Cesquim/Guanais/ Cendon; BUY): Resultados sólidos e em linha, com forte SSS, expansão de margens e melhora na geração de caixa. A receita de varejo cresceu +18% y/y para R$1,67bn, com SSS de +14,7% y/y e ganho de market share (+70bps). A receita consolidada foi de R$1,68bn (+16% y/y), puxada por branded drugs (+21% y/y, com GLP-1). O EBITDA ajustado atingiu R$105mn (+28% y/y; em linha), com margem de 6,2% (+50bps), enquanto o lucro líquido foi de R$45mn (+35% y/y). O FCF foi positivo em R$42mn (vs. -R$30mn), com alavancagem em 0,9x ND/EBITDA. Leitura: bom momento operacional, com crescimento, margem e caixa, sustentando nossa visão BUY. Read the full document here >> <https://www.btgpactual.com/research/report-view/118849>  

 

PETROBRAS & FUEL DISTRIBUTION (Almeida/Cunha): Governo definiu regras do subsídio ao diesel: Petrobras mantém preço em R$3,65/l e recebe R$0,32/l, elevando o preço efetivo para ~R$3,97/l. A empresa ainda pode subir preços, mas perderia o subsídio. Há possibilidade de retomada de leilões, permitindo vender

emails.subject: ENC: Jefferies Morning Highlights - Int’l Equities (03/20/2025)
emails.one_liner: Highlights Macro, TMT, Financials, Utilities, Mining with China energy dominance, $212B index rebalance, supply chain hedges, and Mag7 call spreads.
emails.body:
________________________________

De: Fabio Buffi <fbuffi@jefferies.com>
Enviado: sexta-feira, 20 de março de 2026 10:06:52 (UTC-03:00) Brasilia
Assunto: Jefferies Morning Highlights - Int’l Equities (03/20/2025)



SALES & TRADING NOTE (not a product of Jefferies Research Department)

	1.	Macro & Strategy
	2.	TMT
	3.	Financials
	4.	Power & Utilities
	5.	Metals & Mining

 

 

1.	Today’s Lens

 

 

1.1           Macro & Strategy

 

GREED & Fear | Chinamaxxing (read full report here <https://jefferies.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12833006&ad=4071734465&h=kY7Om7M&sig=1neEM90UJfE8gSbPDbzflr3VgBA&v=2&url=https://javatar.bluematrix.com/links2/pdf/ca2f638d-2b82-4ee9-97db-17c52826c986> )

Investors are at risk of forgetting that economic growth is energy consumed. Risk assets are overly calm on the Iranian conflict. The decision by Trump to delay his visit to Beijing suggests he is not about to disengage from Iran; he risks a “Suez moment”. 2) China has secured energy dominance in a global context, and thus, is on course to win the AI arms race. China is packaging its massive reserves of cheap electricity & computing power to process “tokens”; the result is a surge in “token exports”. 3) The energy bottleneck in the US is one reason why AI capex not to grow as fast in the US as currently anticipated. There is a growing risk that this year could mark the peak of US AI capex in this cycle, which is certainly not discounted in Semiconductor stocks. 4) It should be only a matter of time before we see Chinese consumer spending inflect, given the ongoing build up in household savings. This is in the context of Chris’ base case that the residential property market is in the process of bottoming out. China is the equity market globally least impacted by the Iran war, and news-flow globally, which is why it has outperformed since the US-Israel attack for the past 3 weeks. The opposite is true for most of Asean which was already out of favor before the conflict.

 

-------------------------------------------------

 

March 2026 Quarterly Rebalance Today | JEF Index Strategy (RSVP for full material)

 

he March 2026 Quarterly Rebalance becomes effective at the close of today, March 20, 2026. Several of the major US and global indexes rebalance as part of the quarterly, including S&P, Russell, Nasdaq and FTSE. We estimate this rebalance to be netted a $212 billion total US trade ($106 billion per side).

 

Contents include: 

1.            Rebalance Highlights

2.            Turnover Summary

3.            Sector and Industry Exposures

4.            Top Single Name Flows

5.            S&P 500, 400 & 600 Constituent Changes

6.            Rebalance Performance

7.            Historic Rebalance Day Volume Profiles

8.            Jefferies Algorithmic Trading Solutions for EOD / Auction Liquidity

 

 

-------------------------------------------------

 

JEF VOL | Macro and Mag7

 

Macro

Heading into the second half of March investors appear well hedged with the put/call ratio near 1-yr highs and elevated short interest across ETFs. As such, the desk favors supply chain related hedges because even if tensions cool, damage to supply chains will take time to amend. Strikes on Qatar’s Ras Laffan wiped out ~17% of the regions LNG capacity. For context, Qatar produces roughly 20% of global LNG supply. Furthermore, the doubling of fuel oil threatens the transportation supply. Companies that produce and transport LNG stand to benefit from these bottlenecks. Chemical producers will also likely be disrupted by higher oil and naphtha prices as well as shipping delays. Fortunately for DOW, they’re relatively better positioned versus peers because its inputs are tied to US light feedstocks.

 

Buy DOW May 42.5/50 calls spreads for $1.05 (ref 37.49)

*	Capture earnings currently estimated for 4/23
*	6.1x leverage, 20d, buy 49.4v / sell 53.8v

 

Buy VG Jun 17.5 calls for $1.65 (ref 14.29)

*	Capture earnings currently estimated for 5/15
*	42d, 91v, short interest as a % of float is ~50% (from Bloomberg)
*	Stock almost hit $17 yesterday, traded in a 20% range yesterday alone, 16% range the day before 

 

 

Mag 7

To reiterate from last week, we continue to like upside in the Mag7 names via call spread collars. Positioning is at the extremes and we believe there could be a flight to safer, high-quality names once investors start adding risk again. Updated chart from Vanda below on positioning and sentiment in the group. Skew is still bid across these names (and the market overall), setting up well for risk reversals or call spread collars, and May expiry catches the next round of earnings for most of the group.

 

MSFT – Among Mag7 names, MSFT is trading closest to its 52-week low (+12.8%) and is now only 1% off its 6-month low. Our analyst, Brent Thill, remains bullish after meeting with IR last week and thinks th

emails.one_liner: México: PIB real 4T25 cresce 0.86% qoq sa, impulsionado por demanda doméstica, atingindo 0.6% em 2025.
emails.body:
________________________________

De: GS Macro Economics Research <macro@alerts.publishing.gs.com>
Enviado: sexta-feira, 20 de março de 2026 09:48:56 (UTC-03:00) Brasilia
Para: Sell Side <sellside@torkcapital.com.br>
Assunto: Mexico: Real GDP Firmed During 4Q2025 Driven by Domestic Demand; Negative Contribution from Net Exports (Ramos)


 	 
 	 
	 
 	 Print PDF <https://publishing.gs.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fpublishing.gs.com%2FtrustedLink%3Fopentoken=T1RLAQLHdEHqGH795W9Zhrh5hHz-6QDDIhCIWAGT6fHhrhyx65qYQBSkAACwvYdGUwRXaDjziPySKj2qwoMDaFnB-ggVdE4tCo-b7xutlg7J9yl6H2o4pnPzWybW45qGsDitxaqavrC8PmcUT3QqoyPkHNUKboBabCz07jxn6IYQA32BFpJh1cGeTf273lRJ-86j4Aa2_T1T8zS2b-C_itiVzWYVq06iKiINkvNKGsxiskqLydBZydEr9SFEy__6pHr-zP0hs5PsVSVxouO2CV-RE0wufHdjCdKjiRE*%26id=6cf01784-42fb-468b-9c71-91877897ec09%26utm_medium=pa%26renditionType=pdf/1/0100019d0b4aac0d-5b6aea90-410c-4277-a128-861f888cf8a3-000000/LDbab6G8L7_4yxRpobW7TuHgyS_s26aJoxsL9GzxFFo=449>  	 	 | 	 	 Read <https://publishing.gs.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fpublishing.gs.com%2FtrustedLink%3Fopentoken=T1RLAQLHdEHqGH795W9Zhrh5hHz-6QDDIhCIWAGT6fHhrhyx65qYQBSkAACwvYdGUwRXaDjziPySKj2qwoMDaFnB-ggVdE4tCo-b7xutlg7J9yl6H2o4pnPzWybW45qGsDitxaqavrC8PmcUT3QqoyPkHNUKboBabCz07jxn6IYQA32BFpJh1cGeTf273lRJ-86j4Aa2_T1T8zS2b-C_itiVzWYVq06iKiINkvNKGsxiskqLydBZydEr9SFEy__6pHr-zP0hs5PsVSVxouO2CV-RE0wufHdjCdKjiRE*%26id=6cf01784-42fb-468b-9c71-91877897ec09%26utm_medium=pa/1/0100019d0b4aac0d-5b6aea90-410c-4277-a128-861f888cf8a3-000000/T6leUskkbsuYz-EileEvxFjMapU_yZi_Q7PxzkUbA0c=449>  	
 	 
	
 	 
Mexico: Real GDP Firmed During 4Q2025 Driven by Domestic Demand; Negative Contribution from Net Exports (Ramos) 
	
20 March 2026 | 8:43AM EDT | Research | Economics 	
	
	
Bottom Line: Real GDP growth accelerated to 0.86% qoq sa during 4Q2025, driven by robust household consumption (1.0% qoq sa), gross fixed investment (0.7% qoq sa) and also government consumption (0.8% qoq sa), mitigated by the negative contribution from net exports. The variation of inventories added a minor 10bp to quarterly growth, and the statistical discrepancy contributed with 1.8pt. On the supply side, the 4Q25 expansion of real activity was driven by the secondary (0.9% mom sa) and labor-intensive services (0.9% qoq sa) sectors. Within the secondary sector we highlight soft manufacturing (0.1% qoq sa, vs -1.2% qoq sa during 3Q) and the rebound in construction (+4.3% qoq sa, from -2.5% qoq sa in 3Q25) activity. Mining activity increased by 1.5% qoq sa, second positive print in ten quarters. The statistical carry-over for 2026 is at 0.8%. Overall, real GDP growth decelerated to 0.6% in 2025, from the already lackluster 1.4% in 2024, with household consumption up 1.1% and GFI down 6.3%. Despite higher US tariffs lingering and the uncertainty exports performed well (7.0%) exceeding the annual growth of imports (3.1%).

Going forward, activity is expected to face headwinds from soft business confidence and peaking credit cycle. On the positive side, generous minimum wage increases, and a resilient labor market should offer some support to domestic demand. Investment is expected to continue to be impacted by both domestic and external uncertainty, in particular related to trade-policy direction in the US and the ongoing USMCA review.

DETAILS:

1. 

Real GDP grew 0.86% qoq sa during 4Q2025; a clear rebound from the 0.07% qoq sa Q3 variation. On an annual basis real GDP rose 1.8% yoy in 4Q2025, firming from -0.1% yoy 3Q25 print.

2. 

The economy grew a modest 0.6% in 2025, down from the lackluster 1.4% in 2024.

3. 

Final domestic demand firmed to +0.9% qoq sa in 4Q25, from 0.7% qoq sa in 3Q25. Private consumption grew a solid 1.0% qoq sa in 4Q25 (vs 1.3% qoq sa in 3Q25), government consumption 0.8% qoq sa, and gross fixed capital formation 0.7% qoq sa (vs -0.9% qoq sa in 3Q25), with private investment up 0.6% qoq sa (-0.5% qoq sa in 3Q25) and public investment up 2.0% qoq sa (-3.3% qoq sa in 3Q25).

4. 

Net exports' contribution to sequential growth was negative: -2.0pt, vs -1.9pt in Q3, given the underperformance of exports (+1.1% qoq sa) vis-à-vis the buoyancy of imports (+5.16% qoq sa). The variation of inventories added a minor 0.1pt neutral to 4Q sequential activity, and the statistical discrepancy added 1.8pt.

5. 

On the supply side, the 4Q25 expansion of real activity was driven by the secondary (0.9% qoq sa) and labor-intensive services (0.9% qoq sa) sectors, mitigated by the contraction of the low-weight primary sector (-1.4% qoq sa). Within the secondary sector we highlight soft manufacturing (0.1% qoq sa, vs -1.2% qoq sa during 3Q) and the rebound in construction (+4.3% qoq sa, from -2.5% qoq sa in 3Q25) activity. Mining activity increased by 1.5% qoq sa, second positive print in ten quarters.

6. 

On a seasonally and calendar adjusted basis, real GDP rose 1.8% yoy sa in 4Q25 (-0.1% yoy sa in 3Q25), with positive contributions from household consumption (4.0% yoy sa) and government c

emails.subject: ENC: Chile & North Andean Utilities Clippings & Thoughts (BTG Pactual's Gonzalez/Mery)
emails.one_liner: Aguas Andinas: 4T in-line, BUY. Peru gas crisis ends, but 1T26 impacts on TGP/Colbun. Alto Maipo/Cerro Dominador resume ops.
emails.body:
________________________________

De: BTG Pactual Equities Research <sh-btgpactual-research@btgpactual.com>Em Nome deFernan Gonzalez <fernan.gonzalez@btgpactual.com>
Enviado: sexta-feira, 20 de março de 2026 09:16:37 (UTC-03:00) Brasilia
Assunto: Chile & North Andean Utilities Clippings & Thoughts (BTG Pactual's Gonzalez/Mery)



 <https://salesforce-mailing-distribution-prod.s3.sa-east-1.amazonaws.com/Mailing%20Distribution/assets/2025-01-31/1738324176420.png> 

 

 

LAST DAY, PLEASE DON’T FORGET TO VOTE IN THE EXTEL SURVEY

We are in the final day of Extel / Institutional Investor voting. The ranking is extremely important to our franchise and taken very seriously by us. If you value our work, we kindly ask that you consider voting for our team. Every single vote means a lot to us. You can find us in the “Chile Research”  and “Electric & Other Utilities” categories.

 

CHILE AND NORTH ANDEAN UTILITIES

 

Aguas Andinas: A Clear Quarter With Upside Ahead

Aguas Andinas reported an in-line 4Q performance, with revenues reaching CLP192bn, up 7.0% y/y, mainly driven by tariff indexation (+8.8% y/y) as billed volumes increased only 0.6% y/y. Operating costs rose 6.5% y/y, growing below the top line. As a result, Ebitda increased 7.4% y/y to CLP97bn, with the Ebitda margin expanding marginally by 21bps to 50.5%. Net income reached CLP43bn, representing a solid 37% y/y increase, driven by the stronger operating performance and lower readjustment units as inflationary pressures eased compared to the same period last year. All figures were in line with our estimates. The indebtedness ratio remained broadly stable at 1.39x, while ND/Ebitda declined from 3.74x in 4Q24 to 3.60x in 4Q25. Overall, this was a solid quarter, with high single-digit Ebitda growth, although we continue to see room for margin expansion, particularly as tariffs were set to increase above local inflation. We believe operating leverage has yet to fully materialize, as the tariff reset is only partially in effect and will continue to be rolled out during the current year. We expect margins to improve gradually in 2026, with a more meaningful expansion by 2027. At the same time, provisions for bad debt remain at healthy levels, while maintenance costs appear to be normalizing, both of which should help ease margin pressure in the coming quarters. Electricity costs also declined during the period and should continue trending lower, as new contracts come into effect at more favorable rates, alongside ongoing efforts to optimize consumption by shifting from regulated to unregulated supply. Overall, we expect the full impact of the tariff reset and operating leverage to materialize over the next two years. We remain buyers. Full Report <https://www.btgpactual.com/research/report-view/118832> 

 

Peruvian Utilities: Gas crisis comes to an end, but 1Q26 damages remain

Transportadora de Gas del Perú (TGP) announced over the weekend the completion of repair works on its natural gas pipeline following a leak that occurred earlier in the month in Cusco, enabling the progressive restoration of gas transport and liquids. The restoration process involved complex technical operations to equalize pressure across the 730-kilometer pipeline, with a gradual normalization of service prioritizing residential users, healthcare facilities, and public transportation before expanding to large industries and power generators. After two challenging weeks, this puts an end to the energy emergency and supply restrictions that had impacted various sectors of the Peruvian economy. Spot prices in the Santa Rosa node have already normalized to ~US$30/MWh, as has gas generation. That said, we estimate that Colbun’s Fenix Power will report a negative Ebitda in the quarter.

 

Weather: IRI says chances of El Niño in May-July at 78%

The International Research Institute (IRI) at Columbia published its latest ENSO forecast in which they see big chances of El Niño developing from May onwards, which differs quite a lot from what NOAA published last week. The official forecasts from NOAA and the IRI are often quite similar. However, occasionally they may differ noticeably. There can be several possible reasons for differences. One is the human forecasters, using their experience and judgment, may disagree to some degree with the models, which may have known biases. Another reason is the models are not run at the same time that the forecasters make their assessment, so that the starting ENSO conditions may be slightly different between the two times. The charts are updated at two different times of the month, typically about a week apart, with the IRI forecast run later. IRI sees a 72% chance of El Niño in the May-July period (NOAA below 50%) and an 80%probability in the June-August window (vs just north of 60% from NOAA).

 

 <https://salesforce-mailing-distribution-prod.s3.sa-east-1.amazonaws.com/Mailing%20Distribution/assets/2026-03-20/

By Marcelo Motta AC <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12832164&ad=4071257639&h=E0Mm9Ge&sig=FQ6jp3bIKIFvGAX6Yr15j5N0rbM&v=2&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/analyst/E194704> , Jonathan S. Koutras <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12832164&ad=4071257639&h=9vDGKvq&sig=ExbbTYKpvin8m48TQ8WZvMtBrFk&v=2&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/analyst/V737771>  

Click here for the full document and disclaimers <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12832164&ad=4071257639&h=I-ZsIoF&sig=3ubsY7e8zI7fza0eTRPrbfcqMXw&v=2&isig=3G87aIjfIOZPqmH5TN9e93LV7nh&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/email/scx/cch5gkaj/GPS-5236645-0/f5d63586-53d0-43db-be4c-ad99a8383d05> 

Overweight	 
RAPT4.SA, RAPT4 BZ	 
Price (19 Mar 26):R$4.83	 
	

Randoncorp: It was another challenging month for the company (link here <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12832164&ad=4071257639&h=O5r4R3Q&sig=3vox4ycPgwim1HMs8dAxwQUl2cr&v=2&url=https://www.rad.cvm.gov.br/ENET/frmExibirArquivoIPEExterno.aspx?NumeroProtocoloEntrega%3D1492893> ) with top line -9% yoy vs -7% yoy in January, still impacted by soft figures from Frasle Mobility due the ramp-up of its new ERP and Autostore implementation. However, on a working day basis, revenues were at -4% yoy for Randoncorp and -1% yoy for Frasle Mobility. This compares with ANFAVEA truck production at -17% yoy and ANFIR truck-trailer sales -19% yoy during February. Excluding Frasse, Randomcorp revenues in February were at -11% yoy or -6% yoy when adjusted by working days. 

Frasle: February revenues (link here <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12832164&ad=4071257639&h=HmXlHar&sig=O47sPiDFSUMLBPmSVxpQdHFMIQa&v=2&url=https://www.rad.cvm.gov.br/ENET/frmExibirArquivoIPEExterno.aspx?NumeroProtocoloEntrega%3D1492892> ) were R$409mn (~41% of RAPT’s total), -6% yoy. Adjusted by working days, Frasle revenues were R$21.5mn, -1% yoy (vs -14% yoy in Jan). Keep in mind that Frasle Mobility results still impacted by the ramp-up of its new ERP in Nakata as well as Autostore implementation. 

Our view: Given stocks' weak performance recently with RAPT4 -26% and FRAS -12% in the last 30 days vs IBOV at -5%, we expect a marginal negative reaction to the figures as investors already have bearish expectations on the names. 

Valuation: Randoncorp is trading at 4.6x EV/EBITDA 2026e vs Frasle at 6.3x and the remainder of Latam Auto Parts universe at 3.4-5.4x.

Maria Resende

maria.resende@btgpactual.com <mailto:maria.resende@btgpactual.com>  	
Notícias 	
EDUCATION 	
4 em cada 10 alunos de graduação EAD desistem de estudar; número é o maior desde 2014 	
Folha de São Paulo | 19/03/2026 	
A taxa de evasão nos cursos da rede privada a distância atingiu em 2024 o maior patamar da série histórica. Naquele ano, 41,9% dos alunos abandonaram os estudos. É o maior percentual desde 2014.

Os dados foram divulgados na manhã desta quinta-feira (19) e são do Mapa do Ensino Superior no Brasil, estudo elaborado pelo Semesp (Sindicato das Entidades Mantenedoras de Estabelecimentos de Ensino Superior) com dados do Censo do Ensino Superior do MEC (Ministério da Educação).

⬆️ Leia a reportagem completa aqui <https://www1.folha.uol.com.br/educacao/2026/03/4-em-cada-10-alunos-de-graduacao-ead-desistem-de-estudar-numero-e-o-maior-desde-2014.shtml> .
	
A escola técnica de padrão internacional (e de R$ 260 milhões) da Ternium 	
Brazil Journal | 19/03/2026 	
A Ternium – uma das maiores produtoras de aço da América Latina – acaba de inaugurar uma escola técnica de padrão internacional na Zona Oeste do Rio de Janeiro.

Localizada no bairro de Santa Cruz, a Escola Técnica Roberto Rocca recebeu um investimento privado de R$ 260 milhões.

⬆️ Leia a reportagem completa aqui <https://braziljournal.com/brands/a-escola-tecnica-de-padrao-internacional-e-de-r-260-milhoes-da-ternium/> .
	
Wizard amarra M&A que cria rede de ensino socioemocional 	
Pipeline Valor | 20/03/2026 	
A rede de ensino Mr. Wiz, criada pelo empresário Carlos Wizard, está se unindo à Empoderamento Adolescente — numa fusão de cursos e mentorias presenciais e online para jovens, com currículo voltado principalmente a habilidades socioemocionais. 

“A maior dificuldade dos jovens e dos pais hoje é o desenvolvimento de soft skills, que serão justamente as habilidades mais valorizados no futuro”, diz Wizard, citando uma pesquisa do Fórum Econômico Mundial. As operações somam R$ 10 milhões em faturamento anual e a meta é atingir R$ 50 milhões até 2030. 

⬆️ Leia a reportagem completa aqui <https://pipelinevalor.globo.com/negocios/noticia/wizard-amarra-manda-que-cria-rede-de-ensino-socioemocional.ghtml> .
	

DISCLAIMERS & DISCLOSURES LEGAIS:

Hapvida avalia fechar unidades ociosas após resultados fracos no último trimestre 	
Valor Econômico | 19/03/2026 	
Após um resultado fraco no quarto trimestre, a Hapvida estuda fechar unidades ociosas ou passar a ofertar uma rede credenciada. A operadora de saúde fundada no Ceará — que conta com a maior estrutura verticalizada do setor — não divulgou quais seriam essas regiões, mas vem enfrentando dificuldades para atender nas praças do Sul e Sudeste.

Dos 145 mil usuários perdidos no quarto trimestre, 120 mil eram de São Paulo. Também não haverá aberturas relevantes em praças do Norte, Nordeste e Centro-Oeste – isso inclui hospitais, ambulatórios e clínicas.

⬆️ Leia a reportagem completa aqui <https://valor.globo.com/empresas/noticia/2026/03/19/hapvida-avalia-fechar-unidades-ociosas-apos-resultados-fracos-no-ultimo-trimestre.ghtml> .
	
Anvisa já recebeu 17 pedidos para produzir remédios com a substância do Ozempic; entenda 	
O Globo | 20/03/2026 	
Acaba nesta sexta-feira (20) no Brasil a patente da semaglutida, uma das drogas dentro das canetas emagrecedoras, e começa um novo tempo de esperança concreta, para milhões de pessoas, de que os preços diminuam. E, com isso, que mais gente tenha acesso a remédios que promovem uma revolução no controle da obesidade. 

Este ano, espera-se uma diminuição do preço entre 30% e 40%. Mas as novas drogas não serão genéricos e não necessariamente virão na forma de caneta. Tampouco há data para que as primeiras sejam lançadas. Mas ninguém duvida que uma nova revolução, esta na ampliação do acesso, está por vir no país. 

⬆️ Leia a reportagem completa aqui <https://oglobo.globo.com/saude/noticia/2026/03/20/anvisa-ja-recebeu-17-pedidos-para-produzir-remedios-com-a-substancia-do-ozempic-entenda.ghtml> .
	
‘A EMS se preparou para esse momento por anos', diz vice-presidente sobre nova caneta emagrecedora 	
Exame | 19/03/2026 	
Com o fim da patente da semaglutida no Brasil, previsto para esta sexta-feira, 20 de março, a EMS se posiciona para entrar em uma nova etapa de sua estratégia: ampliar o acesso a terapias modernas com produção nacional e alta complexidade tecnológica.

A farmacêutica, segundo Marcus Sanchez, vice-presidente da EMS, afirma que vem se preparando há mais de uma década para esse momento, com investimentos em ciência, inovação e infraestrutura.

⬆️ Leia a reportagem completa aqui <https://exame.com/negocios/a-ems-se-preparou-para-esse-momento-por-anos-diz-vice-presidente-sobre-nova-caneta-emagrecedora/> .
	
EMS já tem estrutura pronta para produzir ‘Ozempic’ própria 	
Valor Econômico | 20/03/2026 	
A EMS já tem estrutura pronta para começar a produzir sua própria versão de caneta emagrecedora à base da semaglutida, princípio ativo da Ozempic e da Wegovy, da farmacêutica dinamarquesa Nova Nordisk, que expira nesta sexta-feira (20). A farmacêutica do conglomerado de Carlos Sanchez já solicitou o registro do medicamento, mas ainda aguarda o processo de avaliação regulatória.

“Ainda não temos a definição de preço, m

⬆️ Leia a reportagem completa aqui <https://www.btgpactual.com/research/report-view/118847> . 

 

 

A ISPs, governo sinaliza liberar Acessa Crédito em abril 

Telesintese | 19/03/2026 

O Ministério das Comunicações sinalizou a associações estaduais de provedores regionais que pretende publicar, em abril, um novo edital do programa Acessa Crédito, voltado apenas a pequenos provedores de internet. A informação foi divulgada pelas próprias entidades após reunião realizada em Brasília com o ministro das Comunicações, Frederico Siqueira Filho, e o secretário de Telecomunicações, Hermano Tercius.

Segundo o relato das associações, o encontro integrou uma agenda conjunta de representantes da ASPRO (DF), ISP-RIO (RJ), APROINTER (PE), ASPEAL (AL), SINDIPROSE (SE) e APPIT (PA) no Ministério das Comunicações, na Anatel e no Congresso Nacional. As entidades levaram à capital federal uma pauta centrada nas dificuldades enfrentadas pelos pequenos provedores para acessar recursos públicos, usar a infraestrutura de postes, ampliar a competição e lidar com mudanças tributárias.

 

⬆️ Leia a reportagem completa aqui <https://telesintese.com.br/a-isps-governo-sinaliza-liberar-acessa-credito-em-abril/> . 

 

 

Governo negocia concessão para Redata 

Valor Economico | 20/03/2026 

Para o presidente da Abes, é preciso celeridade na votação do projeto porque as multinacionais estão mobilizadas para convencer suas matrizes a trazerem investimentos ao país.

Para viabilizar o Regime Especial de Tributação para Serviços de Data center (Redata), o governo federal negocia com o Congresso a inclusão de dispositivo em projeto de lei complementar que permita a concessão, em 2026, de medidas com renúncia de receita, desde que o impacto já esteja previsto no Orçamento do próprio ano. A proposta está em fase de negociação e ainda não foi formalmente encaminhada, já que depende de negociações com o presidente do Senado, Davi Alcolumbre (União-AP).

 

⬆️ Leia a reportagem completa aqui <https://valor.globo.com/empresas/noticia/2026/03/20/governo-negocia-concessao-para-redata.ghtml> .

emails.subject: ENC: JPM/Cemig: 4Q25a: Beat in power generation
emails.one_liner: Cemig (CMIG4) 4T25 resultados acima do consenso, impulsionado por geração, neutralizado por custos e volumes.
emails.body:
________________________________

De: Arthur Pereira, CFA <arthur.pereira@jpmresearchmail.com>
Enviado: sexta-feira, 20 de março de 2026 08:58:19 (UTC-03:00) Brasilia
Para: Sell Side <sellside@torkcapital.com.br>
Assunto: JPM/Cemig: 4Q25a: Beat in power generation


 	Latin America Equity Research 	

Cemig


4Q25a: Beat in power generation

 	 
By Arthur Pereira, CFA AC <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12831846&ad=4071004432&h=JyKnmfm&sig=QnqvyOzBriHb933HtKAk3jys6rg&v=2&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/analyst/F760714> , Victor Burke <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12831846&ad=4071004432&h=aDMvB_3&sig=5w8OXNfVq5exVTiYNdPTbhd6kPt&v=2&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/analyst/D634196>  

Click here for the full document and disclaimers <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12831846&ad=4071004432&h=mZ9lWZr&sig=YPSGzDjOVpCgm15YSlKa086WIoi&v=2&isig=RDQNUALLy7jWcx2ylBjSGLKv3Hp&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/email/scx/h159k7r6/GPS-5229686-0/42f901aa-a60d-42cb-a889-9e31365d73fa> 

Underweight	 
CMIG4.SA, CMIG4 BZ	 
Price (19 Mar 26):R$12.19	 
	

Cemig’s 4Q25 results came above JPMe and consensus, with adj. EBITDA at R$1.7bn (-4% yoy, but +10% vs JPMe and +5% consensus). The beat was driven by better-than-expected margins in generation vs. our expectations of higher short positions in energy trading. As negatives: 1) Distribution weak volumes (-1.4% yoy), 2) higher manageable expenses (+5% yoy), and 3) Gas: Volumes fell 12% y/y on lower industrial demand.  We see results as neutral, as worse operational trends (costs & volumes) were offset by energy trading.


Adj. EBITDA of R$1.75bn (+10% vs JPMe and +5% consensus) 


Reported EBITDA of R$2.9bn was impacted by 1) +R$1.2bn reduction in pension fund liabilities related to health insurance negotiations (previously announced), 2)+R$147mn in capital gains on asset sales, 3) -R$142mn in extraordinary provisions on litigation, 4) +R$16mn on non-cash DisCo revenues, and 5) -R$104mn on transmission IFRS accounting. Excluding non-cash and one-offs, adj. EBITDA reached R$1.75bn, above JPMe (+10%) and consensus (+5%) due to better than expected margins in generation, partially offset by lower volumes in Power and Gas distribution. Leverage 2.3x, net debt up R$3.7bn q/q.  Dividends: R$676mn, 1.76% div yield.

Conference call details: March 20th, Friday, 10am ET, 11am BRT. link here <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12831846&ad=4071004432&h=jz8ogvI&sig=UZU9ROykxgDMqH0GOmit6g3VuBk&v=2&url=https://tenmeetings.com.br/ten-events/%23/webinar?evento%3DConferenciadeResultados1T25-CEMIG_632> .

 	Latin America Equity Research

By Rodolfo Angele, CFA AC <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12831693&ad=4070895773&h=60UqH5T&sig=15CAy12cq6BtBmkNHaedEzr0rVG&v=2&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/analyst/U950947> , Tathiane Martins Candini <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12831693&ad=4070895773&h=pmLoqr_&sig=Uv5oCRJ7W7Fry3zpsdozI7427Pm&v=2&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/analyst/E778747> , Detlef Winckelmann AC <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12831693&ad=4070895773&h=yb03JFR&sig=2oZssmoKjxsgKLrVKnLgKP0UFXp&v=2&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/analyst/D631629>  

Click here for the full document and disclaimers <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12831693&ad=4070895773&h=nAVni5Y&sig=TkZ5FAt4zMpdDfo5l2HxXpQ8Gjm&v=2&isig=QDVzdMYMf7mOK5Anm7dFd7QEPS0&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/email/scx/-cf2vfj0/GPS-5236695-0/97a20e32-4f3d-4e17-807c-b857eb42785e> 

No change in Chinese PIX amid Shanghai Pulp Wek; Resale takes a hit. In the resale market, except for BHK – unchanged once again at RMB 4,580/t <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12831693&ad=4070895773&h=Y6o2MaL&sig=HtROKqVkokzP9VpYP6MxsN9A5kC&v=2&url=https://sp009.jpmchase.net/sites/spvfqlwh/Americas%2520Resources%2520Wiki/Americas%2520Resources.aspx>  (~$566/t) – all prices contracted. LatAm BHKE came in down by RMB 76/t (~$9.3/t) to RMB 4,502/t (~$556/t), representing contractions of 1.7% w/w and 1.3% m/m. For softwood, NBSK decreased by RMB 171/t (~$21.3/t) to RMB 5,227/t (~$650/t), now down by 3.2% w/w and 3.5% m/m, while Russian BSK decreased by RMB 173/t (~$21.7/t) to RMB 4,977/t (~$617/t), showing decreases of 3.4% w/w and 3.5% m/m. In the PIX market, while Chinese prices were unchanged, European indices slightly increased. In China, BHK remains at $595.4/t and with a 1.8% m/m increase, while NBSK is still down 1.5% m/m at $680/t. In Europe, NBSK increased by $30.3/t to $1,633.3/t (up 1.9% w/w and 3.9% m/m), while BHK increased by $3.5/t to $1,280/t, up by 0.3% w/w and 2.4% m/m.

Weekly News Section: 


Shanghai Pulp Week sees stalemate in contract talks as suppliers resist price cuts amid cost pressures and weak demand. According to Fastmarkets, during Shanghai Pulp Week, contract talks stalled as suppliers resisted price cuts due to rising costs, while buyers pushed back amid weak demand and flat paper prices. Pulp futures and resale prices fell, leading end users to favor cheaper resale BSK over imports, and port inventories declined as mills restocked. Suppliers warned BHK prices could rise further if Middle East tensions persist, but buyers threatened to cut volumes if increases continue, signaling ongoing tough negotiations. 

Domtar slates $95/tonne fluff price increase in North America and Europe, $45/tonne hike in net markets, effective April 1. According to RISI, Domtar will raise fluff pulp prices by $95 per tonne in North America and Europe (to $2,040/t) and by $45 per tonne in net markets like China and MENA, effective April 1. This marks Domtar’s second consecutive monthly increase and the first April price hike in any pulp grade globally, aligning with earlier increases by competitors. Domtar’s annual m

emails.subject: ENC: LatAm Financials Clipping | 20 de Março de 2026 (BTG Pactual's Rosman/Paura/Buchpiguel)
emails.one_liner: LatAm Financials clipping discute mudanças de CEO em Santander/B3, cortes de capital do Fed e volatilidade de juros globais.
emails.body:
________________________________

De: BTG Pactual Equities Research <sh-btgpactual-research@btgpactual.com>Em Nome deEduardo Rosman <eduardo.rosman@btgpactual.com>
Enviado: sexta-feira, 20 de março de 2026 08:47:59 (UTC-03:00) Brasilia
Assunto: LatAm Financials Clipping | 20 de Março de 2026 (BTG Pactual's Rosman/Paura/Buchpiguel)


BTG Pactual | Equity Research

	
LatAm Financials Clipping
	
20-Mar-2026 	
Manchetes 	



FINANCIALS 	
BANCOS	 
🔗 Mario Leão fica no Santander até julho; substituto de Finkelsztain na B3 não foi definido (Exame) 	
🔗 Gilson Finkelsztain será o novo CEO do Santander (NeoFeed) 	
	
	
SERVIÇOS FINANCEIROS/EXCHANGES/SEGUROS	 
🔗 Ibovespa consegue virada no final e fecha no positivo, com guerra e ambiente incerto (InfoMoney) 	
🔗 Fed revela planos para cortar em 4,8% exigência de capital de grandes bancos (Valor Econômico) 	
🔗 B3 confirma saída de Gilson Finkelsztain: CEO permanece no cargo até julho (Exame) 	
	
	
MACRO/MUNDO	 
🔗 Consignado CLT empresta R$ 117 bi em um ano, mas juros ainda são desafio (Folha de São Paulo) 	
🔗 Juros globais disparam com BCs conservadores (Valor Econômico) 	
🔗 Volatilidade no juro futuro triplica com guerra no Irã (Valor Econômico) 	
🔗 Após sessão volátil, juros futuros fecham perto da estabilidade (Valor Econômico) 	
🔗 Vorcaro assina confidencialidade e dá primeiro passo em acordo de delação premiada (Valor Econômico) 	
	
	


Eduardo Rosman 
Tel. +55 11 3383 2772 
eduardo.rosman@btgpactual.com <mailto:eduardo.rosman@btgpactual.com>  

Thiago Paura 
Tel. +55 11 3383 9873 
thiago.paura@btgpactual.com <mailto:thiago.paura@btgpactual.com>  

Ricardo Buchpiguel 
Tel. +55 11 3383 9128 
ricardo.buchpiguel@btgpactual.com <mailto:ricardo.buchpiguel@btgpactual.com>  	
Notícias 	
FINANCIALS 	
Mario Leão fica no Santander até julho; substituto de Finkelsztain na B3 não foi definido 	
Exame | 19/03/2026 	
O Santander Brasil anunciou nesta quinta-feira, 19, a saída de seu diretor-presidente, Mario Leão, prevista para ocorrer até julho de 2026. O executivo está no banco há 11 anos e ocupa o cargo de CEO desde 2021.

Para a sucessão, o conselho escolheu Gilson Finkelsztain, atual presidente da B3, que ainda depende de aprovações regulatórias para assumir o posto. Segundo o banco, a decisão segue o plano de sucessão da alta administração, com recomendação do comitê de nomeação e governança.

⬆️ Leia a reportagem completa aqui <https://exame.com/invest/mercados/mario-leao-fica-no-santander-ate-julho-substituto-de-finkelsztain-na-b3-nao-foi-definido/> .
	
Gilson Finkelsztain será o novo CEO do Santander 	
NeoFeed | 20/03/2026 	
Uma dança nas cadeiras envolvendo dois grandes players do mercado financeiro – e, por consequência, dois executivos de peso desse setor no País - veio à tona na noite desta quinta-feira, 19 de março.

Um dos principais bancos privados no mercado brasileiro, o Santander anunciou que Mario Leão, presidente da operação no Brasil, está deixando o cargo. E que o escolhido para ser o seu substituto será Gilson Finkelsztain, que, atualmente, ocupa o posto de presidente da B3.

⬆️ Leia a reportagem completa aqui <https://neofeed.com.br/negocios/gilson-finkelsztain-sera-o-novo-ceo-do-santander/> .
	
Ibovespa consegue virada no final e fecha no positivo, com guerra e ambiente incerto 	
InfoMoney | 19/03/2026 	
Índices nos EUA caem em meio a notícias da guerra, mas conseguem melhora no final

O mercado parece um campo minado. Basta um passo e uma bomba pode estourar. Daí, o medo passa a se alastrar como um rastro de pólvora. O Ibovespa vinha caindo forte hoje e, então, a virada! Fechou com alta de 0,35%, aos 180.270,62 pontos, uma alta de 630,71 pontos. Um feito e tanto, afinal, o dia estava uma tragédia: na mínima, bateu em 176.295,71 pontos.

⬆️ Leia a reportagem completa aqui <https://www.infomoney.com.br/mercados/ibovespa-hoje-bolsa-de-valores-ao-vivo-19032026/> .
	
Fed revela planos para cortar em 4,8% exigência de capital de grandes bancos 	
Valor Econômico | 20/03/2026 	
As exigências de reserva de capital para os maiores bancos dos Estados Unidos serão reduzidas em 4,8%, segundo propostas de reguladores, em uma das medidas mais agressivas para relaxar as restrições impostas às instituições de Wall Street depois da crise financeira de 2008.

⬆️ Leia a reportagem completa aqui <https://valor.globo.com/financas/noticia/2026/03/20/fed-revela-planos-para-cortar-em-48-exigencia-de-capital-de-grandes-bancos.ghtml> .
	
B3 confirma saída de Gilson Finkelsztain: CEO permanece no cargo até julho 	
Exame | 19/03/2026 	
A B3 informou, em fato relevante, que o CEO Gilson Finkelsztain deixará o cargo ao fim do primeiro semestre de 2026, encerrando um ciclo iniciado em 2017.

A saída foi definida em comum acordo com o conselho de administração e faz parte de um processo estruturado de sucessão, já em andamento e alinhado às práticas de governança da companhia.

Aneel rejeita pedido de impugnação contra edital do 2º LRCap 

MegaWhat | 19/03/2026 

A Agência Nacional de Energia Elétrica (Aneel) rejeitou o pedido de contestação apresentado pelo Instituto Nacional de Energia Limpa (Inel) contra o edital do leilão de reserva de capacidade na forma de potência de 2026 (LRCap 2026), que acontece nesta sexta-feira, 20 de março, voltado à contratação de usinas termelétricas a óleo e biodiesel.

No processo, o Inel solicitou memórias de cálculo completas e justificativas para a revisão dos preços do certame. O pedido de impugnação também contestou a falta de priorização de fontes mais sustentáveis, além da transparência na divulgação da demanda a ser contratada, apontada como uma medida relevante para mitigar riscos concorrenciais.

⬆️ Leia a reportagem completa aqui <https://megawhat.uol.com.br/leiloes/aneel-rejeita-pedido-de-impugnacao-contra-edital-do-2o-lrcap/> .

AXIA troca ativos com ISA Energia, recebe R$ 1,17 bi e converterá ações 

Brazil Journal | 19/03/2026 

A AXIA Energia chegou a um acordo com a ISA Energia para trocar participações societárias em ativos de transmissão, numa transação na qual a antiga Eletrobras receberá R$ 1,17 bilhão em dinheiro.

Em paralelo, a AXIA conseguiu aprovação para converter 19,7 milhões de ações ordinárias que detém no capital da ISA Energia em ações preferenciais.

⬆️ Leia a reportagem completa aqui <https://braziljournal.com/axia-troca-ativos-com-isa-energia-recebe-r-117-bi-e-convertera-acoes/> .

Justiça suspende processo de caducidade da Enel em São Paulo 

EPBR | 19/03/2026 

A Justiça Federal concedeu uma liminar à Enel Distribuição São Paulo para suspender o andamento do processo administrativo na Agência Nacional de Energia Elétrica (Aneel), que discute a caducidade da concessão da distribuidora.

A sessão deliberativa estava marcada para a próxima terça-feira (24/3). Segundo a juíza federal, Pollyanna Kelly Maciel Medeiros, o prazo curto evidencia o risco concreto e atual de consolidação de uma penalidade extrema como a caducidade.

 	Latin America Equity Research 	

Hi Tork,

Today is the LAST DAY of the Extel/II survey. We kindly ask for your recognition with a 5-STAR vote in LATIN AMERICA REAL ESTATE, CEMENT & CONSTRUCTION and CAPITAL GOODS.

Best,
Marcelo Motta & Jonathan Koutras



Latam Real Estate - Today's News


CYRE CFO Interview, MELK Into MCMV, TRXF11 Hospital Deal, ALOS Receives Triple A Rating

 	 
 <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12831559&ad=4070836581&h=bZBR4X0&sig=JK28byEgapmFM60aO4B8RPyHDKP&v=2&url=https://www.extelinsights.com/voting> 	
By Marcelo Motta AC <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12831559&ad=4070836581&h=E0Mm9Ge&sig=X2WnqIrZz6Z29qezeOGSxkTKGZ4&v=2&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/analyst/E194704> , Jonathan S. Koutras <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12831559&ad=4070836581&h=9vDGKvq&sig=GRJyu1FERRU2Dy8d9R9Olx6B6kd&v=2&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/analyst/V737771> , Adrian E Huerta <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12831559&ad=4070836581&h=EUeO2BD&sig=8i4xWiGHx37HCRqBN0N0XiAVsRO&v=2&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/analyst/U034572> , Felipe Barragan Sanchez <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12831559&ad=4070836581&h=IVq0XYT&sig=MKiX4QXdZZ3JIenQPUzIGRvXTRz&v=2&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/analyst/V846240> , Garrett Greenblatt <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12831559&ad=4070836581&h=1uhSRjQ&sig=5wnKP4hcU674lcw20TUIBEQezSF&v=2&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/analyst/V849374>  

Click here for the full document and disclaimers <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12831559&ad=4070836581&h=D803YIT&sig=Bqfuc3w75w6DRBadMRetcp9Y5Ay&v=2&isig=QMFQQoW9Us7YR7Q68I10auwyZBP&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/email/scx/50rknqr2/GPS-5240843-0/9800a03c-7d4c-4063-92e9-07bbb33142bd> 

Homebuilders & Brokers 

*	Cyrela CFO flags potential corporate tower sale in interview - Metro Quadrado <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12831559&ad=4070836581&h=-yQUw7w&sig=2mplAERKKsDp1Gk5ZyGAgwbzKqT&v=2&url=https://metroquadrado.com/comercial/venda-de-predio-locado-para-o-nubank-esta-no-radar-da-cyrela/> . Cyrela’s (OW-rated) CFO Miguel Mickelberg said the 23‑story building (17 floors leased to Nubank, 6 for Cyrela’s new HQ) is unlikely to remain on the balance sheet for long because stabilized office yields tend to sit below the company’s ROE, though high rates mean “no rush” to sell. One option is to sell only the 17 Nubank floors and retain the 6 Cyrela-occupied floors; the tower is due for completion in 2H26. Regarding 2026, management sees “lower chances” of materially accelerating launches in 2026 and sees sales speed at a still “healthy” level with some normalization ahead from an unusually high level in 2025. 
*	Melnick pivots to MCMV in Porto Alegre to reignite growth - Metro Quadrado <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12831559&ad=4070836581&h=-qm2Ljt&sig=2POiBUKYM5vO8f4V4gLkF8fbP2g&v=2&url=https://metroquadrado.com/residencial/melnick-muda-rota-para-pegar-carona-no-avanco-do-mcmv-em-porto-alegre/> . Melnick (MELK, NC) will redirect land bank and focus to the MCMV program, with its Open vertical targeting R$350mn in launches in 2026 (+80% yoy), primarily in Faixa 3 and Faixa 4, after floods and weaker demographics/interest rates eroded mid/high-end demand. The company is renegotiating land deals, with up to 80% discounts in swaps, arguing higher unit volumes at MCMV will offset price cuts. Company’s landbank is at R$4.4bn, with ~21% (~R$1.5bn) earmarked for Open. The pivot seeks to address pent-up affordable-housing demand supported by state “Porta de Entrada” subsidies and pending federal adjustments to income/price caps, while tackling analyst concerns about Melnick’s growth. 
*	Tecnisa withdraws launch and margin guidance for Jardim das Perdizes – Press Release <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12831559&ad=4070836581&h=IAqhO5p&sig=N7U4DAR38kr1khSm7mZ0Qyu1TGm&v=2&url=https://www.rad.cvm.gov.br/ENET/frmExibirArquivoIPEExterno.aspx?NumeroProtocoloEntrega%3D1492701> . Tecnisa (TCSA3, NC) announced the withdrawal of its launch and gross margin guidance for the Jardim das Perdizes project, citing significant changes in the macroeconomic environment and a restructuring of operational strategy for the related projects.

NeoFeed | 20/03/2026 

Ao divulgar seus resultados do quarto trimestre e do ano de 2025 na noite desta quinta-feira, 19 de março, mais do que os números do período, a Panvel encerrou um ciclo iniciado em 2020, quando captou R$ 480 milhões em um re-IPO.

No último capítulo dessa jornada, a rede de farmácias entregou um lucro líquido ajustado trimestral recorde de R$ 45,2 milhões, o que representou um salto de 35% sobre igual período, um ano antes. No ano, o indicador ficou em R$ 135,3 milhões, alta anual de 15,3%.

Apesar de, na prática, ser uma corporation, a RD Saúde se encaixa nesse perfil de empresa por ainda ter as famílias fundadoras da Raia e da Drogasil como acionistas e com representantes no conselho e na gestão.

"A estratégia de entrada e expansão da 99Food tem sido extremamente agressiva e baseada em iniciativas e condutas muitas vezes questionáveis", diz ofício assinado pelo escritório Mattos Filho e encaminhado no último dia 6 de março à Coordenação de Análise Antitruste do Cade.

________________________________

De: BTG Pactual Equities Research <sh-btgpactual-research@btgpactual.com>Em Nome deThiago Duarte <thiago.duarte@btgpactual.com>

Food & Beverages Clipping
	
March 20, 2026 	
Manchetes 	
FOOD & BEVERAGES 	
FOOD 	
🔗 Margens de carne bovina vão cair em 2026, avisa a Minerva; Ação desaba (The Agri Biz) 	
🔗 MBRF espera margens de lucro da carne bovina sustentadas ou maiores na América do Sul (Globo Rural)

Thiago Duarte

Guilherme Guttilla 
Tel. +55 11 3383 9684 
guilherme.guttilla@btgpactual.com <mailto:guilherme.guttilla@btgpactual.com>  
	
 
 
Notícias 	
FOOD & BEVERAGES 	
 
 
Margens de carne bovina vão cair em 2026, avisa a Minerva; Ação desaba 
The Agri Biz | 19/03/2026 

A virada do ciclo pecuário e a guerra no Oriente Médio devem resultar em margens menores para a Minerva em 2026, ainda que o cenário seja favorável para o repasse do aumento de custos aos preços da carne bovina. A perspectiva foi traçada pelos principais executivos da companhia nesta quinta-feira.

“Esperamos, sim, uma pressão de custos ao longo deste ano”, disse o diretor financeiro da Minerva, Edison Ticle, durante teleconferência com analistas.

 
⬆️ Leia a reportagem completa aqui <http://url6009.btgpactual.com/ls/click?upn=u001.8kHVfpmJVwuWd-2BGZAuova7NU13b7vAs4A-2FQ6yw-2FBr6dkXSThayxKQT-2FkpxFDUVQIpzYIeDTCmxpVLFpBuWgehc4VRZs-2FbvPTsF-2FPV2K8rpPnxAjtAKhdDhcqpbfuVhmP0lcBS6fKy22Cu5maVZce8eN4OjCixgl7DSlKwPABx5s-3DXlst_J38k6-2F6nugWWzJj-2BIm6l6u8ZIjWmIGqrHBplWOlFGs6tXc9-2FdYKwsQ6hSaONbOcnZ4YQ05Rx6GCsdBcekGEZ4Jw03TZOQ6FzL9n-2FGRDGzbrrXHUE5l9LDmS-2F9qT5N2SEUIyu9qmr6arD8h3xtcfP-2FLz-2FtEz3KiZFcXA5-2BEuDEFg9ODZKChlgCPEuQgHU3S45MVmQ2laylDduWM4c4R8wW-2BDuMsXPm8v1PF8BKhlP2ms-3D> . 
 
 
MBRF espera margens de lucro da carne bovina sustentadas ou maiores na América do Sul 
Globo Rural | 19/03/2026 

Apesar da cota de 1,1 milhão de toneladas de carne bovina com tarifação reduzida imposta recentemente pela China ao Brasil - menor do que o volume exportado pelo país à nação asiática no ano passado - a MBRF mantém “boas expectativas” de conseguir margens de lucro em 2026 na operação da América do Sul sustentadas ou até maiores do que em 2025, disse nesta quinta-feira (19/3) o diretor-presidente global da companhia, Miguel Gularte, durante teleconferência sobre os resultados do quarto trimestre de 2025.

Em 2025, a margem Ebitda (lucro antes de juros, impostos, depreciação e amortização) ajustada da MBRF foi de 10,1%, segundo o comunicado de resultados divulgado pela empresa na quarta-feira (18/03) à noite.

 
⬆️ Leia a reportagem completa aqui <http://url6009.btgpactual.com/ls/click?upn=u001.8kHVfpmJVwuWd-2BGZAuova6sBK2AW0VGp-2FS3NdVPSD7DGzkeTXiuOq0Nw3pRv4UmO6ZGWr1-2BZsqoBFs9HSsZ1VqRg9W6XH1TdetHALDUGKkDn9TaLpus4Z0v6kXXAO4IhVSih2dBUy4x-2BMSQV7UxsF0hLvY4cTNoT-2B7sEJwQUo51efWcTKCEML2hg6qnlMLOmtaIWqq2WQu7OK5glWABCkA-3D-3DgGyi_J38k6-2F6nugWWzJj-2BIm6l6u8ZIjWmIGqrHBplWOlFGs6tXc9-2FdYKwsQ6hSaONbOcnZ4YQ05Rx6GCsdBcekGEZ4F5c7On8J5K-2BhJw4qtjeQi-2BU1B8F50iQNuMIPNC7YXDOS20NRu83aRNul19ZLZAk9rjEjn5DKJieI2ZCuoKzAOG1W4WcwDTaOOgjdfaJvK4LqnfiQeo7bj2MDmRLuC1PXSmcPu3QHPnC-2BO0heqNbplU-3D> . 
 
 
MBRF diz que embarques para Oriente Médio seguem ocorrendo normalmente 
Globo Rural | 19/03/2026 

O diretor-presidente global da MBRF, Miguel Gularte, disse em teleconferência sobre os resultados do quarto trimestre e do ano de 2025, que não houve interrupção do fluxo de embarques de seus produtos para o Oriente Médio em função dos conflitos na região.

“Os embarques para essa região estão ativos e funcionando perfeitamente”, afirmou Gularte a analistas.

emails.subject: ENC: Cemig (N): Good 4Q figures (BTG Pactual's Junqueira/Gushiken/Schutz)
emails.one_liner: Cemig entregou bons resultados no 4T, EBITDA ajustado R$1.86bn (6% acima), lucro R$1.88bn e dividendos 9% yield para 2025.
emails.body:
________________________________

De: BTG Pactual Equities Research <sh-btgpactual-research@btgpactual.com>Em Nome deAntonio Junqueira <antonio.junqueira@btgpactual.com>
Enviado: sexta-feira, 20 de março de 2026 08:34:38 (UTC-03:00) Brasilia
Assunto: Cemig (N): Good 4Q figures (BTG Pactual's Junqueira/Gushiken/Schutz)



BTG Pactual | Equity Research 


Company Note - Brazil - Utilities 


Cemig (Neutral Rating; TP: R$ 13.20) 


20-March-2026 


 


Good 4Q figures 


Read the full document here >> <https://www.btgpactual.com/research/report-view/118839>  

R$1.86bn in adj. EBITDA, 6% above us

Cemig delivered good 4Q results. Adj. EBITDA (ex. equity income) came in at R$1.86bn, down 3.5% y/y, but 6% above our estimate (R$1.75bn), with stronger-than-expected results from disco and G&T + trading, partially offset by weaker Gasmig figures. After lower-than-expected net financial expenses of R$290mn (vs. BTG’s R$400mn), equity income of R$41mn (vs. BTG’s R$18mn) and a R$788mn positive bottom-line impact from provision reversals related to post-employment health and dental plan obligations (vs. BTG’s R$858mn), net income reached R$1.88bn (vs. BTG’s R$1.93bn), or R$1.02bn when adjusted for non-cash/non-recurring items. Along with 4Q results, Cemig announced R$676mn in dividends (1.7% dvd. yield), bringing total dividends for 2025 to R$3.51bn, or a 9% dvd. yield. Leverage increased to 2.3x ND/adj. EBITDA (vs. 1.76x in 3Q25).

Distribution: lower bad debt expenses and flat energy losses

The distribution arm delivered adj. EBITDA of R$958mn, above our R$849mn estimate and up 4% y/y, driven by the Parcel B readjustment and lower bad debt expenses (-R$29mn vs. 4Q24). Billed volumes were down 1.4% y/y (vs. BTG’s +1.5% y/y), with lower demand from industrial, public services and concessionaire clients, partially offset by higher rural consumption. Energy losses remained flat q/q at 11.42%, below the regulatory threshold of 11.46%. In 2025, Cemig D opex reached R$4.66bn, 0.7% below its regulatory opex, while EBITDA reached R$3.72bn, 2.4% above its regulatory EBITDA.

Reducing short position at G&T, weaker results from Gasmig

The G&T + trading arms reported adj. EBITDA of R$750mn, above our R$689mn estimate, but down 19% y/y due to lower GSF (0.67 vs. 0.8 in 4Q24) and the negative impact of higher energy prices on the trading arm’s short position. The updated energy balance shows that Cemig reduced its short energy position to 3MWavg in 2026 (vs. 127MWavg in Aug-25), 68MWavg in 2027 (vs. 128MWavg) and 233MWavg in 2028 (vs. 326MWavg). Gasmig delivered EBITDA of R$184mn, below our R$191mn estimate, due to lower distributed volumes (-12% y/y).

Thiago Duarte

Guilherme Guttilla

Notícias 	
AGRIBUSINESS 	
 
 
Impasse entre Cosan e Bradesco não deve afetar IPO de Compass, dizem fontes 
Valor Economico | 19/03/2026 

Um impasse entre a CosanCotação de Cosan e o Bradesco no âmbito da CosanCotação de Cosan Dez, veículo que detêm uma participação de 88% na Compass (de gás), não deve afetar a abertura de capital da empresa de gás e energia do conglomerado de Rubens Ometto, apurou o Valor.

 
⬆️ Leia a reportagem completa aqui <http://url6009.btgpactual.com/ls/click?upn=u001.8kHVfpmJVwuWd-2BGZAuovazP2sFH9AeZbfFWic9ai5tg4ncPcATWglrapwPoTZk-2Bq-2Bvb6L509zMc9Mf3FkV4iT-2BGyVX-2FMeFIxRPKthMzHv8OhZ-2F2NumAJHd9oUAFUmNG3WJJCv68dEkyDUkPmSZcSNbVpcfHyK97dLtMHWHhwEXpK571TCgunv56XlSLq1ciepbLc_J38k6-2F6nugWWzJj-2BIm6l6u8ZIjWmIGqrHBplWOlFGs5AaNtgIN6KbVEx78Zdf5jfak8iBq-2BFBYCwH15mjl86vxsmidAI8k85k3AEpElAqdd2kn8ME-2Bv3Pzyd9jKYAtu2KZSPa2-2BhIkjqzvQNdFZbx7T4BtMfmK6L8ejqZhtlbKc-2FwRjXUOmmU6KKMXclp3orJYJxYxQtcI5pK97eO-2Fvl-2BLM6FoR7GgPMwZXSzrPHilI-3D> . 
 
 
Crise da Raízen expõe limites do ciclo de investimentos em etanol no Brasil 
Nova Cana | 19/03/2026 

Há mais de uma década, grandes petrolíferas e tradings globais de grãos avançaram sobre o mercado brasileiro de etanol por meio de uma série de negócios. Agora, um dos acordos mais emblemáticos desse movimento começa a se desfazer, em meio ao arrefecimento do entusiasmo pelo setor.

A Raízen, joint venture entre Shell e Cosan e outrora a maior fornecedora de biocombustível do país, enfrenta uma crise de crédito que levou, na semana passada, a um acordo de reestruturação de dívida de cerca de R$ 65 bilhões (US$ 12,3 bilhões).

 
⬆️ Leia a reportagem completa aqui <http://url6009.btgpactual.com/ls/click?upn=u001.8kHVfpmJVwuWd-2BGZAuova98gPT6Qp6P-2Frr-2BRvi66S7Csdq88dzjLwq4ZeEiGMsWswze-2BekvamFYB2m7uWYJ9DtHi1KEoCL9Q5Qs5iBXgy8qvWOyUkTwBD1W2KDt9-2BHMH-2FJ26wx-2F1cKfqFIYZJdZHlQ-3D-3D8wCj_J38k6-2F6nugWWzJj-2BIm6l6u8ZIjWmIGqrHBplWOlFGs5AaNtgIN6KbVEx78Zdf5jfak8iBq-2BFBYCwH15mjl86v6wbTlke232j5ymgDo2JGUBw2R4Dw6jsV-2FUjve-2B8SvMsnctsQNPAtd-2BiJW6-2BQ2zkveEQdpEk1uk6gxHRuEzmKJ9uZ-2F4O4ZN5lBt2sFflEc9-2B635GoZQhqRvKiLC0NkHbGkwRTorjV5x3G3hnfXzhxsg-3D> . 
 
 
Analistas dizem que alta nos combustíveis permanecerá por muito tempo nos EUA 
Nova Cana | 19/03/2026 

O presidente dos Estados Unidos, Donald Trump, e os republicanos do Congresso estão apostando que o choque nos preços do petróleo provocado pela crise com o Irã terá vida curta demais para prejudicá-los politicamente em novembro, mas operadores e analistas veem sinais de que os preços nas bombas permanecerão dolorosamente altos por muito tempo após qualquer avanço diplomático.

Os preços do petróleo subiram à medida que o conflito com o Irã interrompeu o fornecimento global. O petróleo bruto dos EUA ultrapassou US$ 100 por barril pela primeira vez desde o choque entre a Rússia e a Ucrânia em 2022. O diesel dos EUA subiu acima de US$ 5 por galão, o valor mais alto desde o final de 2022.

 
⬆️ Leia a reportagem completa aqui <http://url6009.btgpactual.com/ls/click?upn=u001.8kHVfpmJVwuWd-2BGZAuova98gPT6Qp6P-2Frr-2BRvi66S7C9ewdcjj

OIL & GAS 

Oil up despite efforts by US, allies to boost supply, unchoke Strait of Hormuz 

Reuters | 19/03/2026 

LONDON, March 20 (Reuters) - Oil prices gained on Friday despite leading European nations, Japan and Canada ​offering to join efforts to secure safe passage for ships through the Strait of Hormuz and the ‌U.S. outlining moves to boost oil supply.

 

"The potential for a quick reversal in energy prices is unlikely because damage has been done to production," said Ole Hansen, the head of commodity strategy at Saxo Bank. "The fact on the ground remains that we have a tight market."

 

Brent ​futures rose $1.20, or 1.1%, to $109.85 a barrel at 0923 GMT, while U.S. West Texas Intermediate (WTI) crude added 6 ​cents, or 0.1%, to $96.20.

 

For the week, benchmark Brent was on track to rise nearly 7%, while ⁠WTI was set to fall about 2% in its first weekly decline in five weeks.

Israel and Iran traded fresh attacks ​on Friday, following a hit on an oil refinery in Kuwait.

 

In a joint statement <https://www.reuters.com/world/asia-pacific/joint-statement-strait-hormuz-by-european-nations-japan-2026-03-19/>  on Thursday, after earlier hesitating, Britain, France, ​Germany, Italy, the Netherlands and Japan expressed "our readiness to contribute to appropriate efforts to ensure safe passage through the Strait", through which 20% of the world's oil and LNG transit.

 

Looking to curb soaring oil prices, U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said the U.S. may soon remove sanctions <https://www.reuters.

emails.subject: ENC: SANTANDER LATAM DAILY: 20 Mar - Haddad steps down, Mexico rate decision preview (hold), Upgrade GAP/Downgrade Volaris, Gen-Z thematic report, WEG T&D opportunity
emails.one_liner: Haddad steps down, Mexico rates likely hold, GAP upgraded, Volaris downgraded, Gen-Z consumer trends, WEG T&D opportunity.
emails.body:
________________________________

De: Lethicia Silva da Conceicao (E) <lethicia.conceicao@santander.com.br>
Enviado: sexta-feira, 20 de março de 2026 08:21:53 (UTC-03:00) Brasilia
Cc: SAN - Local Sales <SAN-LocalSales@santandernet.onmicrosoft.com>
Assunto: SANTANDER LATAM DAILY: 20 Mar - Haddad steps down, Mexico rate decision preview (hold), Upgrade GAP/Downgrade Volaris, Gen-Z thematic report, WEG T&D opportunity





SALES COMMENTS

Friday, March 20, 2026

SANTANDER LATAM DAILY (sales commentary only)

 

Good morning and happy Friday!

 

 LAST DAY to VOTE in EXTEL! <https://pierpont.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12829453&ad=4069322460&h=bZBR4X0&sig=F5tH6IRxlLJ8eXGYdS1lwK7bjfM&v=2&url=https://www.extelinsights.com/voting>  This remains a tremendously important exercise for our team of analysts on the ground. So if we’ve been helpful this year, please don’t forget to vote here <https://pierpont.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12829453&ad=4069322460&h=bZBR4X0&sig=F5tH6IRxlLJ8eXGYdS1lwK7bjfM&v=2&url=https://www.extelinsights.com/voting>  before heading off for the weekend. Our team roster is attached. Thanks so much! 

 

Yesterday, Finance Minister Haddad announced that he has stepped down to run for Governor of Sao Paulo. There was already speculation that this would occur, so it likely does not come as a surprise to the market. Taking Haddad’s place is his 41-year-old deputy, Dario Durigan. Valor notes this morning that while Durigan’s name had already been put forward by Haddad, this was the first time Lula has publicly confirmed the choice himself.

 

With global interest rates and inflation at the forefront of investor attention in the current scenario, our economists in Mexico think the Central Bank will pause <https://pierpont.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12829453&ad=4069322460&h=cci8D1r&sig=NfgC5WfHaIkkbFh50shkt86PQfi&v=2&url=https://santandercibinsights.com/report/5000ac02-7f7d-42a1-9991-794ab5122bdf>  on rates when they meet next Thursday. Inflation remains too lofty and they are not seeing enough improvement within the core indicators to warrant a cut when combined with the geopolitical factors. For the time being, we stick to our view that the CB will cut in June and August.

 

Abraham Fuentes is changing recommendations in Mexico transportation, <https://pierpont.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12829453&ad=4069322460&h=q_GnXnl&sig=E7px84AYBryNk63EqgYZ5ZVSbCW&v=2&url=https://santandercibinsights.com/report/f959d161-87dc-42e1-a69c-480bdd219121> upgrading GAP and downgrading Volaris. For GAP, we upgrade to outperform we are seeing an opportunity on valuation (new TP M$472/sh + 4-5% DY) as we believe near-term headwinds in Guadalajara and Puerto Vallarta (due to cartel related issues in Feb) as well as a soft Mexico international segment are priced in, and that the market is missing a quicker than expected recovery in Jamaica following Hurricane Melissa. On Volaris, we still believe that the demand outlook should be constructive ahead, but the global oil volatility has pushed jet-fuel prices higher and with higher fuel-cost assumptions we now lack upside in the model (TP M$13/sh) and downgrade to Neutral.

 

Aline Cardoso & Andres Soto have conducted a thematic analysis on the impact from Gen Z’s reconfiguration of the consumer universe <https://pierpont.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12829453&ad=4069322460&h=pw250ss&sig=KZX0H505XVNfN4e8OGGlG0LGhiM&v=2&url=https://santandercibinsights.com/report/d196f4e4-950d-468a-9ee4-85b55949b94c> . The key findings are that Gen-Z views wellness as habitual, not aspirational, and there’s a firm preference for mobile-first ecosystems and a “bring it to me” mindset. However, Aline and Andres emphasize recent reporting that, counterintuitively, suggests that in LatAm physical retail isn’t dead, as visiting the mall with friends has become a retro social event. To play this thematic the obvious candidates are Mercado Libre and Yape (Credicorp). But Aline and Andres suggest looking also at names like Smart Fit and PetzCobasi that are tied to recurring, identity-driven tastes. And Multiplan, Iguatemi, Fibra Uno and Parque Arauco which are leveraged to the reconfiguration of physical retail to the social and experiential vibe. In Mexico, we see Alsea as the clearest beneficiary.

 

Lucas Barbosa’s done some serious number crunching on Brazil’s power consumption needs and the implications for grid renewal <https://pierpont.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12829453&ad=4069322460&h=mMO7PDR&sig=A0nonwSSOPWK7Vyyou881Fc2mmY&v=2&url=https://santandercibinsights.com/report/f57dfeed-97e1-48c6-918f-a1bf87ca531f> . The aim is to figure out what this means for WEG given their sizeable investments in their power transmission & distribution business which carries much higher margin potential. He’s come away convinced that WEG’s investment plan could generate net revenue of up to R$186bn through 2035.
