Eventos:

HOJE - 10am: Rural Ventures - Protein & Grains Outlook, webinar w/ Partner & Consultancy Head Registrar <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12831326&ad=4070717329&h=zG7UBgM&sig=Oz74cU38utu6sgGXUqIpOqxv074&v=2&url=https://jpmchase.zoom.us/webinar/register/WN_bYDz_4F1QFuZUEfELH-Nrg%23/registration>

HOJE - 11am: Close-Up International: GLP-1 in Latin America, webinar w/ Commercial Director  Registrar <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12831326&ad=4070717329&h=MLC8HMf&sig=Ivf8VAgG41Nd7iHTP6RgCVrMsEN&v=2&url=https://jpmchase.zoom.us/webinar/register/WN_X_T5G-X0S-OUiDKILYmH5A%23/registration>

HOJE - 2pm: Vale - Global Miners Week, webinar w/ Director of IR & Joint-Ventures Registrar <mailto:giovanna.lopes@jpmorgan.com?subject=JPM%20-%20Global%20MIners%20Week>   
HOJE - 3pm: AI Impact on Brokers/Assets Managers & Exchanges Competition, webinar w/ Yuri Fernandes & Ken Worthington Registrar <mailto:giovanna.lopes@jpmorgan.com?subject=JPM%20-%20Global%20MIners%20Week>    


Earnings 4Q25 

*	Beat: Cyrela 
*	Miss: Tupy, Wiz 

  

TRANSPORTATION:  revisamos o comportamento das acoes do setor em ciclos de corte de juros - concluimos que no periodo de 6-12 meses apos o 1o corte, o setor tende a outperformar a bolsa. Dentro da nossa cobertura preferimos exposição a nomes mais defensivos com fundamento solido - MOTV e RENT.  Note here <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12831326&ad=4070717329&h=43aUNMx&sig=4bw1kmHjN4d4DgF4a9NoVwTQ339&v=2&isig=EczunmZSjmlxLoXiVv2zgcj6qhc&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/email/scx/-qvbl3d8/GPS-5241352-0.pdf/d8d80eb2-2e9b-44e0-b6f7-de1a9c15d726> 

NU: analisamos unit economics no México, de produtos de 20-50% de ROE) e entender os tamanho das oportunidades. Por exemplo Nu pode rodar em 70% em cartoes, mas a indústria ainda é pequena ($ 2-4bn de profit pool) e o mercado precisaria crescer tipo Brasil 2019-2025. Note here <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12831326&ad=4070717329&h=xPuy2E1&sig=YJN8yVo1j26Vypix7KvCbRz9Pml&v=2&isig=GLMn6WFGu1XZuyyjDWJPvZDvyu8&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/email/scx/h20eqb56/GPS-5240638-0.pdf/d8d80eb2-2e9b-44e0-b6f7-de1a9c15d726> 

⁠LATAM FINANCIALS: Feedback do call com nosso analista de US Payments, IT & Processors. Crescimento de TPV desacelerando e penetração de cartões estabilizando levam o setor a migrar para soluções de maior valor agregado para sustentar crescimento. AI surge como fator duplo, reduzindo custo de desenvolvimento, mas melhora underwriting e eficiência, com players como Visa e Block melhor posicionados. Entrada do Nubank nos EUA ainda é incerta em termos de modelo de monetização e funding. No geral, cenário aponta para crescimento mais desafiador e maior foco em diferenciação via tecnologia. Note here <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12831326&ad=4070717329&h=Sucd8r1&sig=4dthr5XpI9lRTDrNn5JDVp1I7Bp&v=2&isig=3GX0AKBbjAga3UynsJHG8tjd4PH&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/email/scx/-730ju2k/GPS-5239544-0.pdf/d8d80eb2-2e9b-44e0-b6f7-de1a9c15d726> 

VTEX: atualizamos estimativas depois do 4Q, incorporando o guidance de menor crescimento, mas maior rentabilidade. Mantemos o Neutro com PT de $5, e preferimos esperar por mais visibilidade de uma potencial reaceleração do crescimento. Note here <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12831326&ad=4070717329&h=wIc7-tL&sig=CbmVWESo1BWoM731TlcOB9nCylN&v=2&isig=FzhRQXn0Vz1QryL8ul0otnDIyqz&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/email/scx/-p6l98fb/GPS-5240979-0.pdf/d8d80eb2-2e9b-44e0-b6f7-de1a9c15d726> 

BLAU: Reiteramos o Neutro e reduzimos PT para R$13 vs. R$14.5 anteriormente. Pipeline robusto em produtos de maior complexidade sustentam perspectiva de crescimento (CAGR ~15% em receita/EPS), mas riscos de execução, timing e capex levam a revisões e postergação de entregas. No curto prazo, momentum permanece limitado por ambiente de preços mais fraco e menor poder de precificação. Com valuation mais equilibrado e up

Valor Econômico | 19/03/2026 

A Cyrela, maior incorporadora paulistana em volume de produção, entende que o impacto de um prolongamento da suspensão de alvarás de construção em São Paulo pode ser “bastante relevante” para a sua operação em 2026, caso a medida — uma liminar do Tribunal de Justiça do Estado de São Paulo (TJSP), que acatou pedido do Ministério Público do Estado — não seja derrubada em breve.

Safra on Cyrela: 4Q25 results: In-line P&L with a few moving parts <https://apiext-bi.safra.com.br/apl-api-download-documentos-rdv/documento/PROSPECT-34413.PDF> 

Cyrela reported neutral quarterly results, with a few moving parts. On the one hand, the company posted a strong 29% YoY top-line expansion (+10% vs. Safra), reflecting the removal of suspensive clauses from highend launches, while it also benefited from stronger-than-expected equity income gains (+11% YoY). Still, EPS were just 3% above our estimates, largely supported by a BRL 58mn deferred tax benefit, which more than offset a higher-than-expected minority interest. Elsewhere, the company posted a BRL 38mn cash burn (vs. Safra’s BRL 12 mn cash burn).

Read more…

emails.subject: ENC: Axia & ISA Energia: Fairly priced transmission assets swap (BTG Pactual's Junqueira/Gushiken/Schutz)
emails.one_liner: Axia e ISA Energia trocam ativos de transmissão (Madeira, Garanhuns) e R$1.17bn, com impacto neutro de valuation.
emails.body:
________________________________

De: BTG Pactual Equities Research <sh-btgpactual-research@btgpactual.com>Em Nome deAntonio Junqueira <antonio.junqueira@btgpactual.com>
Enviado: sexta-feira, 20 de março de 2026 08:13:49 (UTC-03:00) Brasilia
Assunto: Axia & ISA Energia: Fairly priced transmission assets swap (BTG Pactual's Junqueira/Gushiken/Schutz)



BTG Pactual | Equity Research 


Sector Note - Brazil - Utilities 


Axia & ISA Energia 


20-March-2026 


 


Fairly priced transmission assets swap 


Read the full document here >> <https://www.btgpactual.com/research/report-view/118859>  

Simplifying corporate structures

Axia and ISA Energia announced a transmission asset swap aimed at simplifying both companies’ corporate structures. Axia will transfer to ISA Energia its 49% stake in the Madeira transmission line (R$761mn in RAP for 100%), while ISA Energia will transfer to Axia its 51% stake in the Garanhuns transmission line (R$158mn in RAP for 100%) and will pay Axia R$1.17bn in cash. After the transaction, Axia will consolidate 100% of Garanhuns, while ISA Energia will consolidate 100% of Madeira.

Fairly priced asset swap

We ran our calculations and reached an NPV of R$1.8bn for Axia’s 49% stake in Madeira and R$500mn for ISA Energia’s 51% stake in Garanhuns, which, combined with the R$1.17bn cash payment, totals R$1.7bn. As such, we view the transaction as fairly priced, with a neutral impact on valuation for both companies.

Positive transaction

We view the transaction as positive for both companies. In addition to simplifying their corporate structures, the transaction will provide liquidity for Axia and is in line with its strategy of divesting minority stakes. For ISA Energia, it should help slightly reduce leverage, from 3.63x ND/EBITDA in Dec-25 to 3.49x ND/EBITDA with the consolidation of Madeira.

Conversion of ISAE3 shares into ISAE4 shares

Alongside the asset swap, Axia approved the conversion of 19.76mn ISAE ON shares into PN shares at a 1:1 ratio. After the conversion, Axia’s stake in ISA Energia (21.6% in Dec-25) will be concentrated in PN shares, where free float and liquidity are higher. The conversion also aligns with Axia’s strategy of optimizing minority participations and might represent the first step of additional divestment of Axia’s participation in ISAE.

emails.subject: ENC: Petrobras & Fuel Distribution: Federal Government Introduces Guidelines for the Diesel Subvention Program (BTG Pactual's Almeida/Cunha)
emails.one_liner: Governo Federal introduz diretrizes para subsídio diesel: PBR vende a R$3.65/L e recebe R$0.32/L, totalizando R$3.97/L.
emails.body:
________________________________

De: BTG Pactual Equities Research <sh-btgpactual-research@btgpactual.com>Em Nome deRodrigo Almeida <rodrigo-r.almeida@btgpactual.com>
Enviado: sexta-feira, 20 de março de 2026 08:12:56 (UTC-03:00) Brasilia
Assunto: Petrobras & Fuel Distribution: Federal Government Introduces Guidelines for the Diesel Subvention Program (BTG Pactual's Almeida/Cunha)



 


BTG Pactual | Equity Research 


Sector Note - Brazil - Oil & Gas 


Petrobras & Fuel Distribution 


20-March-2026 


 


Federal Government Introduces Guidelines for the Diesel Subvention Program 


Read the full document here >> <https://www.btgpactual.com/research/report-view/118858>  

What´s new?

Yesterday, the federal government announced the decree 12.883/2026 that basically establishes that Petrobras needs to sell diesel at the current price (R$3.65/liter) to gain access to the R$0.32/liter diesel subvention from the federal government. Therefore, the reference price for Petrobras is R$3.97/liter. For importers and private refineries, the reference prices will be based on an import parity price (methodology to be defined by ANP).

What are the implications?

*	For Petrobras, they will be getting the subvention as long as they keep diesel prices at the current R$3.65/liter to distributors. Petrobras will be therefore getting R$3.65 (selling price for the molecule) + R$0.32 (subvention), for each liter of diesel sold.
*	The company could still eventually raise prices. For example, if Petrobras raised prices by R$1.00/liter to narrow the gap vs. Import parity, they would no longer receive the subvention, but the R$4.65/liter would be much closer to IPP. The point here is that raising prices remains at the company’s discretion and the reference price does not freeze.
*	In our model, we assume Petrobras remains at R$3.65/liter for the remainder of the year and gets a R$0.32/liter subvention.
*	Additionally, recent news flow <https://www1.folha.uol.com.br/mercado/2026/03/anp-manda-petrobras-retomar-leiloes-de-combustiveis-cancelados-esta-semana.shtml>  has pointed out that the federal government has requested for Petrobras to re-start the previously cancelled diesel auctions. If Petrobras re-starts its auctions, we believe diesel prices in this format could be offered at prices similar to the past fee weeks (~PB + R$1.80-2.20/liter). Furthermore, by selling imported diesel through auctions, Petrobras could price at IPP and still get the R$0.32/liter subvention. Important to note that these diesel auctions at IPP come as "upside to our model".
*	The removal of ICMS from diesel imports, which the federal government is proposing to states (to be discussed among the states on March 27), on the other hand is something that could cap "post-tax import parity price" for diesel. Assuming imports no longer have the R$1.17/liter, (i) the supply disruption risk would reduce but (ii) Petrobras's pricing policy ceiling ("the alternative customer cost") would reduce meaningfully.

Figure 1: PBR vs. Import Parity Prices If the ICMS Tax is Removed for Imports (R$/liter) 

 <https://salesforce-mailing-distribution-prod.s3.sa-east-1.amazonaws.com/Mailing%20Distribution/assets/2026-03-20/1774005027143.png> 

Source: Bloomberg, Petrobras and BTG Pactual 

Figure 2: PBR vs. Import Parity Prices Without the ICMS Tax Removal on Imports (R$/liter) 

 <https://salesforce-mailing-distribution-prod.s3.sa-east-1.amazonaws.com/Mailing%20Distribution/assets/2026-03-20/1774005027148.png> 

Source: Bloomberg, Petrobras and BTG Pactual 

Rodrigo Almeida 
Brazil – Banco BTG Pactual S.A.

+55 (11) 3383-6278

Safra on Steel: US market pulse   <https://apiext-bi.safra.com.br/apl-api-download-documentos-rdv/documento/PROSPECT-34406.PDF?idu=129698> 

Safra's US Steel Market Pulse, a biweekly intelligence brief designed to deliver concise, data-backed insights into the evolving dynamics of the U.S. steel industry. This edition covers the period from March 05 to March 19, 2026.

Each issue will synthesize developments across the flat and long product markets with a focus on price movements (spot, futures, and indexes), mill pricing strategies, capacity utilization, lead times, and inventory signals. We’ll also track domestic vs. import spreads, tariff and trade policy shifts (e.g., Section 232, AD/CVD), and corporate actions from key producers such as Gerdau, Nucor, Steel Dynamics, Commercial Metals and Cleveland-Cliffs.

Read more...

Safra on IABr: February Data <https://apiext-bi.safra.com.br/apl-api-download-documentos-rdv/documento/PROSPECT-34405.PDF?idu=129167> 

Outlook for local steelmakers is stronger in long steel than in flat steel, supported by marginally better consumption for rolled steel. Domestic sales decreased 3% YoY and imports of rolled steel products increased by ~45% YoY. On the positive side, long steel exports increased YoY and imports of long steel declined YoY. On the negative side, flat steel imports increased, and domestic sales of both products declined YoY. Moreover, crude steel capacity utilization decreased MoM. We see a more challenging dynamic for f lat steel than for long products. In this context, we view Gerdau and CSN as well-positioned names due to their exposure to the US steel market and to Brazil’s long steel and iron ore markets, respectively.

Read more…

Andean Press Headlines 


March 20, 2026 


Please find below the main headlines in the press from Argentina, Chile, Colombia and Peru. 


Argentina 


Argentina’s Trade Surplus Narrows to USD $788 Million in February, a Seven‑Month Low. Argentina’s trade surplus fell to USD $788 million in February, the lowest since July, as exports contracted and imports rebounded after four consecutive monthly declines. INDEC reported exports of USD $5.962 billion for the month, down 2.9% year over year, contributing to the slimmer surplus. 

Link to news here: Ambito <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12831024&ad=4070550669&h=FOoxXeH&sig=IhZCHP5zCF0AX3KsoCUf5pZQvvN&v=2&url=https://www.ambito.com/economia/el-superavit-comercial-se-redujo-us788-millones-febrero-minimo-siete-meses-n6257880>

Finance Minister-designate Jorge Quiroz outlined a policy package aimed at reversing the country’s recent decline, with objectives of lifting GDP growth toward 4%, achieving near full employment, and restoring fiscal balance. 

<https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12831024&ad=4070550669&h=XNi5Yne&sig=GOCbvLRgIFUda1M8gKVVCpuRCvh&v=2&url=https://www.df.cl/economia-y-politica/macro/pib-de-chile-crece-dentro-de-lo-esperado-en-el-cuarto-trimestre-y-la> <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12831024&ad=4070550669&h=XNi5Yne&sig=GOCbvLRgIFUda1M8gKVVCpuRCvh&v=2&url=https://www.df.cl/economia-y-politica/macro/pib-de-chile-crece-dentro-de-lo-esperado-en-el-cuarto-trimestre-y-la> Link to news here: Diario Financiero <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12831024&ad=4070550669&h=bRKf48z&sig=O738Py1OzRabDygnbnjMXMn5XWO&v=2&url=https://www.df.cl/economia-y-politica/politica/quiroz-revela-las-claves-de-su-formula-reactivadora-reinstaurar>  


Colombia 


January Imports Reach USD $5.90 Billion, Up 9.7% Year-on-Year. January imports totaled USD $5,902.9 million, a 9.7% increase from a year earlier. It is the highest January level so far this decade, second only to January 2022’s USD $6,050.5 million. 

Link to news here: La Republica <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12831024&ad=4070550669&h=gT3hEjI&sig=B6noPOqbO3bWXLLKTx52jpNl41t&v=2&url=https://www.larepublica.co/economia/importaciones-de-enero-sumaron-us-5-902-9-millones-9-7-mas-altas-que-hace-un-ano-4353154>  

GAD3 Poll: Cepeda and De la Espriella Would Advance to Runoff if Presidential Election Were Held Tomorrow. The first GAD3 survey shows Iván Cepeda leading with 35% and Abelardo de la Espriella in second with 21%, which would send both to a second round. Paloma Valencia ranks third with 16%. 

Link to news here: La Republica <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12831024&ad=4070550669&h=fdt4WI8&sig=HrgnQHj7prh6ftg3oeYk2uecQE0&v=2&url=https://www.asuntoslegales.com.co/actualidad/resultados-de-la-primera-gran-encuesta-de-gad3-despues-de-las-consultas-4353688?_gl%3D1*1j6smyk*_ga*Mjk2NDQwNjk4LjE3NzI3OTQwNzE.*_ga_M1RB1H0RF3*czE3NzQwMDI2NzckbzE0JGcxJHQxNzc0MDAyODU5JGo2MCRsMCRoMjA4NjU1MzU2Mg>  


Peru 


SNMPE Warns Congressional Changes to General Mining Law Threaten Formal Mining in Peru. Peru’s National Society of Mining, Oil and Energy (SNMPE) cautioned that amendments approved by Congress’s Energy and Mines Committee to the General Mining Law endanger formal mining. The industry group argues the bill effectively undermines the mining concession regime, shortening timelines to bring projects online and would contract investment and render the USD $63 billion project pipeline unviable in the coming years.

Current high‑conviction view: Is now the time to take profits? 


This year has begun strong—with over $6B of inflows into Brazil alone—and firm commodity prices have driven outsized gains in the most liquid names. At current valuation levels, we prefer to calibrate where upside has compressed or where multiples sit meaningfully above historical ranges and focus core exposure to the highest‑quality names. Within our coverage, our high-conviction ideas are: (a) for Oil & Gas: Petrobras, PRIO, Vista, and Vibra; (b) Metals & Mining: Vale and Aura; and lastly: (c) on Pulp & Paper: Suzano.

emails.subject: ENC: Brazil Retail & Consumer Goods: When the ball stops the mall: The dual impact of the World Cup on Brazilian retail (BTG Pactual's Guanais/Cesquim/Cendon)
emails.one_liner: World Cup 2026 impact on Brazil retail: +18-25% sporting goods sales vs -10-15% retail sales on match days. SBF a winner.
emails.body:
________________________________

De: BTG Pactual Equities Research <sh-btgpactual-research@btgpactual.com>Em Nome deLuiz Guanais <luiz.guanais@btgpactual.com>
Enviado: sexta-feira, 20 de março de 2026 08:00:00 (UTC-03:00) Brasilia
Assunto: Brazil Retail & Consumer Goods: When the ball stops the mall: The dual impact of the World Cup on Brazilian retail (BTG Pactual's Guanais/Cesquim/Cendon)



BTG Pactual | Equity Research 


Sector Note - Brazil - Retail 


Brazil Retail & Consumer Goods 


19-March-2026 


 


When the ball stops the mall: The dual impact of the World Cup on Brazilian retail 


Read the full document here >> <https://www.btgpactual.com/research/report-view/118831>  

The kick-off boost: sporting goods as the primary beneficiary

Historical World Cup cycles provide consistent evidence of meaningful sales acceleration in sporting goods, but the often-quoted +20–40% uplift is best understood as a triangulation across multiple datasets rather than a single disclosed figure. In Brazil 2014, industry data from IBGE and Abrasce showed sporting goods and apparel categories growing ~+18–25% y/y in June–July, versus low single-digit growth in preceding months. Nielsen retail panels also pointed to football-related SKUs (jerseys, balls) growing >+50% y/y, albeit from a smaller base. In 2018, Centauro (Grupo SBF) reported double-digit SSS in football categories, with management commentary indicating “significant acceleration vs baseline,” while Nike’s global reports showed football revenue growth in the high-teens, disproportionately driven by key markets like Brazil. In 2022, despite macro pressure, SBF indicated football categories growing ~+30% during the cycle. Importantly, this uplift is concentrated in a 6–8 week window, implying a temporary but highly profitable demand surge.

The “Brazil game effect”: lost hours, lost sales

The negative impact of Brazil match days on broader retail activity is equally well documented, though less frequently quantified. Data from Cielo and Getnet during the 2018 World Cup showed that retail sales volumes dropped between -10% and -15% on days when Brazil played, versus comparable weekdays. Foot traffic data from shopping malls (Abrasce) indicated intra-day declines of up to -40% during match hours, with partial recovery before and after games. In 2022, similar patterns were observed: credit/debit transaction data suggested that categories unrelated to the event (e.g., fashion, electronics) underperformed meaningfully on match days, while food and beverage partially offset declines. From a modeling perspective, assuming Brazil plays 5–7 matches (in this edition, national teams that reach at least the semifinals play 8 matches) and each match reduces effective selling hours by ~30–40% of a given day, the aggregate monthly impact translates into a -1% to -2% drag on total retail sales. Importantly, this effect is asymmetric: while sporting goods benefit from pre-event demand, most discretionary categories face a net loss of selling time. This creates distortions in high-frequency data, often requiring analysts to normalize monthly trends to avoid misinterpreting underlying demand conditions.

Match calendar dynamics: weekday vs weekend impact based on Brazil’s actual schedule

Using Brazil’s confirmed group-stage fixtures for the 2026 World Cup, we can build a much more accurate estimate of the weekday vs weekend disruption. Brazil will play: June 13 (Saturday) vs Morocco, June 19 (Friday) vs Haiti, and June 24 (Wednesday) vs Scotland . This already implies 2 weekday matches (Fri + Wed) and 1 weekend match (Sat) in the group stage. Assuming Brazil finishes first in the group, the team would likely play the Round of 32 on June 29 (Sunday), followed by the Round of 16 between July 4–7 (one of which is midweek), quarterfinals between July 9–11 (Thu–Sat), semifinal on July 14 or 15 (Tue/Wed), and the final on Sunday, July 19. Putting this together, a realistic path to the final would result in approximately 4–5 weekday matches and 3–4 weekend matches. Compared to prior World Cups, the 2026 calendar suggests a slightly heavier weekday skew.

A global parallel: the UK and European football tournament effect

The World Cup’s retail dynamics are not unique to Brazil and can be observed globally, particularly in football-centric markets such as the UK and Western Europe. During the 2018 FIFA World Cup and UEFA Euro tournaments, UK retailers reported similar patterns: strong growth in sporting goods and licensed merchandise, offset by weaker foot traffic in shopping districts during match hours. Data from the British Retail Consortium indicated that overall retail sales dipped on England match days, with high streets effectively “pausing” during key games. However, categories linked to the event—such as sports apparel, televisions, and food & beverages—experienced significant uplift. Notably, large-format retailers and supermarket

Source: Tesouro Direto, Economatica and BTG Pactual as of 19-Mar-2026 

Chart 2: Historical IRR & ERP - Last 3Y 

 <https://salesforce-mailing-distribution-prod.s3.sa-east-1.amazonaws.com/Mailing%20Distribution/assets/2026-03-20/1774000515856.png> 

Source: Tesouro Direto, Economatica and BTG Pactual as of 19-Mar-2026 

Recap of Last Week's Reports

Utilities Portfolio: Taking profits on the auction-related relative trades; Orizon vs. Cemig in

We’re closing two of our current relative ideas: Copel vs. Cemig (up 8% since inception) and Eneva vs. BOVA11 (up 26.3% since inception). One of the reasons for these ideas was to gain exposure to this week’s Capacity Auction. While we see potential upside in both long stories mentioned (as we believe markets haven't fully priced in all the value created), the recent move has been quite positive, and we’ve decided to close these positions. As a reminder, this L/S Utilities Portfolio is short-term focused and seeks short-term triggers and asymmetries. We believe both Eneva and Copel are long-term winning growth stories with further upside.

Read the full document here >> <https://www.btgpactual.com/research/report-view/118840> 

Brz Utilities: 19GW of peak capacity added to the system

The eagerly awaited 2026 Capacity Auction (LRCAP) took place this Wednesday. The government contracted a total of 19 GW of peak capacity. About 40% of this was from existing thermal plants, while the rest was from new capacity. Three-quarters of the new capacity was from thermal plants, with the remaining quarter from hydro plants. The government secured peak capacity for 2026-2031. The average discount on cap tariffs was 5.52%. The plants contracted will be paid a total of R$39 billion per year.

Read the full document here >> <https://www.btgpactual.com/research/report-view/118804> 

Taesa (S): 4Q - In-line EBITDA, strong bottom line

Taesa posted in-line 4Q results. Net revenues (BRGAAP) reached R$644mn, in line with our R$641mn and up 11% y/y. The y/y growth reflects the energization of Pitiguari and reinforcements at Novatrans, as well as IPCA-linked RAP adjustments (+3.9%), partially offset by deflation in IGP-M-linked RAPs (-0.3%). Total opex stood at R$119mn, or R$107mn when adjusted for non-recurring items (vs. BTG’s R$109mn), most related to consulting services. On a recurring basis, opex declined 3.7% y/y below 2025 4.2% inflation, driven by lower contingencies and bad debt expenses. As a result, adj. EBITDA came in at R$537mn, in line with us (R$532mn) and up 12% y/y.

Read the full document here >> <https://www.btgpactual.com/research/report-view/118776> 

Sabesp (B): Ending the year with tight lid on costs

Sabesp reported good 4Q figures. Adj. EBITDA reached R$3.37bn, 6% above our R$3.18bn estimate and up 10% y/y, driven by cost control. Net revenues reached R$5.68bn, in line with our R$5.67bn and up 2% y/y, as growth in billed volumes (+4% y/y vs. BTG’s +2.6% y/y) and the removal of discounts to large clients (+R$138mn) were partially offset by a weaker tariff mix due to social tariffs, with a negative impact of R$93mn.

Read the full document here >> <https://www.btgpactual.com/research/report-view/118741> 

Eneva (B): Two Days to go. What are markets pricing in?

Brazil’s long-awaited Capacity Auction (LRCAP) is about to occur. Eneva’s stock experienced intense volatility in the days leading up to the price cap announcement, and more volatility could be ahead.

Read the full

 <https://rsch.baml.com/r?q=WlDQtWWxHJP1fioezvTPng&e=claudio.franco%40bofa.com&h=n1e-wQ>  <https://www.extelinsights.com/voting> 

Picture of the day

 

US AI safe haven? US AI stocks basket (MLUSAI) 30-day vol differential vs EEM at recent history lows..



 

Highlights

 

FAQs <https://rsch.baml.com/r?q=yYltwCIvP6j1fioezvTPng&e=claudio.franco%40bofa.com&h=9xioVw> . FAQs: 1) Buy the geopolitical dip? History says yes but some key diffs today. 2) Oil impact: 10% increase=2-3% EPS boost..but consumption impacted with a 3 qtr lag. 3) Are Financials telling us this is a credit cycle? Sector lagging..we prefer GSIBs and highlight areas of risk. 4) Best hedge against equity vol? Quality. Plus: 5) AI in '26 & beyond. - Savita Subramanian

2026 Asia Tech Conference <https://rsch.baml.com/r?q=unJlykQVZDz1fioezvTPng&e=claudio.franco%40bofa.com&h=IEaHeg> . Highlights from the 2026 Asia Tech Conference held from 17-20 March 2026. A summary of insights and key takeaways from our latest conference - APR Technology Team


Global Memory Tech <https://rsch.baml.com/r?q=LdNOEy1yuOzJq5!6NWCEzA&e=claudio.franco%40bofa.com&h=sD3BSQ> . Our Asia tech conference was well hosted this week in Taiwan; memory guidance was broadly bullish due to price/margin/AI. DRAM spot price no longer rises meaningfully (flat this week) but NAND contract price rally (March) still seen. Samsung/Hynix/Micron capex rises but it includes lots of infrastructure spend; thus, low impact on global memory supply - Simon Woo

US Semiconductors <https://rsch.baml.com/r?q=9o99AAozRpbJq5!6NWCEzA&e=claudio.franco%40bofa.com&h=o1DyAA> . We attended OFC 2026, meeting with mgmt. from LITE, COHR, MRVL, MTSI, and CRDO and participating in many technical workshops. Expect a strong wave of growth in AI connectivity/optics through CY30 up to $90bn TAM. CPO, OCS, scale across all drivers. We raise estimates for LITE and COHR following constructive investor briefings, but prefer MRVL, CRDO, and MTSI - Vivek Arya

 

Global Pulp, Paper & Forest Products <https://rsch.baml.com/r?q=SbjVKsP-EO7Jq5!6NWCEzA&e=claudio.franco%40bofa.com&h=6w4XRw> . Over the past days we hosted several meetings at Shanghai Pulp Week. Although the March HWP hike remains very much alive.. we sensed that conviction over pass-through likelihood dropped. Although we wouldn't rule out that it gets implemented.. it could come at a cost (lower order intake for producers). Our impression is that we are nearing the end of the upcycle. - Caio Ribeiro

emails.body:
________________________________

De: Arthur Onishi De Godoy Silva <arthur.godoy@safra.com.br>Em Nome deLuiz C Da Motta Pecanha Filho <luiz.pecanha@safra.com.br>
Enviado: sexta-feira, 20 de março de 2026 07:38:56 (UTC-03:00) Brasilia
Cc: Arthur Onishi De Godoy Silva <arthur.godoy@safra.com.br>; Carlos Alberto de A Damasceno <carlos.albuquerque@safra.com.br>
Assunto: Safra's Morning Notes - Transportation




 

 

 

 <https://www.extelinsights.com/support/voting-help>                                                                 March 20, 2026

 

DAILY SUMMARY | COMP TABLE | EARNINGS CALENDAR | SOYBEAN AND CORN PRICES | LAST 10 REPORTS | TOP 10 MUST-READ REPORTS

Good morning!
Here is a summary of the news and comments in our Transportation daily. You can find details below:

HIGHLIGHTS

 

*         Government publishes Provisional Measure (MP 1.343/2026) establishing mandatory freight registration and blocking below-floor operations in real time. The measure requires all freight to be registered via CIOT — which must now capture origin, destination, values paid and applicable floor — and empowers ANTT to block issuance for any operation priced below the minimum. Penalties for contracting companies range from BRL 1 mn to BRL 10 mn per operation for repeat violators, with escalating suspension and eventual cancellation of carrier RNTRC registration. (Ag.Infra <https://agenciainfra.com/blog/governo-publica-mp-com-punicoes-a-descumprimento-do-piso-do-frete/> )

*         ANTT and Ministry of Transport announce operational rules implementing the MP, effectively blocking below-floor freight before it moves. The regulatory framework enters into force immediately, with ANTT given seven days to finalize supplementary regulation. The measures represent a structural shift from ex-post enforcement — where fines had become an operating cost — to ex-ante prevention via real-time CIOT blocking. (gov.br <file://arneb/cme/COMUM/Luiz%20P/Clipping/2025%20Clipping/•%09https:/www.gov.br/transportes/pt-br/canais_atendimento/avisos-de-pauta/2026/03/ministerio-dos-transportes-e-antt-anunciam-regras-que-regulamentam-mp-e-passam-a-impedir-fretes-fora-do-piso-minimo> )

*         Higher diesel costs are pushing freight rates up by as much as 7%, adding pressure to supply chains at a critical point in the harvest. With diesel S10 reaching BRL 6.89/liter — a 13.3% rise since the last rate adjustment — carriers are increasingly passing through costs, while trucker associations warn of a potential strike if the floor is not effectively enforced. (Globo Rural <file://arneb/cme/COMUM/Luiz%20P/Clipping/2025%20Clipping/•%09https:/globorural.globo.com/especiais/caminhos-da-safra/noticia/2026/03/com-diesel-mais-caro-custo-do-frete-chega-a-aumentar-ate-7percent.ghtml> )

*         The Middle East conflict is exposing a structural vulnerability in Brazilian agribusiness: concentrated dependence on imported inputs and narrow export windows. With fertilizer shipments blocked at the Strait of Hormuz, bunker fuel costs doubling for cabotage operators and freight rates rising sharply, the sector faces a simultaneous shock to input costs, logistics expenses and export competitiveness at harvest peak. (Globo Rural <https://globorural.globo.com/economia/noticia/2026/03/guerra-expoe-elo-vulneravel-do-agro-brasileiro.ghtml> ) 

 

*         Ministry of Cities approves BRL 9 bn in incentivized debenture issuances for metro and rail projects, concentrated in São Paulo. The largest authorization covers TIC Trens' BRL 7.86 bn raise for the Trem Intermetropolitano, Trem Intercidades (São Paulo–Campinas) and Line 7 modernization. Additional approvals include BRL 500 mn for the Baixada Santista LRT Phase II and BRL 460 mn for the Tietê and Jabaquara terminal requalifications. Separately, five projects received REIDI tax benefit approvals, the largest being Trivia Trens at BRL 14.3 bn covering Lines 11, 12 and 13. (Ag.Infra <https://agenciainfra.com/blog/aval-para-debentures-no-setor-metroferroviario-preve-r-9-bi-em-captacoes/> ) 

 

*         Rota 2 de Julho concession (BR-116/324, Bahia) to undergo technical adjustments at the TCU before auction, with new tender expected in July. The Ministry of Transport confirmed the TCU issued a routine clarification request — not a suspension — and that modifications to the project are likely while it remains under court review. (Ag.Infra <https://agenciainfra.com/blog/concessao-da-rota-2-de-julho-passara-por-ajustes-no-tcu-diz-secretaria/> )





DAILY SUMMARY

 

*         STF to attempt mediated settlement between trading companies and soybean producers over moratorium dispute (Globo Rural <https://globorural.globo.com/politica/noticia/2026/03/stf-tentara-acordo-entre-tradings-e-produtores-sobre-fim-da-moratoria-da-soja.ghtml> ) 

*         Abiove estimates 61.5 mn tonnes of soybean processing in Brazil in 2026 (Portos e Navios <https://www.portosenavios.com.b

BTG Pactual | Equity Research

	
LatAm Transportation Clipping

TRANSPORTATION 	
RAILWAYS & WATERWAYS	 
🔗 Grãos avançam em Chicago enquanto guerra perturba mercados de combustíveis e fertilizantes (Money Times)

Companhia	 Descrição	 Link	 
CCRO3	 Assembleia	 ➡️ <https://www.rad.cvm.gov.br/ENET/frmExibirArquivoIPEExterno.aspx?NumeroProtocoloEntrega=1492535> 	 

Notícias 	
TRANSPORTATION 	
Grãos avançam em Chicago enquanto guerra perturba mercados de combustíveis e fertilizantes 	
Money Times | 19/03/2026 	
Os contratos futuros da soja, milho e trigo negociados na bolsa de Chicago ampliaram seus ganhos nesta quinta-feira (19), com os ataques a instalações de petróleo e gás no Oriente Médio aumentando as preocupações sobre a guerra que interrompe o fornecimento de combustíveis e fertilizantes, segundo analistas.

A soja e o milho podem acompanhar os preços do petróleo porque ambas as culturas são amplamente utilizadas como matéria-prima para biocombustíveis, enquanto os grãos como um todo atraíram a demanda dos investidores em meio ao conflito.

⬆️ Leia a reportagem completa aqui <https://www.moneytimes.com.br/graos-avancam-em-chicago-enquanto-guerra-perturba-mercados-de-combustiveis-e-fertilizantes-pads/> .
	
Do aluguel à revenda: o plano da Localiza para rodar 10 mil carros híbridos e elétricos da BYD 	
Exame | 19/03/2026 	
A Localiza&Co, maior locadora da América Latina, firmou no início deste ano um acordo com a BYD para a compra de 10 mil veículos híbridos e elétricos ao longo dos próximos dois anos.

Os modelos — Song Plus, Song Pro, Dolphin e Dolphin Mini — serão incorporados às diferentes soluções da empresa: aluguel diário e mensal, carro por assinatura, gestão de frotas corporativas e a Localiza Seminovos.

⬆️ Leia a reportagem completa aqui <https://exame.com/esg/do-aluguel-a-revenda-localiza-detalha-plano-para-rodar-10-mil-carros-da-byd-no-brasil/> .
	
Abiove eleva projeção de processamento de soja para recorde de 61,5 milhões de t 	
Isto É Dinheiro | 19/03/2026 	
São Paulo, 19 – A Abiove elevou de 61 milhões para 61,5 milhões de toneladas a projeção de processamento de soja no Brasil em 2026, alta de 0,8% em relação à estimativa divulgada em janeiro. Se confirmada, a marca representará novo recorde para o esmagamento da oleaginosa no País e avanço de 4,8% ante as 58,698 milhões de toneladas consolidadas em 2025.

A revisão veio acompanhada de ajuste positivo também na produção brasileira de soja, que passou de 177,124 milhões para 177,847 milhões de toneladas, alta de 0,4% sobre a projeção anterior. Com mais matéria-prima disponível, a entidade elevou de 47 milhões para 47,4 milhões de toneladas a estimativa de produção de farelo, aumento de 0,9%, e de 12,25 milhões para 12,35 milhões de toneladas a de óleo de soja, alta de 0,8%.

⬆️ Leia a reportagem completa aqui <https://istoedinheiro.com.br/abiove-eleva-projecao-de-processamento-de-soja-para-recorde-de-615-milhoes-de-t> .
	
Companhias aéreas pedem que UE adie regras de combustível verde 	
Folha de São Paulo | 19/03/2026 	
Dirigentes das companhias aéreas europeias pediram a UE (União Europeia) nesta quinta-feira (19) para adiar as medidas "inviáveis" de sua agenda verde, alertando para o aumento das tarifas devido ao conflito no Oriente Médio.

O bloco rejeitou rapidamente qualquer adiamento, afirmando que as metas climáticas continuam em curso. "Temos um caminho que precisamos seguir. Continuamos com nossas metas e o setor precisa investir", disse Apostolos Tzitzikostas, comissário da UE para transporte sustentável e turismo, à Reuters

emails.subject: ENC: ...Feedback, ideas and 2 charts...
emails.one_liner: Analisa posicionamento de mercado, subdesempenho da Big Tech (NVDA, AMZN) e superdesempenho de "Shortage Stocks" (MU, COHR). COHR adicionada ao S&P 500.
emails.body:
________________________________

De: Franco, Claudio <claudio.franco@bofa.com>
Enviado: sexta-feira, 20 de março de 2026 05:40:53 (UTC-03:00) Brasilia
Para: Sell Side <sellside@torkcapital.com.br>
Assunto: ...Feedback, ideas and 2 charts...




Hartnett, Feedbacks & Ideas…

________________________________

This is Sales Commentary Only, Not Research. For Institutional Investor Use Only. 

 

First, Hartnett…BofA positioning metrics <https://rsch.baml.com/r?q=l8ZFAI1svwa836pqVj!jMQ&e=claudio.franco%40bofa.com&h=G5VHAw>  do not yet show a capitulation of bull sentiment seen at lows and great entry points back into stocks/credit…I remain on the sell the rips camp as long as positioning remains unfavorable…


 

Second…why no capitulation in equities yet? Maybe market is interpreting that way -> with Trump hitting new lows in terms of approval on economy & rising political costs for a president elected to end war & end inflation, big incentive for US to de-escalate and compress time of conflict…



 

Before we had into Villani’s…correction started Oct (Fed cutting at highs, Nvidia $100bn to OpenAI…peak liquidity/AI capex optimism); big corrections end when a. sell-off leaders become so oversold they trough (this is happening in software, bank loans, Bitcoin, Mag7, private credit), b. investors forced to painfully liquidate overbought longs (portfolios now de-grossing gold, semis, metals, EM, Europe, banks…), and c.  oil & US dollar lose bid ; sequence is playing out but ends only once markets convinced oil back below $100/bbl for good (exponential surges in oil always recessionary & consensus forecasts breezy 18% global EPS growth in '26)…

 

To Villani’s…

 

Comments touch on… Big Tech’s Leadership Crisis, Can “Shortage Stocks” keep working?, Staples Bears Re-Emerging, “44 Years in front of a Screen”, GenZ & Goldfish, Looksmaxxing, questioning the Industrial Acceleration, Healthcare CHAOS (and 2 tactical ideas), a must-read for your COO, Energy: Crossing the Rubicon, Financial Trader Q&A, plus more. 

 

From the TOP DOWN… tonight, I am going to take a departure from giving you the usual macro spin. There has been so much of that. I want to tread elsewhere. 

 

In the body of this e-mail, I am just going to offer up the stock-centric discussion points that The BofA Sector Specialist Team have harped on all week in their investor conversations. 

 

Just feedback, big ideas, and themes (plus a few other things I think you need to see). 

 

Starting with TECH… this is what the incomings to Specialists Brian Fenske and Annie Liu look like… 

 

INCOMING QUESTION: Why doesn’t Nvidia act better? 

 

CEO Jensen Huang in fine form at the GTC Event <https://rsch.baml.com/r?q=btKvvfe!9KP6N9NsluY1nw&e=claudio.franco%40bofa.com&h=g3LIGA> . Strong Guide(s). Powerful pipelines. +25% increases to Hyperscaler Capex in 2026/27. Upward revision… after upward revision… after upward revision. All for just 15x P/E?

 

WELL, it’s actually not an “Nvidia thing”. Every Magnificent 7 Stock (besides Google which is +1% YTD) has lagged the S&P this year… Tesla -21%… Microsoft -16%… Amazon -6%… Meta -5%… Broadcom -4%… Nvidia -1%. 

 

Is Brian surprised? Yes. Are many investors? Also, yes. 

 

REASONS? Positioning (still crowded), the obvious pivot to gargantuan Debt Issuance/Capex, more competitive overlap (various Business Models that are either buying/selling GPUs to accelerate Revenue), future AI-driven Job Loss fears [eventually] driving Government intervention, and just, until recently, a genuine infatuation with International Stocks, Cyclical Stocks, Small Cap Stocks, “Breadth”, Gold and just… other stuff.

 

A stark contrast to multiple years of narrative domination.  

 

Brian says: we have been here before. 

 

He thinks you should revisit his piece from 2022 called “The Leadership Crisis <mailto:brian.fenske@bofa.com;%20nicholas.villani@bofa.com;%20%20aliu11@bofa.com;%20daniel.orlov@bofa.com;?subject=Hey.%20%20%20can%20you%20send%20me%20THE%20LEADERSHIP%20CRISIS?> ”, when Large Cap Tech all uniformly felt and traded badly. 

 

HOWEVER, whereas the sustained downturn of 2022 was characterized by fundamental cracks, Revenue deceleration, post-COVID restructurings and capacity rationalization… he thinks the 2026 set-up is markedly different… AKA jaw-dropping Revenue accelerations (at scale), while beating/guiding higher on basically every key KPI. 

 

In short, he thinks the Big Tech underperformance will prove short-lived. 

 

Which name would he would point you to first escape The Leadership Crisis <mailto:brian.fenske@bofa.com;%20nicholas.villani@bofa.com;%20%20aliu11@bofa.com;%20daniel.orlov@bofa.com;?subject=Hey.%20%20%20can%20you%20send%20me%20THE%20LEADERSHIP%20CRISIS?> ? Amazon. 

 

AWS is accelerating to high +20% in 2026 on added capacity. There is an Anthropic kicker on demand, too. Their Trainium AI C

emails.subject: ENC: IT Hardware: Monthly Data Tracker: iPhone Tracking Well-Ahead vs. App Store Tracking Modestly Behind
emails.one_liner: iPhone builds +12% ahead of forecast; App Store revenue 1pt behind. Cloud capex up 63% Y/Y to $753B in 2026.
emails.body:
________________________________

De: Erik W Woodring <Erik.Woodring@morganstanley.com>
Enviado: sexta-feira, 20 de março de 2026 04:23:14 (UTC-03:00) Brasilia
Para: Sell Side <sellside@torkcapital.com.br>
Assunto: IT Hardware: Monthly Data Tracker: iPhone Tracking Well-Ahead vs. App Store Tracking Modestly Behind



	 
	 
	 
IT Hardware 	
Monthly Data Tracker: iPhone Tracking Well-Ahead vs. App Store Tracking Modestly Behind	 
Read Full Report <https://ny.matrix.ms.com/eqr/article/webapp/9bfe464c-22ea-11f1-99ea-46f6fece5bca?t=1773991379:5862:11968:acba1c0882e9&m=1&ch=CDF Research ISG Autoblast#e=c2VsbHNpZGVAdG9ya2NhcGl0YWwuY29tLmJy> 	 
(PDF: 66 Pages) 	
North America 	
Industry View   Cautious

Erik W Woodring, Dylan Liu, Maya C Neuman, Rauf Ural	 
March 20, 2026

	
	 App Store rev growth is decelerating in C1Q to +6% Y/Y, resulting in net revs of +7% Y/Y QTD, 1 point below our +8% Y/Y. Separately, another qtr of above-seasonal iPhone builds in the Jun qtr supports our well-above-Street iPhone forecasts. Finally, 2026 cloud capex now at $753B or +63% Y/Y. 

March App Store net revenue is growing 6% Y/Y MTD, a 310bps deceleration from February off a 170bps tougher Y/Y compare, implying 30bps ($80M) of downside to our March quarter Services revenue, all else equal. Based on the latest disclosures from Sensor Tower, we estimate App Store net revenue is growing 6% Y/Y month-to-date through March 18th, a 310bps deceleration from February off a 170bps tougher Y/Y compare (Exhibit 25). This implies that QTD through March 18th, App Store net revenue is growing 7.0% Y/Y, tracking 1 point behind our +8.0% Y/Y March quarter App Store forecast. Therefore, if the quarter ended on March 18th, App Store net revenue would be growing 7.0% Y/Y in the March quarter (vs. +6.2% Y/Y in the December quarter), translating into 30bps of downside (or ~$80M) vs. our March-quarter Services growth forecast of 13.5% Y/Y (vs. Consensus estimate of +12.6% Y/Y), all else equal.

Digging into the details, growth of the top 3 App Store markets in aggregate is decelerating in March — China is growing 4% Y/Y (vs. +5% Y/Y in February; Ex 28), the US is declining 2% Y/Y, a 3-point deceleration from February off a 50bps tougher Y/Y compare (Exhibit 29), and Japan is tracking -5% Y/Y (vs. +1% Y/Y in February; Exhibit 30) off a 70bps easier Y/Y compare. That said, Rest of World App Store growth is +18% Y/Y this month-to-date, a 410bps deceleration vs. 23% growth in February (Exhibit 31). Notably for the US App Store, net revenue per download is flat Y/Y MTD, down modestly from +0.5% Y/Y in February (decelerating 50bps M/M). While this represents a deceleration, US monetization per download remains stronger than the ROW, where revenue per download is decelerating 220bps M/M. We cannot clearly conclude that US App Store monetization deterioration M/M due to non-monetized link-outs, but it's something we continue to track closely. Looking ahead, we expect a slight headwind from the reduced App Store commission rate in China (from the standard 30% to 25%, effective March 15th), which accounts for ~20% of total App Store revenue, with mini app monetization — notably from Tencent, the single biggest developer on App Store — providing a partial positive offset.

C2Q26 iPhone builds of 52M units points to another above‑seasonal quarter of iPhone shipments. Our Greater China Hardware team (led by Sharon Shih) introduced their preliminary C2Q26 iPhone builds of 52M units (+12% Y/Y, ‑7% Q/Q) late last week, which includes 10M iPhone 17e builds, 1M units higher than a year ago. Following above‑seasonal C1Q26 builds of 56M units (unchanged), a ‑7% Q/Q decline in C2Q26 again outperforms typical seasonality (‑17% Q/Q). Based on the historical builds‑to‑shipments relationship, 52M June quarter builds would imply 57-58M iPhone shipments — largely in-line with our 58M unit forecast, but 7M units above Consensus shipments at 50M — reinforcing our above‑Street June quarter revenue forecasts. We see this iPhone build strength as evidence of sustained iPhone end‑demand amidst 3nm wafer constraints, consistent with our view <https://ny.matrix.ms.com/eqr/article/webapp/7491b2d2-c3b4-11f0-bfa1-4781374640f1?ch=rp&sch=ar>  that Apple’s additional wafer orders placed late last year should support upside to shipments in both C1Q26 and C2Q26. We leave our bullish iPhone shipment forecasts unchanged.

Data we're watching closely over the coming weeks:

*	Cloud Capex Tracker now points to 63% Y/Y growth in 2026, 5 points stronger than our last update (Exhibit 109). After refreshing Consensus and incorporating C4Q25 results from Alibaba, Tencent, Baidu, and CoreWeave, 2026 aggregate cash capex amongst the top 11 cloud spenders increased by $14B to $753B, implying a third consecutive year of 60%+ cloud capex growth. The higher 2026 base comes alongside a slightly lower 2025 estimate (‑$4B) driven by C4Q25 capex downside at Tencent and CoreWeave. Co

EM equity fund outflows worsened to -$5.0bn from -$3.1bn last week. ETFs saw heavy outflows (-$3.1bn) for the second consecutive week, following last week’s break from a 30-week inflow streak. Non-ETFs (-$2.0bn, -$1.3bn last week) sold-off for the third week. Within regional funds, redemptions from GEMs funds accelerated (-$2.7bn, -$300mn last week) while Asia ex-Japan continued to see heavy sell-off (-$2.5bn, -$2.6bn last week). EMEA outflows narrowed significantly (-$21mn, -$109mn last week). LatAm was back in the green with inflows of +$245mn after a brief sell-off last week(-$103mn). EM ex-China funds squeezed in +$145mn. YTD, EM equity funds are +$78.1bn. Incorporating February monthly data leads to a revision of +$169mn. 

Market-level flows. Taiwan posted a third week of heavy sell-off (-$5.7bn, -$5.3bn last week) while outflows also worsened in India (-$3.5bn, -$3.0bn last week). Korea also posted large outflows (-$3.2bn), selling off for the fourth week. The UAE (-$323mn, -$478mn last week) posted another week of heavy sell-off. Redemptions in Thailand eased versus last week, though still remaining high (-$358mn, from -$838mn). Malaysia (-$96mn), the Philippines (-$96mn), Qatar (-$91mn), and Saudi (-$101mn, one-week lag) also saw significant outflows. Brazil continued to counter the sell-off trend with inflows extending to 10 weeks (+$354mn) while Indonesia saw small inflows of +$73mn.

emails.body:
________________________________

De: BTG Pactual Equities Research <sh-btgpactual-research@btgpactual.com>
Enviado: sexta-feira, 20 de março de 2026 03:00:00 (UTC-03:00) Brasilia
Assunto: BTG Pactual: The Earlybird (March 20th, 2026)



The EarlyBird | Research

 <https://salesforce-mailing-distribution-prod.s3.sa-east-1.amazonaws.com/Mailing%20Distribution/assets/2026-03-19/1773972255722.png> 

03/20/2026 

 <https://salesforce-mailing-distribution-prod.s3.sa-east-1.amazonaws.com/Mailing%20Distribution/assets/2026-03-19/1773972255724.png> 


Company Research and News


LatAm 

Peru metals and mining - Mining concession draft proposal to face defeat

Peru’s Energy and Mines Commission has advanced a comprehensive overhaul of the General Mining Law, approving a substitute text by a margin of 11 votes to one, with three abstentions. This legislative package merges a dozen draft bills originating from diverse political factions and introduces three principal reforms: an escalation of concession fees, the imposition of more rigorous penalties, and a substantial reduction in the period allowed before a concession expires due to non-initiation of production. The amendments are particularly consequential for large-scale concessionaires, stipulating an increase in annual fees from US$3/ha to US$12/ha for holdings exceeding 100,000 ha – a threshold that encompasses nearly all major established mining firms.

 <https://salesforce-mailing-distribution-prod.s3.sa-east-1.amazonaws.com/Mailing%20Distribution/assets/2026-03-19/1773972255725.png> 

Read the report here <https://www.btgpactual.com/research/report-view/118844> . 

Colombia & Argentina Strategy - Corporate access recap: Ahead of the curve

Direct access to key decision-makers fosters superior investment insight. (I) Colombia Virtual Week featured deep dives into electoral risks, infrastructure stability, hydrocarbon exploration, monetary constraints, and financial sector resilience, revealing regulatory and fiscal challenges alongside structural strengths. (II) Argentina Energy Week showcased sector leaders emphasizing shale scaling, cost leadership, integrated strategies, and regulatory normalization, highlighting robust growth and cash flow visibility. (III) Overall, corporate access enabled early policy signals, clear capital allocation views, real-time thesis validation, and cross-sector macro-political linkage, essential amid Latin America’s evolving risk-return landscape.

 <https://salesforce-mailing-distribution-prod.s3.sa-east-1.amazonaws.com/Mailing%20Distribution/assets/2026-03-19/1773972255725.png> 

Read the report here <https://www.btgpactual.com/research/report-view/118848> . 

 


Brazil 

Brazilian Steels - What is going on?

The Brazilian steel sector faces persistent challenges despite anticipated price hikes. (I) Antidumping effects remain limited; imports surged 34% y/y, pushing penetration to 28%-33%. Domestic sales fell 3%, demand softened, and margins are near crisis levels below 10%. (II) Contract resets reduced automotive prices; flat steel commands a 14% premium over China, limiting hikes; construction shows resilience. (III) Global oversupply, notably China’s, depresses prices; absent broad tariffs, profitability will stay pressured, with gradual improvement expected only later in the year.

 <https://salesforce-mailing-distribution-prod.s3.sa-east-1.amazonaws.com/Mailing%20Distribution/assets/2026-03-19/1773972255726.png> 

Read the report here <https://www.btgpactual.com/research/report-view/118837> . 

The Builder #491 - Edition 491 | 19 Mar 2026

Main themes in a nutshell: In housing: SECOVI-SP data showed housing sales +6% y/y to R$3.3bn in January, driven by low-income. In the properties sector: (i) malls sales decreased 0.7% y/y in January according to Abrasce; and (ii) IBGE's PMC (retail index) rose 2.1% y/y in January.

 <https://salesforce-mailing-distribution-prod.s3.sa-east-1.amazonaws.com/Mailing%20Distribution/assets/2026-03-19/1773972255727.png> 

Read the report here <https://www.btgpactual.com/research/report-view/118787> . 

ESG Research - Brazil's truck fleet renewal: a new attempt to reduce emissions

Renewing Brazil's aging truck fleet offers a pathway to emission reduction and improved safety yet faces systemic limits. (I) In 2024, Brazil’s fleet of 2.3 million trucks averaged over 12 years old, contributing to high emissions and inefficiency. (II) The Move Brasil program allocates R$10 billion in financing to replace older trucks, requiring dismantling of retired vehicles; initial uptake reached R$5 billion in approved credits. (III) Broader emission cuts necessitate diversifying transport modes, enhancing rail and water freight, intermodal logistics, and biofuel adoption to address structural dependencies beyond fleet renewal.

Gui Mendes and Julia Orsi


Brazil Transportation


Views on Brazil’s Easing Cycle and Read-Through for the Sector

 	 
 <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12828639&ad=4068544062&h=bZBR4X0&sig=4CPKuDyGkBM9ROxaoHif4RllkR6&v=2&url=https://www.extelinsights.com/voting> 	
By Guilherme Mendes AC <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12828639&ad=4068544062&h=BjWz2Yu&sig=IOVoHRUcGrPITLyYwBZsaycstkO&v=2&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/analyst/O668345> , Julia Orsi <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12828639&ad=4068544062&h=BnbZ8B7&sig=7kwgZnOqeIWNQ7zh1xUJTATqa1j&v=2&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/analyst/F711278>  

Click here for the full document and disclaimers <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12828639&ad=4068544062&h=n5ZFEIH&sig=HEZl4yqxr15ZNziMhfmCYZ2hvgx&v=2&isig=aEnoAEn1Eqfn1W4EfqidsSiprdW&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/email/scx/-aresvb6/GPS-5241352-0/6931b4b1-74be-4e49-971d-0b2247def9da> 

As the first interest rate cut takes effect in Brazil—with the Selic reduced by 25 bp to 14.75% on Wednesday—we review how Brazilian transportation stocks have behaved in prior cycles. Across the last four hiking and cutting cycles (2008/2009, 2011, 2015/2016, and 2022/2023), transportation sub‑segments outperformed the Ibovespa in the 6–12 months following the first Selic cut in three of them, with 2022 being the exception. For this cycle, we acknowledge that there may be a lower correlation with prior periods of outperformance, as easing begins against the backdrop of an ongoing war that is lifting oil prices—adding uncertainty to the depth and duration of cuts—while Brazil’s Presidential elections in 2H26 likely introduce another layer of volatility. The J.P. Morgan Economics team currently expects the Brazilian Central Bank to resume 50 bp cuts from the May meeting onward, taking the policy rate to 11.75% by year‑end (from 11.50% previously). Within our Brazilian coverage, we prefer more defensive names with solid fundamentals, favoring MOTV3 (OW) and RENT3 (OW) - more details here <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12828639&ad=4068544062&h=m3dVmT2&sig=1r12TemgC3GzNrCKb8GQeDG1hBr&v=2&isig=7GeuTu87jKnQzcI9lTRTGmowDJJ&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/email/scx/ip43mg6o/GPS-5229477-0/6931b4b1-74be-4e49-971d-0b2247def9da> . 

*	Views on Previous Interest Rate Cycles. When analyzing the past four hiking/cutting cycles (2008/2009, 2011, 2015/2016, and 2022/2023), we observed that Transportation sub-segments—car rental, toll roads, and Infrastructure (ex-toll roads) & logistics—tended to outperform the most in the 6–12 months following the first Selic cut, with the exception of the 2022 cycle, during which our coverage underperformed. In the 2008/2009 cycle, the sector outperformed the Ibovespa by 55 p.p. on average 12 months after the first cut; by 27 p.p. in the 2011 cycle; and by 40 p.p. in the 2015/2016 cycle. However, in the most recent cycle, the transportation sector underperformed the IBOV by 17 p.p. in the 12 months after the initial cut. We attribute this primarily to bottom-up fundamentals. Moreover, despite policy easing, long‑term rates remained elevated, constraining a further re‑rating for long‑duration companies. Looking ahead to the current cycle, we believe the historical correlation may diverge, as stocks should also be influenced by two meaningful factors: the ongoing conflict in Iran, which is pushing oil prices higher, and Brazil’s presidential elections this year. 
*	Brazil Macro: 2026 Outlook Implies Higher Inflation. According to the J.P. Morgan Economic team <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12828639&ad=4068544062&h=OGr_YTA&sig=Z6vjjzll5GBb8yILu3V00s8wBsa&v=2&isig=K19pWhJGcjhkBTocjN1tnK0nW9V&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/email/scx/-hephq9r/GPS-5236591-0/6931b4b1-74be-4e49-971d-0b2247def9da> , the 2026 base case envisions a slowdown in Brazil’s economy alongside disinflation, allowing the BCB to cut rates more aggressively than the consensus. While data through late February supported this outlook, the Middle East conflict has introduced upside risks to oil prices and trade costs, complicating the easing path. In this revised scenario, J.P. Morgan now expects t

IT Hardware 	
March VAR Check / SNX Earnings Preview - Solid Near-Term, Uncertain 2H	 
Read Full Report <https://ny.matrix.ms.com/eqr/article/webapp/79f142be-2242-11f1-99ea-46f6fece5bca?t=1773981307:4869:9027:95722cac585c&m=1&ch=CDF Research ISG Autoblast#e=c2VsbHNpZGVAdG9ya2NhcGl0YWwuY29tLmJy> 	 
(PDF: 18 Pages) 	
North America 	
Industry View   Cautious

Erik W Woodring, Maya C Neuman, Dylan Liu, Rauf Ural	 
March 20, 2026

	
WHAT'S CHANGED?	 
TD Synnex Corporation (SNX.N)	 
Price Target	 
From:   $172.00	 To: $174.00	 
March VAR checks suggest C1Q ent HW spending is tracking slightly above prior expects, partially aided by pull-forward, though fast-rising prices leaves the 2H outlook more uncertain. This creates a tactically positive setup for SNX in F1Q, even as we cut 2026 global PC shipments to -16% Y/Y. 
Key takeaways	 

*	Ent Hardware spending is tracking slightly ahead of VAR expectations in C1Q (aided by pull-forward), w/ the C2Q pipeline supporting further momentum. 
*	However, risks are skewed to the downside as visibility into 2H is limited & HW prices are expected to move even higher as the memory supercycle intensifies. 
*	Large enterprise spend remains strong, mid-market and FED are showing incremental signs of improvement vs. 30 days ago, and SLED is still under pressure. 
*	We are reducing our PC market outlook again as memory headwinds intensify; now forecast units -16% Y/Y (vs. -5% Y/Y previously) in 2026 and -3% Y/Y in 2027. 
*	Tactically, we are positive into SNX earnings on 3/31 given (1) high likelihood of F1Q/F2Q upside and (2) incremental Hyve disclosures that can expand multiple. 

Preview to earnings

	 Modest revision higher	 
View our takeaways <https://ny.matrix.ms.com/eqr/article/webapp/79f142be-2242-11f1-99ea-46f6fece5bca/#tkr=SNX.N> 	
* Direction of next 12-month consensus EPS	 
Source: Company data, Morgan Stanley Research	 

VAR checks continue to point to near-term strength, though component cost inflation remains a key downside risk. Based on our latest VAR checks - conducted earlier this week - C1Q enterprise hardware spending is tracking in-line to slightly above expectations 30 days ago, with incremental improvement across Federal and mid-market demand in the month of February and March-to-date and continued strength in large enterprise, partially offset by SLED weakness. While still hard to quantify, the VAR we spoke with acknowledged there is ongoing pull-forward in the quarter as customers look to procure supply and get ahead of future price increases. Looking forward, the VAR we spoke with remains optimistic on 2026 and expects growth to accelerate Y/Y supported by a strong C1Q and expectations for an even stronger C2Q based on current pipelines, though he also acknowledged that visibility beyond the June quarter remains limited. While the reseller hasn't seen any demand weakness from higher vendor prices yet, pricing remains the primary swing factor impacting traditional hardware spending, and further price increases would likely weigh on demand and put his 2026 outlook at risk.

At the product level, server spending remains strong across both traditional and AI servers and networking spend continues to accelerate, consistent with what we heard from VAR's last month. What was most incremental from our checks earlier this week however, is an improvement across Storage and PCs where demand is tracking slightly ahead of prior expectations in the March quarter, with this momentum expected to carry over into C2Q as well. As a result, given the near-term demand strength - partially aided by pull-forward - spending growth for CY26 is tracking in-line to slightly above expectations from 30 days ago. However the reseller we spoke with was clear - visibility into 2H is extremely limited and the full-year outlook would be jeopardized by further hardware price hikes (which the VAR sees as highly likely) as the memory cycle continues to strengthen.

Memory inflation is driving OEM prices even higher with no relief in sight. Since our last check ~30 days ago, Hardware prices have continued to move higher across the board for the third consecutive month alongside memory inflation, and the reseller expects an additional 15% price increase across Hardware products more broadly by the end of C2Q26. While the risk of additional price hikes is driving some near-term demand strength, the VAR cautioned that in the event prices move another 20-25% higher (vs. 15% in his base ca

 <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12828528&ad=4068455191&h=bZBR4X0&sig=ZIZxKmBcvhgj1xc7LwOA8uPgYRo&v=2&url=https://www.extelinsights.com/voting> 	
By Joseph Giordano AC <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12828528&ad=4068455191&h=1oafVmh&sig=Mo35XwBBJqvZghHNLwLQSmgLguA&v=2&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/analyst/R103209> , Guilherme A. Vilela, CFA <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12828528&ad=4068455191&h=O-axWR_&sig=VhE3uQAZyDKIgBjeucGz8eUbSCv&v=2&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/analyst/R720687> , Giovanni Vescovi <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12828528&ad=4068455191&h=cUz-wv5&sig=a1H7J2ELa01x6i4N9yMqnPl2g8i&v=2&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/analyst/O798110> , Nicolas Larrain <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12828528&ad=4068455191&h=5k1WB8K&sig=QJQ6mNvxnanjc3HvqHOlQebpAYR&v=2&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/analyst/R596032> , Froylan Mendez <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12828528&ad=4068455191&h=OJFKx_a&sig=RPtkCL26yOROK0QssFYoB7Jk4cl&v=2&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/analyst/V579633>  

Click here for the full document and disclaimers <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12828528&ad=4068455191&h=nnXuMjS&sig=Mn4LTRCCk9JZfX4KdBwQAtOCF58&v=2&isig=4zMOfu807Rw6dursDVVxmUUA9e9&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/email/scx/-a0obb17/GPS-5229656-0/d2d68c75-f7ec-4bc1-9877-14bb26799e6d> 

Neutral	 
BLAU3.SA, BLAU3 BZ	 
Price (19 Mar 26):R$9.80	 
▼Price Target (Dec-26):R$13.00	 
Prior (Dec-26):R$14.50	 

Blau (N-rated) stands at a pivotal juncture, leveraging a robust pipeline of high-complexity and biological products, but facing meaningful execution risks and market uncertainties. In this report, we refresh our MT top-line modeling, applying differentiated probabilities to pipeline delivery to reflect risks around timing, product success, and capex overruns. As pipeline execution evolves, we plan to revisit our framework, but at present, we are delaying some deliveries versus prior estimates. Our revised model forecasts a good 5y top-line and EPS CAGR of 15%. However, ST momentum remains limited due to a softer public market and a likely tougher private environment. At 8.4x/8.0x 2026E/2027E P/E and with limited upside to our revised Dec’26 PT of R$13.00/sh (from R$14.50), we see risk-reward as balanced, estimating a fair forward multiple at 10x while low share liquidity remains a key constraint. We remain Neutral on Blau. 

*	Revisiting our MT top-line forecast. Blau’s pipeline, guided through 2032E, targets a market of ~R$14bn, on top of the current R$10.4bn TAM where Blau holds ~20% share. The pipeline splits into synthetic products (2025-32E) and monoclonal antibodies (+2028E), with the latter carrying higher execution risk. Adjusting for delivery risk and market growth, we estimate an incremental TAM of ~R$10.9bn, and a fair value of R$13.0/sh assuming a 25% terminal market share. In a full delivery scenario, Blau could be worth R$16.5/sh, by our estimates. 
*	On a risk-adjusted basis, Monoclonal Antibodies have limited impact on mid-term P&L but could be transformational if fully delivered. On a risk-adjusted basis, mAbs have limited mid-term P&L impact, reaching just 4% of consolidated top-line by 2028E, but potentially ramping to 20% (R$1.04bn) by 2035E as new technologies come online. If fully successful, mAbs could be transformational, driving significant upside to sales and EBITDA. 
*	ST momentum is likely soft. Short-term performance is constrained by limited pricing power, as reflected in the IPM-H index (-0.3% y/y in 4Q25, -0.9% in January), and a challenging market environment. We forecast 2026E net revenues of R$2.0bn (+16% y/y, but 3% below our prior estimate), with sequential gross margin improvements to 40.4% in 2026E (+90bps y/y), supported by better mix and operational efficiency. 

  

ST Trends 


Drug prices underscore limited pricing 


The IPM-H index measures the average price of drugs sold to hospitals through the Bionexo platform – a private company that consolidates pricing data of drugs in the non-retail market. In 2025, we saw

We update our estimates for Opsimex post 4Q25 results, raising our estimates reflecting the latest guidance, with the tower deployment expectation up to 755 for 2026E (vs 500 previously). We are raising our estimates for Net Revs by 1.3%/1.9% and keep our margins virtually stable at 96.1%, driving our EBITDA up by 1.4%/2.0%. As we incorporate the higher tower adds, we also raise our Capex by 43% for 26E, resulting in Ps1.2bn/Ps0.9bn for 26E/27E. We keep our Dec-26 PT stable at Ps 26, as the adjustments to estimates are offset by higher capex and a higher WACC of 10.9% (vs 10.5% previously). We maintain our Overweight rating on Opsimex, which we see as a discounted play: it delivers 11% FCF yield ‘26E, while growing that FCF by a 9% CAGR’26-29E.

By Marcelo Santos, CFA AC <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12828514&ad=4068425614&h=wnJ4ZTi&sig=AHYyHqL563eWhREuRxAtT52OGzD&v=2&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/analyst/R036945> , Livea Mizobata <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12828514&ad=4068425614&h=ePRKAdB&sig=LVVVdbtPXfJERUVvS6kIKZAnXqP&v=2&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/analyst/R717084>

Neutral	 
VTEX, VTEX US	 
Price (19 Mar 26):$3.85	 
Price Target (Dec-26):$5.00	 
	

We update our VTEX estimates post 4Q25 results, incorporating the company’s guidance of lower growth, but higher profitability. We assume subscription revenues will grow 6.5% y/y FXN in 2026 (guidance: mid-to-high singles), while adj. EBIT ex-SBC margin would reach 22% (guidance: low 20s). This represents a 2% decline in the revenues expected for 2026, but a 28% increase in adj. EBIT ex-SBC, although this is mostly an anticipation of a margin we already expected the company to achieve in future years. For 1Q26, we assume subscription revenues will grow 5% (guidance: mid-single digits), while margins will be 15% (guidance: mid-teens). We remain Neutral and keep our $5 Dec-26 PT, as we wait for more visibility on a potential revenue re-acceleration.

emails.subject: ENC: IDEA: Autos & Shared Mobility: Autos and Energy Converge
emails.one_liner: Automakers (TSLA, GM, F, BWA) pivot to energy storage, AI/data centers; TSLA signs $4.3bn LGES battery deal, GM retools.
emails.body:
________________________________

De: Andrew S Percoco <Andrew.Percoco@morganstanley.com>
Enviado: sexta-feira, 20 de março de 2026 01:02:55 (UTC-03:00) Brasilia
Para: Sell Side <sellside@torkcapital.com.br>
Assunto: IDEA: Autos & Shared Mobility: Autos and Energy Converge



	 
	 
	 
Autos & Shared Mobility 	
Autos and Energy Converge	 
Read Full Report <https://ny.matrix.ms.com/eqr/article/webapp/f80fbd1e-23d8-11f1-99ea-46f6fece5bca?t=1773979341:5694:11968:acba1c0882e9&m=1&ch=CDF Research ISG Autoblast#e=c2VsbHNpZGVAdG9ya2NhcGl0YWwuY29tLmJy> 	 
(PDF: 7 Pages) 	
North America 	
Industry View   In-Line

Andrew S Percoco, Young Suk Shin, Jack Lu, David Arcaro, CFA, Daniela M Haigian, Jahvonte G Bain & 1 more...	 
March 20, 2026

	
This week's announcements reflect efforts from automakers to diversify revenue streams from cyclical auto end markets tethered to the uncertain fate of the consumer within a volatile market and attach to industries experiencing secular growth – see TSLA, GM, F, BWA, etc. 

On Tuesday, the US Department of Interior confirmed <https://www.reuters.com/business/energy/us-government-confirms-tesla-lg-energy-solutions-43-billion-battery-deal-2026-03-17/> that Tesla and LG Energy Solution (covered by Young Suk Shin) signed a $4.3bn battery supply agreement (~50-60 GWh on our estimate). This facility in Lansing, Michigan was originally meant to support General Motors' <https://investor.gm.com/news-releases/news-release-details/gm-sell-stake-lansing-battery-cell-plant-partner-lg-energy>  EV business, but was subsequently sold back to LG in December 2024 as a part of GM's capacity rationalization exercise. Production at the LFP battery cell plant is scheduled to begin next year and will supply Tesla's Megapack 3 energy storage systems produced in Houston.

In a separate announcement <https://financialpost.com/pmn/business-pmn/gm-pivots-tennessee-battery-plant-to-tap-energy-storage-boom> , Ultium Cells, a battery joint venture between General Motors and LG Energy Solution, will start production of LFP cells at its Tennessee plant next month in effort to revitalize factories idled by the EV demand slowdown. LG is retooling four other EV battery plants across North America to make LFP cells for stationary ESS, adding flexibility beyond EV battery production to stabilize demand.

These newly announced partnerships reinforce 3 key themes: 1) repurposing BEV capacity for ESS, 2) onshoring critical supply chains, and 3) lessening China supplier dependence.

1) The electrification landscape is shifting as OEMs increasingly redirect capital and capabilities from BEV to energy storage systems, while suppliers similarly look to diversify battery exposure beyond autos. We forecast <https://ny.matrix.ms.com/eqr/article/webapp/594f59ca-b8d3-11f0-a4e9-13dd0cf82def#/section=4> a 15.9mn 2026 US Auto SAAR, with ICE volumes +1% and BEVs -20% y/y – while autos is levered to the consumer with a wide variability in outcomes (2027 US SAAR bull case of 17.5mn vs. bear case of 14.5mn), energy demand has grown exponentially on the Powering AI theme. Ford is a notable example, entering the BESS market <https://ny.matrix.ms.com/eqr/article/webapp/7453c766-da1a-11f0-a3ae-2aac6342f563?ch=rp&sch=cc> with roughly $2bn of planned investment and targeting ~20 GWh of annual capacity from 2027. This aligns with our Global Utilities analysts’ forecast for stationary battery deployments to grow at a ~24% CAGR to ~875 GWh annually by 2030, before considering any incremental upside from data center co-location.

See our recent Global Insight from the team: <https://ny.matrix.ms.com/eqr/article/webapp/c7a24f94-f1af-11f0-b5dd-981530a7f3f3?ch=rp&sch=ar> Flexible Power – The Next Wave of Growth in AI <https://ny.matrix.ms.com/eqr/article/webapp/c7a24f94-f1af-11f0-b5dd-981530a7f3f3?ch=rp&sch=ar> .

Global ESS annual incremental deployment forecast for data centers 
 
Source: Rystad, Morgan Stanley Research (e) estimates 
...including utility-scale ESS in power market 
 
Source: Rystad, Morgan Stanley Research (e) estimates 

Beyond energy storage, BorgWarner announced with 4Q earnings its entry into the data center power market, potentially adding $3bn of revenue when ramped to 2GW – the stock rallied 22% on the day. See our deep dive here: BorgWarner Inc.: The Next Data Center Power Beneficiary? (12 Feb 2026) <https://ny.matrix.ms.com/eqr/article/webapp/0c08ba30-075c-11f1-8aaa-5b8e25e1b220?ch=rp&sch=cr> .We would caveat that we believe the commercial development cycle is likely much longer than appreciated by the market (several years before they ramp to the full 2 GW of targeted capacity).

2) Shoring up critical supply chains. Across 4Q25 earnings, OEM/supplier management teams consistently highlighted onshoring and regionalization as key drivers of their supply chain strategy. In their 4Q25 release, Tesla highlighted 4680 cell production has begun in Austin and reiterated expectations tha

emails.subject: ENC: Colombia & Argentina Strategy | Corporate access recap: Ahead of the curve (BTG Pactual's Guardiola/Muñoz/Leyva)
emails.one_liner: Recapitulamos 18 interações corporativas com CEOs, CFOs e formuladores de políticas na Colômbia/Argentina.
emails.body:
________________________________

De: BTG Pactual Equities Research <sh-btgpactual-research@btgpactual.com>Em Nome deDaniel Guardiola <daniel.guardiola@btgpactual.com>
Enviado: quinta-feira, 19 de março de 2026 23:00:00 (UTC-03:00) Brasilia
Assunto: Colombia & Argentina Strategy | Corporate access recap: Ahead of the curve (BTG Pactual's Guardiola/Muñoz/Leyva)



BTG Pactual | Equity Research 


Colombia & Argentina Strategy 


19-March-2026 


 


Corporate access recap: Ahead of the curve 


Read the full document here >> <https://www.btgpactual.com/research/report-view/118848>  

Over the past six months, we have delivered investors with an extensive corporate access agenda, hosting 18 virtual and in person events with CEOs, CFOs, policymakers, pollsters, and industry leaders across Colombia and Argentina. These interactions provided investors with direct and unfiltered insights into strategy, regulation, capital allocation, voting dynamics, and macro risks at a time of elevated uncertainty in the region.

Our agenda combined high impact flagship events, including Colombia Virtual Week, a dedicated session with the CEO of AtlasIntel to discuss Colombia’s presidential elections, a timely discussion following Nicolás Maduro’s arrest and its regional implications, and Argentina O&G and Energy Week, alongside a continuous flow of targeted sessions across sectors.

Across interactions, one message stood out: access creates edge. Rising macro risks, improving corporate discipline, and a structurally attractive energy complex are redefining the investment landscape in the region. By connecting investors directly with decision-makers, our corporate access platform enabled early identification of inflection points, deeper conviction in investment theses, and a differentiated perspective on risks and opportunities not yet fully priced by the market.
