emails.subject: ENC: Cyrela (Buy): Strong revenue and decent FCF drove a solid Q4 (BTG Pactual's Cambauva/Fabris/Pascale)
emails.one_liner: Cyrela Q425 sólido: receita forte, FCF decente. Mantida recomendação de Compra, TP R$40,00, P/L 6x 26E.
emails.body:
________________________________

De: BTG Pactual Equities Research <sh-btgpactual-research@btgpactual.com>Em Nome deGustavo Cambauva <gustavo.cambauva@btgpactual.com>
Enviado: quinta-feira, 19 de março de 2026 22:59:21 (UTC-03:00) Brasilia
Assunto: Cyrela (Buy): Strong revenue and decent FCF drove a solid Q4 (BTG Pactual's Cambauva/Fabris/Pascale)



BTG Pactual | Equity Research 


Company Note - Brazil - Real Estate 


Cyrela (Buy Rating; TP: R$ 40.00) 


19-March-2026 


Strong revenue and decent FCF drove a solid Q4 


Read the full document here >> <https://www.btgpactual.com/research/report-view/118841>  


EPS of R$1.55 (+41% y/y; exactly what we had); ROE of 27%


Cyrela's 4Q25 results were solid: (i) nice P&L expansion y/y (in line with us), due to strong revenues (more on that below) and (ii) decent FCF generation (slightly above our call), on big dividends from subsidiaries. Net revenues totaled R$3.23bn (+29% y/y, in line with us). Adjusted gross margin was 33.7% (+40bps y/y; 30bps below us), impacted by the sale of compact units in Vista Milano project, and EPS was R$1.55 (+41% y/y; exactly what we had), leading to a strong annualized ROE of 27%.


Our two cents on Cyrela's big revenues in Q4


As aforementioned, Q4 revenues were boosted by a change in accounting rules. Prior to Q4, Cyrela would wait (i) either 6 months after launch or (ii) until the project reaches 50% of sales, before starting to book revenues. But, since the company has never quit a project after its launch, Cyrela is now booking revenues once its sales team decides that it will go ahead with the project. Consequently, we had big revenue recognition from some projects in Q4 (e.g., Epic and Vista Milano, with R$328mn and R$131mn, respectively, plus R$302mn came from projects launched in 4Q25).


FCF generation of R$74mn; ND/Equity of 17%


Cyrela posted a decent R$74mn FCF generation in 4Q25 (slightly above our R$50mn forecast). Cashflow generation was also helped by the collection of ~R$240mn from its JVs (mostly Cury and Lavvi), which opted to bring forward dividends to Q4 to avoid taxation. Cyrela also announced R$1.4bn in dividends in Q4, which drove its net debt to R$1.97bn, resulting in a 17% ND/Equity ratio, which is above historical average, but still pretty comfortable (mostly in light of the strong FCF we expect in 2026).


Solid (in line) 4Q25, with FCF a tad above us; Buy rated (6x P/E '26E)


Q4 was solid: (i) EPS was in line with us, with strong top line growth and 27% ROE; and (ii) FCF generation was slightly above us, helped by its JVs. We expect a positive market reaction tomorrow, but we highlight that most of the P&L expansion was driven by an accounting adjustment in the company’s revenue recognition. Despite a more challenging environment for the mid/high-income segment (recently, we discussed that in two reports: 2026 Outlook: Look before you leap <https://www.btgpactual.com/research/report-view/109585>  and SP's mid/high-end inventory under the microscope <https://www.btgpactual.com/research/report-view/117091> ), we keep our Buy rating on Cyrela, our Top Pick in the mid/high-income segment at 6x P/E 2026E.

emails.subject: ENC: Petrobras (PBR): First Take: Government announces guidelines for diesel price reference for subsidies purpose; negative
emails.one_liner: Governo anuncia diretrizes para preço de diesel e subsídios, impactando negativamente Petrobras com possível perda de US$1.2bn FCF em 2026.
emails.body:
________________________________

De: Bruno Amorim, CFA <bruno.amorim@alerts.publishing.gs.com>
Enviado: quinta-feira, 19 de março de 2026 22:34:53 (UTC-03:00) Brasilia
Para: Sell Side <sellside@torkcapital.com.br>
Assunto: Petrobras (PBR): First Take: Government announces guidelines for diesel price reference for subsidies purpose; negative


 	 
 	 
	 
 	 Print PDF <https://publishing.gs.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fpublishing.gs.com%2FtrustedLink%3Fopentoken=T1RLAQJ1g5wkbyGd3UlC_UwmV-dbIsn9WxBpgb1ivfFRK15hOm6z8u7bAACw_jZ7ZqyX6dZF3pCi6vccYEs4wBYaSwU7N2sLNVxCWIbTdHv7thhHG7J8r7ika0KD4yMb6SHn-YzDjTZJ4S3jLIiBraxvWKzWrkV81DbfrF4z-IMuI0K7oIEmEbJs1Jw1mptq4NBCXH95WUztwtEUsiy8iSJwbehfo1Gjdy8MBWw-WX0m4AT5jeOGb3iNmpA4VoK7l_uY88qpc0mo0V6Vg38uTKSKTeyOtYahbRkNcYY*%26id=55e2f5f0-e589-4bc1-9da1-a2a90f1564c2%26utm_medium=pa%26renditionType=pdf/1/0100019d08e19131-968f03c5-74f7-496d-ba26-68e829cfa937-000000/euDSvFxJd5-q3JCfOkbiOI2AI2zoJUhiyE3sMj8oulI=449>  	 	 | 	 	 Read <https://publishing.gs.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fpublishing.gs.com%2FtrustedLink%3Fopentoken=T1RLAQJ1g5wkbyGd3UlC_UwmV-dbIsn9WxBpgb1ivfFRK15hOm6z8u7bAACw_jZ7ZqyX6dZF3pCi6vccYEs4wBYaSwU7N2sLNVxCWIbTdHv7thhHG7J8r7ika0KD4yMb6SHn-YzDjTZJ4S3jLIiBraxvWKzWrkV81DbfrF4z-IMuI0K7oIEmEbJs1Jw1mptq4NBCXH95WUztwtEUsiy8iSJwbehfo1Gjdy8MBWw-WX0m4AT5jeOGb3iNmpA4VoK7l_uY88qpc0mo0V6Vg38uTKSKTeyOtYahbRkNcYY*%26id=55e2f5f0-e589-4bc1-9da1-a2a90f1564c2%26utm_medium=pa/1/0100019d08e19131-968f03c5-74f7-496d-ba26-68e829cfa937-000000/r_j89Db3fmjH3jesPHssJwPpBkBDvXaZXCFO09dGmcY=449>  	
 	 
	
 	 
Petrobras (PBR): First Take: Government announces guidelines for diesel price reference for subsidies purpose; negative 
	
19 March 2026 | 10:31PM BRT | Research | Equity 	
	
	
Our take. Negative news. While we still have limited visibility on the practical implications from today's decree, our preliminary assessment suggests Petrobras will likely lose BRL 0.32/L competitiveness vs. other diesel players (importers and refineries), for an estimated negative FCF impact of USD 1.2bn in 2026 FY. We believe this measure increases uncertainty around timing for an eventual adjustment in diesel prices, especially when considering recent news flow <https://publishing.gs.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fpublishing.gs.com%2FtrustedLink%3Fopentoken=T1RLAQIQa6RyuPxCBW6H5qqYdQzOX7StBBAZQNXFO8mWXHLlcxeb6wT5AACwDvH_Ee2a1KCTB4aCTjaJGtQMJp0povafIs2C9sDbdBKburOab993D7YUbAcjeACBHClg25G3sBgo46Rrm8dKtrJjOaOYMMz-E_4qdF8yEKcMiFsW1r97jotVwHXtO1SQPlVClw1Owg8WtG-qUr0NvTUUC27mUhFIcbER-nZep1ZfR_idT6zPIf01U_G6cBypfIy3oncX2n1crEd-VytFncnCADinlO02CiPw6ztK3gM*%26id=d1c282ef-c3d0-4fe8-971b-eeba331b2e4a%26utm_medium=pa/1/0100019d08e19131-968f03c5-74f7-496d-ba26-68e829cfa937-000000/yQFmGaHrcXiie5aDPLH1TispKaiyWYXhuLTjaeMfcaE=449>  around potential diesel shortages and the suspension from fuel auctions originally scheduled for early next week. 

The Brazilian government published today (March 19) a decree specifying the general rules and broader guidelines for the calculation of the diesel price reference which shall be used to determine at which price local producers and importers would have the right to receive the previously announced subsidy of BRL 0.32/liter. Recall, the government recently announced <https://publishing.gs.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fpublishing.gs.com%2FtrustedLink%3Fopentoken=T1RLAQKR7eHjWH1-9Jl_RlnaS_MTZiqlkBBjIkJoSmWWxjf4_vyYZdXIAACwpe3LE4r6Nuk_Lp2lYgqTlAoBLGuB_sGOkNs2h_cowXUZjbtj1AA0HGJG0saLhKf7D64mEn0ZS6PJmrNLxMLwSI6Gd1jpviRS7G5toFcdMSInqF24PTnEqZMq3NLBuobOGcWKYIapkBFrVZqbcGCM78BXnPwJwqdYtBXGANIy0kvNo7VhOax3vzzdFdEIm-Tx-Bv7Ke0vDaT07VZw1jjjCJXfZkyFXuM6lyAbCtrMCkI*%26id=41301e7f-0854-424b-8f55-c122bf931e8f%26utm_medium=pa/1/0100019d08e19131-968f03c5-74f7-496d-ba26-68e829cfa937-000000/nx9Sm4tZmUTP05W1Rp1tZzIdaIzUlL8s24-KV72aEcw=449>  i) the reduction of federal taxes over diesel prices, and ii) a BRL 0.32/liter subsidy to refineries and importers, both measures aiming to dampen the effect of higher diesel prices for final consumers. 

According to the document <https://publishing.gs.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fwww.in.gov.br%2Fweb%2Fdou%2F-%2Fdecreto-n-12.883-de-19-de-marco-de-2026-694120980/1/0100019d08e19131-968f03c5-74f7-496d-ba26-68e829cfa937-000000/KGDhVMyNjBeiCpLo7Oir849YLKBTdwGuSZ_pvklq5b4=449> , the reference price for i) diesel importers and ii) local producers which acquire oil from third parties; will consider import parity prices as benchmark. Meanwhile, the reference price for local producers which use their own oil volumes as input (Petrobras for instance) will consider their current diesel prices + BRL 0.32/liter as a benchmark. Those charging fuel prices in line with their respective reference price (with BRL 0.32/liter deducted) will be eligible to receive the subsidy.

In practical terms, if Petrobras seeks to receive the subsidy from the government, we understand the measure implies Petrobras cannot raise diesel prices. Otherwise, if PBR were to decide to increase diesel prices above such level, it would not

emails.subject: ENC: Peru metals and mining: Mining concession draft proposal to face defeat (BTG Pactual's Perez-Novoa/Mery/Correa/Arazi)
emails.one_liner: Proposta de concessão de mineração no Peru (taxas mais altas, prazos reduzidos, lucro-compartilhado) deve ser rejeitada pelo Congresso.
emails.body:
________________________________

De: BTG Pactual Equities Research <sh-btgpactual-research@btgpactual.com>Em Nome deCesar Perez <cesar.perez@btgpactual.com>
Enviado: quinta-feira, 19 de março de 2026 22:11:40 (UTC-03:00) Brasilia
Assunto: Peru metals and mining: Mining concession draft proposal to face defeat (BTG Pactual's Perez-Novoa/Mery/Correa/Arazi)



BTG Pactual | Equity Research 


Sector Note - Peru - Infrastructure 


Peru metals and mining 


19-March-2026 


Mining concession draft proposal to face defeat 


Read the full document here >> <https://www.btgpactual.com/research/report-view/118844>  


Bicameral legislature anticipated to veto controversial measure


Peru’s Energy and Mines Commission has advanced a comprehensive overhaul of the General Mining Law, approving a substitute text by a margin of 11 votes to one, with three abstentions. This legislative package merges a dozen draft bills originating from diverse political factions and introduces three principal reforms: an escalation of concession fees, the imposition of more rigorous penalties, and a substantial reduction in the period allowed before a concession expires due to non-initiation of production. The amendments are particularly consequential for large-scale concessionaires, stipulating an increase in annual fees from US$3/ha to US$12/ha for holdings exceeding 100,000 ha – a threshold that encompasses nearly all major established mining firms. Moreover, the permissible window to commence extraction activities is halved from 30 to 15-years. The penalty framework is also restructured: following a decade of inactivity or failure to demonstrate minimum production or investment, concessionaires will incur a penalty equivalent to 10% of the annual concession payment. In addition, the new provisions mandate direct profit-sharing agreements with communities affected by mining operations, including the potential requirement for annual or periodic contributions to community trusts.

From a technical perspective, this legislative trajectory poses significant challenges for mining. The reduction of concession terms to 15-years is particularly problematic, echoing similar proposals from Mexico’s AMLO administration <https://www.btgpactual.com/research/report-view/93006>  in 2023, and disregards the protracted and capital-intensive nature of mineral exploration, resource delineation, and the subsequent transition to feasibility and bankability. 15-years is an inadequate timeframe to accommodate the full cycle of exploration, permitting, and development.

Additionally, the provision compelling mining companies to negotiate profit-sharing with local communities introduces substantial regulatory and operational complexity. Mining operations already generate fiscal contributions through existing tax and royalty structures; it is a core governmental function to administer these revenues and direct them toward social and infrastructure investment. The imposition of quasi-governmental responsibilities upon private operators – whether through mandated local allocations or compulsory profit-sharing – risks distorting the roles and expectations of both the state and the private sector. Given the lack of technical rigor underpinning these amendments, we assess that the measure is unlikely to achieve passage in Congress. In our view, its enactment would undermine the attractiveness of Peru’s mining sector, potentially setting an adverse precedent for parallel extractive industries such as O&G. 

We anticipate that the forthcoming bicameral Congress will subject the proposal to more robust scrutiny and predict that the current draft will be rejected – particularly as it appears to benefit informal miners. Politically, the issue is likely to feature prominently in the upcoming presidential campaigns, with candidates such as Lopez Aliaga – who has already advocated for shorter concession terms <https://www.btgpactual.com/research/report-view/115476>  – expected to leverage the debate as part of their electoral platforms.

emails.subject: ENC: Panvel (Buy): A healthy dose of momentum (BTG Pactual's Cesquim/Guanais/Cendon)
emails.one_liner: Panvel (PZIL3) Q4/25: SSS +14.7%, EBITDA +28%, Lucro +35%. Reiterada Compra, TP R$19,00, alavancagem 0.9x.
emails.body:
________________________________

De: BTG Pactual Equities Research <sh-btgpactual-research@btgpactual.com>Em Nome deYan Cesquim <yan.cesquim@btgpactual.com>
Enviado: quinta-feira, 19 de março de 2026 22:03:09 (UTC-03:00) Brasilia
Assunto: Panvel (Buy): A healthy dose of momentum (BTG Pactual's Cesquim/Guanais/Cendon)



BTG Pactual | Equity Research 


Company Note - Brazil - Retail 


Panvel (Buy Rating; TP: R$ 19.00) 


19-March-2026 


 


A healthy dose of momentum 


Read the full document here >> <https://www.btgpactual.com/research/report-view/118849>  

Solid SSS growth, boosted by GLP-1s and branded drugs

Panvel reported a solid set of Q4 results tonight, broadly in line with our estimates, showcasing strong SSS growth, solid operating performance (with another round of margin gains), and healthy cash flow generation. As already anticipated by the company during its Investor Day in February (see more in our report Dose of ambition: unveiling bold LT guidance <https://www.btgpactual.com/research/report-view/118251> ), retail sales grew 18% y/y to R$1.67bn (1% below our estimate), reflecting SSS growth of 14.7% y/y (vs. 9.1% in 3Q25 and our +13%) and MSSS growth of 11.6%. Productivity rose 13% y/y to R$849k/store/month, while market share in the South region increased 70bps y/y to 13.9%. As a result, consolidated gross revenues grew 16% y/y, below retail growth due to the phase-out of its wholesale division, reaching R$1.68bn. This was largely driven by the growth in branded drug sales, up 21% y/y (helped by solid GLP-1 momentum), followed by generics (+19% y/y), HPC (+16% y/y), and OTC (+12% y/y).

Adjusted EBITDA up 28%; net profit grew 35% y/y

Gross profit reached R$503mn, up 19% y/y (2% above us), with a margin of 29.8% (+50bps y/y), mostly explained by the discontinuation of lower-margin wholesale operations. Adjusting for R$21mn in profit sharing/bonuses provisions and R$2mn in one-offs, adjusted EBITDA (pre-IFRS16) reached R$105mn (in line), up 28% y/y, with a margin of 6.2% (+50bps), as SG&A remained virtually flat y/y as a percentage of net revenues. After accounting for financial results (-R$12mn vs. -R$7mn a year ago), net income (pre-IFRS16) came in at R$45mn (+35% y/y; vs. our R$50mn estimate, on slightly higher D&A).

A welcome FCF print; leverage down to 0.9x ND/EBITDA

On the back of lower capex (-31% y/y to R$34mn), better operating results, and a healthier cash cycle (down 9 days y/y, mostly due to improved inventory dynamics), FCF reached R$42mn (vs. -R$30mn a year ago). After accounting for interest payments, net debt stood at R$287mn (vs. R$311mn last quarter), with leverage declining to 0.9x ND/EBITDA (vs. 1.0x in 3Q25).

Resiliency + attractive valuation + momentum = BUY

We continue to see decent upside for Panvel as a resilient pharma player with solid momentum, and Q4 was another testament to that. Our positive view on the company is based on four pillars: (i) high store density in its core market, creating a natural barrier to entry and supporting continued expansion into other cities within the same region; (ii) room for margin gains, including a structurally stronger profitability profile following the divestment of the distribution business; (iii) strong private label penetration (+1.2k active SKUs), supported by a solid multichannel proposition (28% of retail sales via online channels); and (iv) an attractive valuation (12x P/E 2026). We reiterate our BUY.

De: BTG Pactual Equities Research <sh-btgpactual-research@btgpactual.com>Em Nome deCarlos Sequeira <carlos.sequeira@btgpactual.com>

BTG Pactual | Equity Research 


Brazil Strategy 


19-March-2026 


 


Dashboard Enhancements: Insider Activity & Cheat Sheet 


Read the full document here >> <https://www.btgpactual.com/research/report-view/118843>  

Access the dashboard here >>> <https://btg10sim.com/> 

Dear clients,

We are pleased to announce the addition of new features to our Strategy Dashboard <https://best.pactual.net/btg10sim.com> . The platform now includes buyback and insider activity data for all companies listed on B3. At the top of the page, you will find the top 10 companies active in buybacks, with the option to toggle between % of market cap and total financial amount (see screenshot below). Further down the page, you can filter the data by specific periods and select individual companies to explore their buyback activity in more detail. In the “Insiders” tab, you will find similar information on insider buying and selling for companies listed on B3. The platform updates automatically in real time as soon as a company files a new document with the CVM.

 

Screenshot of Buyback tab in our Strategy Dashboard

 <https://salesforce-mailing-distribution-prod.s3.sa-east-1.amazonaws.com/Mailing%20Distribution/assets/2026-03-19/1773963613213.png> 

We have also added a “Strategy Cheat Sheet” section, a comprehensive table designed to help investors quickly distill the key high-frequency data points driving markets. In this section, you can track movements across Brazilian and U.S. nominal and real interest rates, forward expectations for inflation, GDP, and FX, as well as key commodities and equity indices, among other indicators. The goal is to provide a centralized and intuitive snapshot of the macro landscape, helping investors monitor trends and identify potential inflection points more efficiently.

Screenshot of our 10SIM Dashboard 

 <https://salesforce-mailing-distribution-prod.s3.sa-east-1.amazonaws.com/Mailing%20Distribution/assets/2026-03-19/1773963613226.jpeg> 

We invite you to explore the platform and navigate through the available data. Our intention is to continue evolving the tool by incorporating additional features and analytical layers over time.

As always, your feedback and suggestions are very welcome. Our goal is to make this platform as useful as possible for you.

emails.subject: ENC: Bemobi (Buy): Another across-the-board beat; Upward revisions ahead (BTG Pactual's Carfi/Sequeira/Ferreira/Neder)
emails.one_liner: Bemobi (BEMOB3) entrega fortes resultados no 4T25, com 23% de crescimento FX-neutral e 50% em Payments; revisões para cima esperadas.
emails.body:
________________________________

De: BTG Pactual Equities Research <sh-btgpactual-research@btgpactual.com>Em Nome deOsni Carfi <osni.carfi@btgpactual.com>
Enviado: quinta-feira, 19 de março de 2026 21:04:54 (UTC-03:00) Brasilia
Assunto: Bemobi (Buy): Another across-the-board beat; Upward revisions ahead (BTG Pactual's Carfi/Sequeira/Ferreira/Neder)



BTG Pactual | Equity Research 


Company Note - Brazil - Telecom & Technology 


Bemobi (Buy Rating; TP: R$ 35.00) 


19-March-2026 


 


Another across-the-board beat; Upward revisions ahead 


Read the full document here >> <https://www.btgpactual.com/research/report-view/118847>  

Impressive 23% y/y FX-neutral growth

Bemobi delivered another strong set of results. Net revenue reached R$199.2mn, up 20.5% y/y (or 23% FX-neutral) and 6% q/q, coming 4% above our estimate. The strong top-line performance was primarily driven by Payments (+50% y/y, +17% q/q; 8% above BTGe) and SaaS (+23% y/y, +8% q/q; 2% above BTGe). Microfinance revenues totaled R$20.2mn, up 6% y/y, supported by solid momentum in credit scoring, while Subscription revenues declined 7% y/y (or were roughly flat FX-neutral), coming in better than feared. 4Q marks the seventh consecutive quarter of double-digit y/y growth and the fifth with high-teens to low-twenties top-line expansion. Profitability also remained strong, with EBITDA margin at 33.1%, broadly stable y/y. Adjusted EBITDA reached R$65.9mn (+20% y/y), 4% above our estimate. Finally, adjusted net income (excluding mark-to-market effects from swap contracts) totaled R$61mn, up 31% y/y and well above our R$45mn estimate, mainly driven by lower taxes.

Payments +50% y/y and +17% q/q; Telcel onboarded

The 50% y/y growth in the Payments division was driven by TPV growth of 36% y/y and a take-rate expansion from 2.5% in 4Q24 to 2.8% in 4Q25. The main highlights in the quarter were the Education segment and Sabesp. We estimate that Education and Utilities already account for nearly 40% of total TPV (R$3.1bn), with Telecom representing the remainder. In revenue terms, however, the mix is more balanced, given the higher take-rates in these segments. Bemobi also announced a new flagship client in 4Q25: Telcel, the leading telecom operator in Mexico and the company’s first client in the country. Unlike Brazil, Bemobi does not have exclusivity in Telcel’s channels and will initially operate in only one channel. That said, the opportunity is significant, as Telcel has ~85mn subscribers, of which ~69mn are prepaid.

Growth, profitability and shareholder return. A rare mix breed in tech

Bemobi combines double-digit growth, profitability, and shareholder return — a rare mix in Brazil’s tech landscape. On a relative basis, it also looks inexpensive under every angle (at just 11x 2026E earnings). Today’s results reinforce our bullishness on the name.

emails.one_liner: TUPY 4Q25 resultados piores: R$627mn prejuízo líquido, 1.8% margem EBITDA ajustada.
emails.body:
________________________________

De: Marcelo Motta - JPM <marcelo.g.motta@jpmresearchmail.com>

Para: Sell Side <sellside@torkcapital.com.br>
Assunto: TUPY: 4Q25 Results: Much Worse Than Feared with Net Loss at R$627mn and Adj. EBITDA Margin at 1.8%


 	Latin America Equity Research 	

Hi Tork,

Extel/II survey is reaching its LAST 24H, so we kindly ask for your recognition with a 5-STAR vote in LATIN AMERICA REAL ESTATE, CAPITAL GOODS and CEMENT & CONSTRUCTION.

Best,
Marcelo Motta & Jonathan Koutras



TUPY


4Q25 Results: Much Worse Than Feared with Net Loss at R$627mn and Adj. EBITDA Margin at 1.8%

 	 
 <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12827815&ad=4067988175&h=bZBR4X0&sig=BTi7roe8JiOL5wFPu36gfZy3JvF&v=2&url=https://www.extelinsights.com/voting> 	
By Marcelo Motta AC <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12827815&ad=4067988175&h=E0Mm9Ge&sig=WahSXwG6uAKOxjpyDG67Q2Ztruk&v=2&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/analyst/E194704> , Jonathan S. Koutras <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12827815&ad=4067988175&h=9vDGKvq&sig=1KCtQnxAFK7bFn1pK2ffk9ZbnaE&v=2&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/analyst/V737771>  

Click here for the full document and disclaimers <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12827815&ad=4067988175&h=7UJbafe&sig=UTa2TGXTGniCgpw4WYHY7XIugv4&v=2&isig=Aq4YubLd0qZYDUAFHq8maQQuwUC&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/email/scx/gc5huqm9/GPS-5236637-0/74756a98-1050-48a2-ab68-29c2f5815de0> 

Underweight	 
TUPY3.SA, TUPY3 BZ	 
Price (19 Mar 26):R$11.50	 
	

Tupy released its 4Q25 results (link here <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12827815&ad=4067988175&h=8I2Ua9k&sig=Uv7gm9kxXLn6WSVPJgy5k3cagT7&v=2&url=https://www.rad.cvm.gov.br/ENET/frmExibirArquivoIPEExterno.aspx?NumeroProtocoloEntrega%3D1492682> ) on Mar. 19th after market close. It was another quarter of dismal results as adj. EBITDA missed JPMe and consensus by 63% and net income was negative at R$627mn vs expectations of a loss of ~R$50mn. Excluding ~R$545mn in one-offs (impairments, restructuring costs and others), net income would have been -R$80mn, still worse than expected. Weakness in heavy vehicle volumes in the automotive segment (sales -17% yoy) and an impairment of R$325mn were the main culprits for the lackluster quarter. We see TUPY trading at 4.3x EV/EBITDA 26e vs POMO at 4.5x and RAPT at 4.6x. 

*	Our take. Despite the stock’s underperformance at -6% YTD vs IBOV at +12%, we expect a negative reaction in tomorrow’s trading session given the higher than expected loss of R$627mn. Moreover, the stock’s RSI remains at ~36, above the 30 threshold deemed as oversold, and there is limited visibility on earnings improvements (i.e. NA Class 8 truck build rates down ~35% YTD). 
*	Main positives. i) Net revenues were at R$2.18bn, -1% vs JPMe but in-line with consensus; ii) SG&A was R$228mn, -4% vs JPMe and representing 10.4% of revenues, -0.4pp vs our call and -0.6pp yoy; and iii) Operational FCF was positive despite the weak bottom line at R$358mn (-40% yoy) with working capital down 8 days qoq and down 12 days yoy, mostly driven by a reduction in inventories (we need to understand how sustainable this is). 
*	Main negatives. i) Gross margin was at 8.0%, -8.9pp yoy and -3.9pp vs JPMe; iii) Adj. EBITDA margin was at 1.8%, -3.0pp vs JPMe and -8.3pp yoy, also being 4.0pp below implied consensus of 4.9%; iv) Net financial cost was at -R$92mn, 46% worse than our call; and v) Income tax was at -R$168mn vs -R$23mn a year ago, impacted by tough comps given a tax recovery that happened a year ago. 
*	Revenue breakdown. Domestic market represented 37% of 4Q top-line, and the foreign market the remaining 63%. In terms of segment breakdown: i) Automotive segment revenues (80% of top-line) were at R$1.75bn (-17% yoy) and flat vs. JPMe; ii) Energy & Distribution revenues (10% of top-line) were R$219mn (+23% yoy), +1% vs. JPMe; and iii) Distribution revenues (10% of top-line) were at R$215mn (-6% yoy), +6% vs JPMe. 
*	Other highlights: i) The Adjusted EBITDA margin of the traditional business, which includes structural components and hydraulic products, was at -1% vs MWM at +10%; ii) The appreciation of the BRL and MX peso against the USD had a negative impact of R$70mn; iii) Lower production levels also affected quality indicators, resulting in negative impact of R$57mn in 4Q; iv) Capex was at R$177mn, +27% vs JPMe and +1% yoy; and v) During 4Q, Tupy sold IPI future tax credits in the amount of R$174mn (tax benefit related to exports carried out between 1988 and 1990). 
*	Conference call details. Friday, Mar 20th at 10am ET / 11am BZ – registration here <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12827815&ad=4067988175&h=rjuFc57&sig=NiHmXFBoZmlRSTBvHOlXG2wB9Ji&v=2&url=https://mzgroup.zoom.us/webinar/register/WN_nyoCIh1x

emails.subject: ENC: IDEA: Alibaba Group Holding: Multi-year Alicloud Growth Acceleration
emails.one_liner: Morgan Stanley vê Alicloud BABA acelerar para 40% no 4T26 e 45% no AF27, com PT US$180. Mantém Top Pick.
emails.body:
________________________________

De: Gary Yu <Gary.Yu@morganstanley.com>
Enviado: quinta-feira, 19 de março de 2026 20:23:09 (UTC-03:00) Brasilia
Para: Sell Side <sellside@torkcapital.com.br>
Assunto: IDEA: Alibaba Group Holding: Multi-year Alicloud Growth Acceleration



	 
	 
	 
Alibaba Group Holding (BABA.N)   	
Multi-year Alicloud Growth Acceleration	 
Read Full Report <https://ny.matrix.ms.com/eqr/article/webapp/7719df82-23b0-11f1-99ea-46f6fece5bca?t=1773962551:5406:11968:acba1c0882e9&m=1&ch=CDF Research ISG Autoblast#e=c2VsbHNpZGVAdG9ya2NhcGl0YWwuY29tLmJy> 	 
(PDF: 14 Pages) 	
Asia Pacific 	
Stock Rating   Overweight
Price Target   US$180.00

Gary Yu, Joanne Lau, Eddy Wang, CFA	 
March 19, 2026

	
We see cloud growth accelerating to 40% in 4Q26 and 45% in F27. CMR should improve to 6% as consumption shows signs of better recovery. EBITA should continue to drag in the near term, but cloud margin upside and narrowing QC losses point to improving profitability in F27. Maintain Top Pick. 

Alicloud to accelerate: Alicloud F3Q revenue growth rose to 36%, in line with MSe. We expect the growth trajectory to be robust in the next few quarters due to token surge (6x in Mar vs. Dec), Alicloud price hikes, <https://ny.matrix.ms.com/eqr/article/webapp/6506246c-228a-11f1-99ea-46f6fece5bca?ch=rp&sch=cr> and rising adoption in 2B and 2C agentic use cases. Management sees Alicloud external revenue of US$100bn in five years (40%+ CAGR). Prior to the results, MSe is already Street-high for cloud, and we are now more confident that BABA can post 40% cloud growth for 4Q26 and 45% for FY27. As management still prioritizes adoption over profitability, we expect margins to stay largely stable followed by upside from F27 onward, with potential to reach 20% (bull case) in the medium to long term.

T-head: Management touched upon T-head for the first time during results. It indicated >60% of revenue is from external cloud customers, and has delivered >470k units. Management did not rule out a potential spin-off opportunity, which could further unlock SOTP value <https://ny.matrix.ms.com/eqr/article/webapp/329f658c-fb6c-11f0-8bb0-3a84fbe785bc?ch=rp&sch=cr> , but no time-line was noted.

Recovering CMR trends: Management attributed the soft CMR in 3Q to a weak macro environment and a late CNY. We expect CMR to inflect, rising 6% in 4Q, with core EBITA growth improving due to strong industry growth of+10% <https://ny.matrix.ms.com/eqr/article/webapp/6cc3d734-20e2-11f1-99ea-46f6fece5bca?ch=rp&sch=ar>  in January and February. We expect total E-comm EBITA of Rmb22bn (-45% YoY).

QC loss to narrow QoQ: We estimate the QC loss was Rmb22bn in 3Q (vs. 2Q estimated at Rmb35bn). Management highlighted synergies with e-comm, noting that Taobao Annual Active Consumers (AAC) increased by 150mn. Management reinforces its Rmb1tn GMV target by F28, and expects to turn profitable by F29. Based on improving UE, we expect the loss to narrow further to Rmb19bn in 4Q.

Investments to weigh on EBITA: We expect a continuous drag on EBITA from QC losses and increasing All Others losses (mainly Qwen model training and Qwen App expenses). We expect F4Q total adj EBITA of Rmb10bn (margin 4.1%).

Maintain DCF-based PT at US$180: Our revenue estimates are unchanged, but we trim adj EBITA estimates by 14% for F26 and 4% for F27 on Qwen and QC investments. We remain bullish on the Alicloud thesis with improving CMR trends, and expect profitability to gradually improve into F27. Our DCF-based PT implies 18x F28e EPS (assuming normalized QC loss level), vs. Tencent 18x F26. Our SOTP valuation <https://ny.matrix.ms.com/eqr/article/webapp/5f63dcc4-1e17-11f1-881a-897d542fee46?ch=rp&sch=cr> remains US$245. Read more... <https://ny.matrix.ms.com/eqr/article/webapp/7719df82-23b0-11f1-99ea-46f6fece5bca?t=1773962551:5406:11968:acba1c0882e9&m=1&ch=CDF Research ISG Autoblast#e=c2VsbHNpZGVAdG9ya2NhcGl0YWwuY29tLmJy>

emails.subject: ENC: ESG Research: Brazil's truck fleet renewal: a new attempt to reduce emissions (BTG Pactual's Marquiori/Recchia/Alkmim/Faber/Dortas)
emails.one_liner: Brasil's Move Brasil renova frota de caminhoes com R$5bn de R$10bn financiados para reduzir emissoes.
emails.body:
________________________________

De: BTG Pactual Equities Research <sh-btgpactual-research@btgpactual.com>Em Nome deLucas Marquiori <lucas.marquiori@btgpactual.com>
Enviado: quinta-feira, 19 de março de 2026 20:12:11 (UTC-03:00) Brasilia
Assunto: ESG Research: Brazil's truck fleet renewal: a new attempt to reduce emissions (BTG Pactual's Marquiori/Recchia/Alkmim/Faber/Dortas)



BTG Pactual | Equity Research 


Sector Note - Brazil - Car Rental & Logistics 


ESG Research 


19-March-2026 


 


Brazil's truck fleet renewal: a new attempt to reduce emissions 


Read the full document here >> <https://www.btgpactual.com/research/report-view/118834>  

Truck fleet renewal in Brazil: a new policy to address an old challenge

In 2024, Brazil’s truck fleet totaled about 2.3 million vehicles, with an average age of slightly more than 12 years. The large share of aging trucks in Brazil increases carbon emissions, reduces productivity, and negatively affects road safety. This report focuses on the environmental impact of Brazil’s large and aging truck fleet, as well as on a new program designed to accelerate fleet renewal.

Move Brasil: a program to renew and modernize Brazil’s truck fleet

The Move Brasil program is a nationwide initiative aimed at renewing and modernizing Brazil’s truck fleet in order to improve road safety, reduce emissions, and increase the efficiency of road freight transport. The program supports the replacement of older trucks through R$10 billion in financing funded by the National Treasury and the Brazilian Development Bank (BNDES). The financing covers new trucks produced in Brazil and used trucks manufactured from 2012 onward. When financing the purchase of a new truck, the program requires that an older vehicle be removed from circulation and dismantled. In the first two months of the project, 4,620 credit operations have already been approved, totaling R$5bn in financing.

Fleet renewal helps, but structural challenges remain

Addressing emissions from freight transport in Brazil may require a broader strategy beyond fleet renewal. While replacing older trucks with more efficient models can reduce emissions per vehicle, the overall environmental impact of the sector also depends on the structure of the transport system. Policies that encourage greater use of rail and water transport, combined with improvements in intermodal logistics and the increasing use of biofuels, could play an important role in achieving more substantial and long-term reductions in emissions.

By Marcelo Motta AC <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12827673&ad=4067901420&h=E0Mm9Ge&sig=K4Jy4DKW6FQ5SlUtooRR132nJiL&v=2&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/analyst/E194704> , Jonathan S. Koutras <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12827673&ad=4067901420&h=9vDGKvq&sig=G4iwbek4ODAjhJRXTLgXNuqAKIH&v=2&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/analyst/V737771> , Adrian E Huerta <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12827673&ad=4067901420&h=EUeO2BD&sig=pBbxbHwZcxXLdYKTxaMWqYxJLY&v=2&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/analyst/U034572> , Garrett Greenblatt <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12827673&ad=4067901420&h=1uhSRjQ&sig=8mcMdHLQFKDjEDQSJq7Kr253bQD&v=2&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/analyst/V849374>  

Click here for the full document and disclaimers <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12827673&ad=4067901420&h=MGtGoma&sig=ZvZXxdoDgmHZxzUKKBC5pxy1psY&v=2&isig=DOEVTqRwaiUCHnIRq4kpVQf7o7y&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/email/scx/-nsqqhre/GPS-5236628-0/d2da104a-5d51-4b3b-ba85-e9d85056477b> 

Overweight	 
CYRE3.SA, CYRE3 BZ	 
Price (19 Mar 26):R$27.11	 
	

Cyrela reported 4Q25 results (link here <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12827673&ad=4067901420&h=SjLBUaX&sig=Rt9zX06m6chxzIdpH0p3t9GbtvB&v=2&url=https://www.rad.cvm.gov.br/ENET/frmExibirArquivoIPEExterno.aspx?NumeroProtocoloEntrega%3D1492614> ) on Mar. 19th after the market close. The company had a strong quarter with top-line beating consensus expectations by 16% at R$3.2bn (+29% yoy) leading to a net income of R$682mn, +17% vs consensus, at a record high. Cyrela’s stake in Cury ended the quarter at ~15.0% vs ~15.9% in 3Q due to Cury’s YE follow-on offering. We see CYRE trading at 6.3x P/E 26e and 1.2x P/BV vs. EZTC at 7.3x/0.76x. 

*	Our take. We expect a positive reaction in tomorrow’s trading session given the strong bottom line being 17% above consensus (even without any CURY divestment), driven by solid top-line growth and implying an annualized ROE of 21.1%, +1.2pp yoy and +7.5pp vs JPMe. Moreover, the stock is down 14% in the past month vs IBOV at -4%. 
*	Main positives. i) Top-line was at R$3.2bn, +29% yoy and +41% vs JPMe / +16% vs consensus; ii)  SG&A / Revenues was at 12.8%, +0.4pp yoy, but 2.7pp below JPMe; iii) Net Financial Results were positive at R$66mn, 20% better than JPMe and compared with R$16mn a year ago; iv) Taxes were positive in R$2mn vs our call of -R$54mn (we look for color during tomorrow’s call); and v) Net income was at R$682mn, +37% yoy and implying a net margin of 21.1%, +1.2pp yoy and +0.6pp vs JPMe and consensus. 
*	Main negatives. i) Adj. gross margin was at 33.7%, +0.4pp yoy but -1.7pp vs our expectations, being impacted by sales of the studios and store of Vista Milano project (~1.8pp impact, implying recurring reported gross margin at 34.0%); ii) Minority payout was substantial at 18.8% vs our call of 15.4% and 15.6% a year ago; and iii) CYRE had an operational cash burn of R$38mn in the quarter vs generation of R$172mn in 3Q and R$61mn a year ago. 
*	Operational figures. As previously released, net presales (% CYRE) in 4Q were at R$2.50bn (-31% yoy) with concluded inventory sales at R$195mn (100% stake) vs R$185mn in 3Q25 and R$216mn year ago. Launches (% CYRE) were at ~R$3.44bn (-31% yoy), with the low-income segment representing ~35% of 4Q launches. Quarterly consolidated sales speed was at 17.2% (-14.2pp yoy due to tough comps) and inventory sales speed was at 10.8%, -1.7pp yoy and -0.2pp qoq, marking the worst figure since 1Q22. Inventories at market value totaled R$12.0bn (% CYRE), representing ~15 months of sales. 
*	Other highlights. i) Landbank totaled R$19.8bn (%CYRE at R$18.6bn), with São Paulo amounting to R$12.2bn, Rio de Janeiro R$5.9bn, the South Region R$1.3bn and others at R$0.4bn; ii) CYRE delivered 20 projects during 4Q25 (vs. 15 in 3Q25 and 16 in 4Q24), with a PSV of R$2.9bn (100%) and 7.0k units; iii) Net revenues to be recognized stood at R$11.2

emails.subject: ENC: Tupy (Neutral): No signs of recovery in Q4 (BTG Pactual's Marquiori/Recchia/Alkmim)
emails.one_liner: Tupy Q4 fraco: EBITDA ajustado R$39mn (-85% a/a), prejuízo R$93mn. Expectativa de cenário desafiador. TP R$35.00.
emails.body:
________________________________

De: BTG Pactual Equities Research <sh-btgpactual-research@btgpactual.com>Em Nome deLucas Marquiori <lucas.marquiori@btgpactual.com>
Enviado: quinta-feira, 19 de março de 2026 19:57:09 (UTC-03:00) Brasilia
Assunto: Tupy (Neutral): No signs of recovery in Q4 (BTG Pactual's Marquiori/Recchia/Alkmim)



BTG Pactual | Equity Research 


Company Note - Brazil - Capital Goods 


Tupy (Neutral Rating; TP: R$ 35.00) 


19-March-2026 


 


No signs of recovery in Q4 


Read the full document here >> <https://www.btgpactual.com/research/report-view/118845>  

The bar was already low, but Q4 results were weaker

We were expecting a weak set of Q4 results for Tupy, but the company reported an even weaker print, mostly due to misses on both topline and margins. Accordingly, net revenue clocked in at R$2.2bn (-12% y/y; 6% below our estimate), while reported EBITDA reached -R$273mn. This was primarily due to R$312mn of non-recurring items, mostly related to (i) a R$325mn impairment in the period; (ii) R$72mn in restructuring costs and (iii) R$40mn regarding the reduction to realizable value of inventories. On the other hand, we note that these negative impacts were partially offset by a positive impact of R$174mn related to the sale of IPI tax credit claims through the assignment of rights. Adjusting for these effects, adj. EBITDA came in at R$39mn (-85% y/y, 55% below our estimate), implying a weak 2% margin (down 8.3p.p. y/y, 2.0p.p. below our estimate). Lastly, we estimate a adj. net loss of R$93mn (vs. BTG's -R$154mn estimate) for the period. 

Weaker volumes across the board, especially in domestic structural components 

Revenue in the Structural Components & Manufacturing Contracts division fell 17% y/y, with domestic revenue down 40% y/y (impacted mainly by weak heavy vehicle sales amid high interest rates and customers’ indirect exports) and external revenue down 5% y/y (due to lower sales of commercial vehicles and the uncertain environment related to tariffs). The Energy & Decarbonization segment posted a 23% y/y increase in revenue, with domestic revenue up 36% y/y (driven by new businesses and revenues from proprietary engines) and external revenue down 28% y/y. Lastly, revenue in the Distribution division increased 6% y/y (domestic up 13% y/y and external down 17% y/y). All in, domestic revenue fell 22% y/y to R$799mn, while external revenue declined 6% y/y to R$1.4bn.

ST story remains challenging and we prefer to stay on the sidelines; reiterate Neutral

Capex totaled R$177mn (vs. R$93mn in Q3), primarily related to new foundry and machining programs, initiatives aimed at improving operational efficiency, and synergies between operations, in addition to investments in health, safety, and environmental standards. Additionally, leverage increased q/q to 3.4x Net Debt/Adjusted EBITDA (from 2.6x in Q3), reflecting the weaker operating trend. Looking ahead, we expect investors to monitor (i) the impacts of lower tariffs on volumes in external markets, (ii) domestic automotive production trends, (iii) the ramp-up of manufacturing contracts, and (iv) the leverage and cash generation outlook. All in, we believe earnings momentum is likely to remain sluggish in the coming months, and until we gain greater visibility on earnings, we prefer to remain Neutral on the name.

emails.subject: ENC: Wiz (Neutral): Slower top line; stronger recurring margins (BTG Pactual's Buchpiguel/Rosman/Paura)
emails.one_liner: Wiz (WIZC3) reporta 4T25 com lucro ajustado 7% acima; mantemos Neutro, TP R$11,00.
emails.body:
________________________________

De: BTG Pactual Equities Research <sh-btgpactual-research@btgpactual.com>Em Nome deRicardo Buchpiguel <ricardo.buchpiguel@btgpactual.com>
Enviado: quinta-feira, 19 de março de 2026 19:45:02 (UTC-03:00) Brasilia
Assunto: Wiz (Neutral): Slower top line; stronger recurring margins (BTG Pactual's Buchpiguel/Rosman/Paura)



BTG Pactual | Equity Research 


Company Note - Brazil - Financials 


Wiz (Neutral Rating; TP: R$ 11.00) 


19-March-2026 


Slower top line; stronger recurring margins 


Read the full document here >> <https://www.btgpactual.com/research/report-view/118846>  


Healthy recurring net profit; dividend payout increased to 50%


Wiz reported 4Q25 results, with GAAP net income coming in 14% above our estimate at R$49mn (-10.5% q/q; +29% y/y). While the headline print came below our expectations, it is important to highlight a couple of points: (i) there was a ~R$10mn impairment impact with no cash effect; (ii) the sale of Wiz Concept at the end of October impacted the bottom line by ~R$1.8mn, which we had not accounted for in our estimates. Adjusting for these effects, the bottom line was actually ~7% above our estimate at ~R$60mn. Despite slower top-line growth, margins benefited mainly from lower financial expenses. Wiz generated almost R$220mn in cash flow in 2025 (15% FCF), and with net debt/EBITDA now below 0.5x and debt duration extended, the company decided to increase the payout ratio from 25% to 50%, in line with our model.


Weaker top-line growth; R$10mn impairment impacted GAAP EPS


Net revenue declined 5% q/q (+6% y/y; 5% below our estimate), impacted by the sale of Wiz Concept and lower revenues from BMG Broker, BRB Broker, and Promotiva. On the other hand, we saw strong performance from Wiz Corporate, where revenues increased 32% q/q and 16% y/y. We also highlight the improvement in equity income (+18% q/q; +12% y/y), mainly driven by results from Inter Seguros. Costs and expenses rose 12% q/q (+11% y/y; 13% above our estimate), mainly impacted by a one-off impairment of R$10mn, largely related to Wiz Corporate, whose goodwill had not been revised for nearly 12 years. The company also made progress in improving efficiency, with headcount (ex-Wiz Concept) down 4% q/q to 746. Finally, financial expenses declined ~52% q/q to R$7mn (-72% y/y).


Stock down 10% since our downgrade; bias improved, but still Neutral


Last month, we decided to downgrade Wiz following its Investor Day (link <https://www.btgpactual.com/research/report-view/117769> ). The rationale was straightforward: the stock had risen nearly 80% over the previous 12 months, and although we remained constructive on the story, the combination of a more demanding valuation and doubts around BRB Seguros’ growth going forward (~25% of EBITDA) led us to take profits. Since then, the stock has declined 10%, mainly pressured by the global sell-off observed amid the conflict in the Middle East. That said, trading at 6.1x 2026 P/E, our bias has improved a bit, but we remain Neutral, mainly until we have greater clarity on what to expect from BRB Seguros going forward.

emails.subject: ENC: Wiz: 4Q25 - Earnings Miss with Softer Revenues and Impairment
emails.one_liner: WIZC3 missed 4Q25 earnings (R$50mn, -7% JPMe) due to softer revenues (BMG -21%, Promotiva -22%) and R$9mn impairment.
emails.body:
________________________________

De: Guilherme Grespan <guilherme.grespan@jpmresearchmail.com>
Enviado: quinta-feira, 19 de março de 2026 19:41:14 (UTC-03:00) Brasilia
Para: Sell Side <sellside@torkcapital.com.br>
Assunto: Wiz: 4Q25 - Earnings Miss with Softer Revenues and Impairment


 	Latin America Equity Research 	

Wiz


4Q25 - Earnings Miss with Softer Revenues and Impairment

 	 
 <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12827637&ad=4067884743&h=bZBR4X0&sig=Y4a18KDNUepKwfzS9bOyFcgslqS&v=2&url=https://www.extelinsights.com/voting> 	
By Guilherme Grespan AC <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12827637&ad=4067884743&h=OGu-s5p&sig=LaEaDo4FeKAFh3H1u2znqc594m1&v=2&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/analyst/R578266> , Yuri R Fernandes <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12827637&ad=4067884743&h=BhNZ9Ro&sig=4Pm4kXJTb5V3wmq6g6BJWbc0ygW&v=2&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/analyst/E689071> , Fernanda Sayao <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12827637&ad=4067884743&h=sCLP3vv&sig=YdGGpd4TqIPnbUNdzRoAHBcWxbh&v=2&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/analyst/I807027> , Diego Marquez Antonio <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12827637&ad=4067884743&h=PmeoAkb&sig=N74UcoUl7LYorF94ALxQWoEKefj&v=2&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/analyst/O817967>  

Click here for the full document and disclaimers <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12827637&ad=4067884743&h=PVEcOZJ&sig=JYZorUxNRQrAGvJAhBbhKSnmG7o&v=2&isig=3Xfb4QN6oS7udZJHjS4CBxjl69G&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/email/scx/q2jtdmd4/GPS-5236490-0/7503e67e-e7d5-47b3-b8c5-49ab4e8b92ca> 

Neutral	 
WIZC3.SA, WIZC3 BZ	 
Price (19 Mar 26):R$8.88	 
	

Wiz reported earnings of R$50mn, up 28% Y-o-Y and ~7% below JPMe. Our models did not incorporate the sale of Wiz Concept (link <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12827637&ad=4067884743&h=nnVQsiQ&sig=1U7u0ZAfTElmvlEcoTNtcoy0sV3&v=2&url=https://api.mziq.com/mzfilemanager/v2/d/0b486057-7845-4b55-ace2-4b4842d128c1/496ba56c-3001-0287-1f79-b47caf3fae02?origin%3D2> ) hence not fully comparable but we note ~R$1.6mn equity income contribution from the division not much different from 3Q25. In particular, we note softer top line this quarter at BMG broker (-21% Y-o-Y) impacted by lower sales across credit life (-11% Y-o-Y), life (-57% Y-o-Y) and corporate (+15% Y-o-Y but -21% Y-o-Y). Promotiva was also lackluster with gross revenues down by -22% Y-o-Y but we recap the vertical carries high variable commission costs hence net revenues weren’t as pressured, down a more modest -3% Y-o-Y. On the brighter side, Wiz Corporate had a record quarter with R$46mn in revenues . Despite the good top line performance, we also saw ~R$9mn impairment at the division which pressured consolidated D&A. On costs, COSR and G&A were mostly in line (up +25% and -4% Y-o-Y, respectively), but R$2.6mn in net operating expenses drove EBITDA to miss JPMe by 9% at R$165mn. Equity income from Inter was strong this quarter (R$12mn vs R$9mn in 3Q25), preventing a larger EPS miss. On a separate note, Wiz announced this week CFO Lucas is assuming the role of CEO (link <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12827637&ad=4067884743&h=zSoqCO7&sig=OC7UgXZx3bgTnueNflVNg4upnAn&v=2&url=https://api.mziq.com/mzfilemanager/v2/d/0b486057-7845-4b55-ace2-4b4842d128c1/8484543e-9dfc-bc1f-832d-eebea573b3ad?origin%3D2> ). All in, a soft quarter. We see Wiz trading at 6.1x 26E P/E. 

*	Softer revenues from BMG, BRB and Promotiva. Gross revenues totaled R$472mn, down -8% Y-o-Y and -10% below JPMe. It is worth noting our model was not adjusted to reflect the sale of Wiz Concept in October — adjusted for that, revenues would have missed our estimates by 7%. Elsewhere, we highlight: (i) Promotiva -14% below our estimates with lower sales on credit letters (-5% Y-o-Y) and credit for individuals (-28%) -  but commissions were also lower, hence net revenues were not as negative; (ii) BMG -22% below JPMe with lackluster life and credit life; (iii) BRB -7% below JPMe with also weakness across most products. 
*	Mostly in line core expenses. Expenses ex-D&A came in at R$111mn, up 17% Y-o-Y and 4% above our estimates, largely explained by “others” (release mentioned dividend distribution from Omni impacting the line). Meanwhile, cost of services and G&A came in a touch above our estimates. The company continued to cut down on headcount by -15 employees this quarter (-80 LTM) – Wiz Concept sale reduced headcount by an additional 524. All in, EBITDA totaled R$165mn, missing JPMe by 9%. EBITDA margin (JPM criteria) reached ~60%, down vs. ~64% last quarter. 
*	Impairment costs adding to the earnings miss. Equity income (basically Inter) came in at R$11.6mn, up 12% Y-o-Y (+17% Q-o-Q), but coming -2% below JPMe. Specifically for Inter, premiums reached a record R$116mn, although number of sales a

emails.subject: ENC: Utilities Portfolio: Taking profits on the auction-related relative trades; Orizon vs. Cemig in (BTG Pactual's Junqueira/Gushiken/Schutz)
emails.one_liner: BTG encerra Copel vs Cemig (+8%) e Eneva vs BOVA11 (+26.1%); inicia Orizon long, Cemig short após sell-off.
emails.body:
________________________________

De: BTG Pactual Equities Research <sh-btgpactual-research@btgpactual.com>Em Nome deAntonio Junqueira <antonio.junqueira@btgpactual.com>
Enviado: quinta-feira, 19 de março de 2026 19:38:16 (UTC-03:00) Brasilia
Assunto: Utilities Portfolio: Taking profits on the auction-related relative trades; Orizon vs. Cemig in (BTG Pactual's Junqueira/Gushiken/Schutz)



BTG Pactual | Equity Research 


Sector Note - Brazil - Utilities 


Utilities Portfolio 


19-March-2026 


 


Taking profits on the auction-related relative trades; Orizon vs. Cemig in 


Read the full document here >> <https://www.btgpactual.com/research/report-view/118840>  

Closing two calls

We’re closing two of our current relative ideas: Copel vs. Cemig (up 8% since inception) and Eneva vs. BOVA11 (up 26.1% since inception). One of the reasons for these ideas was to gain exposure to this week’s Capacity Auction. While we see potential upside in both long stories mentioned (as we believe markets haven't fully priced in all the value created), the recent move has been quite positive, and we’ve decided to close these positions. As a reminder, this L/S Utilities Portfolio is short-term focused and seeks short-term triggers and asymmetries. We believe both Eneva and Copel are long-term winning growth stories with further upside.

New idea in

After the significant MTD sell-off, and despite positive newsflow in recent months (including a strong win in Wednesday’s auction), we recommend investors go long on Orizon. We believe that eventually, market conditions will stabilize, and the stock will regain its positive momentum. Operationally, the company is performing well. We would consider Cemig as a funding option.

Two ideas open

Our L/S portfolio currently holds two ideas: (i) Equatorial x BOVA (down 12.6%) and (ii) Orizon x Cemig (new). Considering the poor performance of the Equatorial relative idea so far, we clearly got it wrong (some of the Ibovespa components significantly outperformed the power distributor). That said, if we didn’t have the idea, we would be considering it now, so we’re choosing to keep it there.

emails.subject: ENC: LOG (Buy): Takeaways from meeting with CFO (BTG Pactual's Cambauva/Fabris/Pascale)
emails.one_liner: LOG (Buy, TP R$35.00) CFO meeting highlights strong logistics momentum, 13% real IRR, and 2mn sqm target.
emails.body:
________________________________

De: BTG Pactual Equities Research <sh-btgpactual-research@btgpactual.com>Em Nome deGustavo Cambauva <gustavo.cambauva@btgpactual.com>
Enviado: quinta-feira, 19 de março de 2026 19:38:08 (UTC-03:00) Brasilia
Assunto: LOG (Buy): Takeaways from meeting with CFO (BTG Pactual's Cambauva/Fabris/Pascale)



BTG Pactual | Equity Research 


Company Note - Brazil - Real Estate 


LOG (Buy Rating; TP: R$ 35.00) 


19-March-2026 


Takeaways from meeting with CFO 


Read the full document here >> <https://www.btgpactual.com/research/report-view/118797>  


Sector tailwinds, growth and dividends; Buy rated (13% real IRR)


We hosted a meeting with LOG's CFO (Rafael Saliba) and IRM (Henrique Schuffner) to discuss the company's outlook. Highlights: (i) momentum in the logistics segment is very strong; (ii) LOG is developing with 13%-14% YoC; (iii) they are optimistic on delivering their "LOG 2mn" plan; (iv) LOG is expanding its service platform (LOG ADM); and (v) the outlook for M&As is promising. We revisited LOG's investment thesis (see Growing with attractive returns; Reiterating Buy <https://www.btgpactual.com/research/report-view/118160> ) and maintain our Buy rating on the stock, due to: (i) a favorable sector outlook (rents growing a lot); (ii) value-accretive M&As; and (iii) attractive valuation (13% real IRR and 6.5x P/E 2026E).


Demand for logistics is strong across regions


Management highlighted a highly favorable backdrop for the logistics segment nationwide, with robust demand for AAA logistic assets (driven by e-commerce) and low vacancy rates supporting LOG’s strong real rental growth. In their view, this dynamic is structural rather than cyclical, reinforcing a positive long-term outlook. On a geographic basis, LOG sees opportunities outside SP/Rio as more attractive since it has strong know-how in operating in these areas and competition is a lot lower.


LOG is on track to fulfill its 2mn sqm delivery target, with YoC of 13-14%


Given the positive sector momentum and the strength of the company’s portfolio, the CFO stated they are confident in delivering their plan of 2mn m² of GLA by 2028YE ("LOG 2mn"). He reinforced that the company has the operational capacity to develop 500–600k m² per year (in line with what is required to meet the target) and added that recent projects have been delivered with a 13-14% yield on cost (above the 12% target) and high pre-leasing levels (~90% in the latest developments).


Solid outlook to continue selling assets at value-accretive terms


LOG remains constructive on the asset recycling outlook (R$1.8bn sold in the LTM), with M&As remaining key to the thesis, both to fund LOG's expansion plan and to allow more dividends. These transactions are accretive, as assets are sold at ~9% cap rates while being developed at ~13% YoC. They also highlighted their services platform, an asset-light business with high growth that should support deleveraging and enhance returns. Overall, management believes LOG is well positioned to continue generating value, combining NAV growth with dividend distribution.

Gustavo Cambauva

emails.subject: ENC: Mexico Retail: Industry February SSS - Soft, But Trailing In-line With Conservative View
emails.one_liner: Mexico Retail Feb SSS +1.1% y/y, soft but inline with conservative view. TBBB remains top pick (OW).
emails.body:
________________________________

De: Joe Giordano & Froy Mendez <joseph.giordano@jpmresearchmail.com>
Enviado: quinta-feira, 19 de março de 2026 19:21:01 (UTC-03:00) Brasilia
Para: Sell Side <sellside@torkcapital.com.br>
Assunto: Mexico Retail: Industry February SSS - Soft, But Trailing In-line With Conservative View


 	Latin America Equity Research 	

The Extel survey (formerly Institutional Investor) is open through March 20 (TOMORROW) and is VERY important to us. Please support us in the following categories: 1) RETAIL, 2) FOOD & BEVERAGES and 3) HEALTHCARE by giving a 5-star rating. We are focused on the LATIN AMERICA survey (not the Brazil survey). To vote, please click the banner below.

Thanks!
Joe, Froy & Nico



Mexico Retail


Industry February SSS - Soft, But Trailing In-line With Conservative View

 	 
 <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12827605&ad=4067865694&h=bZBR4X0&sig=1zi8Mzbmw0dDnIe7nO8pTEPNLOX&v=2&url=https://www.extelinsights.com/voting> 	
By Joseph Giordano AC <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12827605&ad=4067865694&h=1oafVmh&sig=CnM7j4dOLB8XWc33yhhVi6DYocd&v=2&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/analyst/R103209> , Froylan Mendez AC <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12827605&ad=4067865694&h=OJFKx_a&sig=dysfjM5wx7vYTioXRdkBoo2Hl2&v=2&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/analyst/V579633> , Nicolas Larrain <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12827605&ad=4067865694&h=5k1WB8K&sig=NalrtIPC0tAdc0vQSnBvnEGrGaQ&v=2&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/analyst/R596032> , Guilherme A. Vilela, CFA <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12827605&ad=4067865694&h=O-axWR_&sig=NqVdYF6TQvQI2zm8guEjLNhxsTs&v=2&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/analyst/R720687> , Giovanni Vescovi <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12827605&ad=4067865694&h=cUz-wv5&sig=QJbS5lwd3CltC7uNKDwaPQv1dDy&v=2&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/analyst/O798110>  

Click here for the full document and disclaimers <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12827605&ad=4067865694&h=n0St1De&sig=82VqriNlAW0ExObAfP1KpVRCb9a&v=2&isig=VPzDhuFRBFPpiGqDNsOeFpuJEk8&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/email/scx/-ikkr3p3/GPS-5240968-0/38751416-2b49-46db-a676-f2d882e6a092> 

ANTAD’s February SSS grew 1.1% y/y, down from 2.0% in January 2026. Total sales growth of +3.1% y/y in February decelerated 220bps from January’s pace. Within segments, Specialized Stores were the only segment to accelerate versus January, while Department Stores remained in negative territory and Supermarkets, though soft, performed in line with our 1Q26 expectations, which anticipate an acceleration compared to 4Q25. Overall, February’s reading supports our view that discretionary consumption is the most at risk as consumers face higher price elasticity (here <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12827605&ad=4067865694&h=GgUhbLv&sig=3racKq6QTWhYoq9P4FzlBUgV3SQ&v=2&isig=TaOKx1BkX7tLMfcPq9APyYuld7d&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/email/scx/78e64nlv/GPS-5137865-0/38751416-2b49-46db-a676-f2d882e6a092> ) than in 2025, while the pace of recovery in basic consumption remains soft. With this in mind, we reiterate our preference for OW-rated TBBB, which remains our top sector pick given its good value proposition in the current environment with solid mature SSS underscoring solid volume and wallet share gain. 

*	Supermarkets SSS posted headline growth of +1.1% y/y in February, following 2.9% in January, with no calendar effect for the month. If momentum in February holds for March, it would imply SSS for 1Q could be at 2.8%. This compares to ~5.0% on average expected for our covered Co avg (ex. Soriana), a 2.5pp outperformance vs ANTAD — in line with YTD outperformance. 
*	Department Stores trends remained in negative territory for the third month in a row with headline and calendar-adjusted figures -0.7%/-4.1%, respectively. The calendar-adjusted figure represents an improving trend vs. January’s -6.2%, yet is still in negative territory. This supports our view that discretionary spending is at risk considering the loss of cyclical income and savings depletion during 2025. 
*	Specialized Stores posted 3.4% y/y growth in February, up from 3.2% in January but down from 3.8% in 4Q25. While the correlation is loose, this compares to the 3.3% we currently forecast for FEMSA in 1Q26E, which implies an acceleration vs. 2.1% in 4Q25. 

Figure 1: Same-Store-Sales Growth by Store Type

emails.subject: ENC: Latam Daily Briefing 2026-03-19
emails.one_liner: Latam Macro: Brazil fiscal spending impact R$215bn, Argentina unemployment 7.5%, Chile inflation, Colombia trade/pension reform, upcoming events.
emails.body:
________________________________

De: BTG Pactual Macro Research <sh-btgpactualmacroresearch@btgpactual.com>
Enviado: quinta-feira, 19 de março de 2026 19:05:15 (UTC-03:00) Brasilia
Assunto: Latam Daily Briefing 2026-03-19



BTG Pactual | Macro Research 


Macro High Frequency Note 


Macroeconomic Research - Latam Daily 


19-March-2026 


 


Latam Daily Briefing 


Read the full document here >> <https://www.btgpactual.com/research/report-view/118833>  

Getting to the point 

Brazil

Brazil’s fiscal imbalance is largely rooted in the rapid growth of mandatory spending. In this report, we focus on the role of expenditure indexation, which affects roughly 45% of federal primary spending. We estimate that the changes introduced by the Transition Amendment to indexation rules will increase federal primary spending by R$215bn by 2026, relative to a counterfactual scenario with more moderate rules. This reflects primarily the reinstatement of the minimum wage adjustment rule, the retroactive recalculation of health and education spending floors with indexation to revenue, and the expansion of congressional earmarks. Under this scenario, real primary spending growth reaches 21% over the period, compared with 11% in the counterfactual. For the 2027–2034 period, we simulate a set of changes to indexation rules, including linking social benefits to inflation, adjusting health and education floors by inflation and population growth, partially de-linking Fundeb and the FCDF, and keeping congressional earmarks flat in nominal terms. Under these assumptions, primary spending would be R$355bn lower by 2034, with the primary balance reaching 2% of GDP, compared with 0.5% under current rules, and real spending growth slowing to 9%, versus 19% if current rules remain unchanged. In this scenario, assuming a 4.5% real interest rate, public debt would stabilize at around 88% of GDP by 2030. (Full story here) <https://www.btgpactual.com/research/report-view/118823> 

Argentina

The unemployment rate jumped from 6.6% in Q3 to 7.5% in the last quarter of 2025. The jobless rate also grew significantly y/y coming from 6.4% in the last quarter of 2024. In the last year, the participation rate fell marginally from 48.8% to 48.6%, but the employment rate declined from 45.7% to just 45%. This rate is not as bad as it may look, as the 2024 employment rate was the highest on record for the fourth quarter, and 2025’s is the second highest. Also, the 6.6% unemployment rate in Q3 was surprisingly low given the state of the economy, with all the electoral turmoil. (Full story here) <https://www.btgpactual.com/research/report-view/118818> 

Tomorrow's events:
GDP YoY (4Q) - 04:00 PM
GDP QoQ (4Q) - 04:00 PM

Chile

Market participants now expect March inflation to reach 0.6% m/m, representing a 0.2pp increase relative to the previous survey. The BCCh released the results of the pre-March monetary policy meeting Traders Survey, showing that short-term inflation expectations stand at 0.6% m/m for March, followed by 0.5% m/m in April and 0.2% m/m in May. Longer-term expectations have risen notably, with the one-year horizon increasing from 2.9% y/y to 3.5% y/y, while the two-year horizon stands at 3.1% y/y. Regarding monetary policy, participants no longer anticipate a rate cut at the March 24 meeting and expect the MPR to remain at 4.5% over the next four monetary policy meetings. At the 12-month horizon, the policy rate is projected at 4.25%, before returning to 4.5% at the 24-month horizon. In the foreign exchange market, expectations point to the CLP trading around 910 CLP/USD over the next seven days, followed by a moderate appreciation toward approximately 900 CLP/USD over the subsequent 28-day horizon.

Colombia

According to DANE, imports reached US$5.9bn CIF in Jan-26, up 9.7% y/y (vs. US$5.4bn in Jan-25), driven mainly by manufacture (+14.1% y/y; +10.5p.p. contribution), particularly machinery and transport equipment. Agricultural goods, food and beverages also contributed positively (+13.2% y/y; +1.9p.p.), while fuels and extractives declined (-25.3% y/y; -2.7p.p.), partially offsetting the headline print. In terms of composition, manufactures accounted for 77.7% of total imports, followed by agriculture (14.9%) and fuels (7.3%).

As per DANE, the January trade deficit was to US$1.3bn FOB (vs. near balance at ~US$1.2mn a year earlier). Imports accounted for US$5.6bn, while exports clocked at US$4.3bn.

Banrep warned that the draft decree allowing the transfer of COP25tn in pension savings from private funds (AFPs) to Colpensiones would likely be illegal, as it contradicts Law 2381/2024, which permits regime switching but explicitly requires that assets remain managed by AFPs until pension rights are realized; the proposal instead mandates the transfer of all balances (capital + returns) within 15 business days, effectively exceeding regulatory powers and altering the law’s scope.

events.body:
________________________________

De: Yuri R Fernandes <yuri.r.fernandes@jpmresearchmail.com>

 <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12835603&ad=4073424274&h=bZBR4X0&sig=KOSxWK9I2REWMwO5Fr3G8ADfqGx&v=2&url=https://www.extelinsights.com/voting> 	
 	LATIN AMERICA EQUITY RESEARCH 	
YOU'RE INVITED 		
 	 
JPM Global Platform Series – AI Impact on Brokers/Asset Managers & Exchanges Competition 
 	 

 	 
	
 	 
Date & Time	 
 	 
March 20, 2026 at 2pm EST / 3pm BRT / 6pm UK 
Pre-Registration required: Please register your attendance. 
 	 
REGISTER <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12835603&ad=4073424274&h=0ueyzWV&sig=PI65qfkP2uK6FFNVe8ypmBt0jFy&v=2&url=https://jpmchase.zoom.us/webinar/register/WN_Mxw_VtmLRxyzy66PCG9aJQ%23/registration>

Replay will be available after the call. 
Find it on J.P. Morgan Markets Multimedia Page <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12835603&ad=4073424274&h=gmadEws&sig=EEY7sqJNKoHVq42lL7ZF3P5cll5&v=2&url=https://www.jpmm.com/%23research.browse.multimedia>  once available. 

We have been busy. After hosting 45+ calls as part of our Brazil CEO Series, LatAm ex-Brazil CEO Series, LatAm Fintech Series, we are now launching our JPM Global Platform Series – please pre-register using the links below. We plan to host meetings with J.P. Morgan global analysts to offer cross-reads of global discussions into LatAm. Notably, we plan to explore the AI winner vs. loser debate in the US for banks, software, brokers and asset managers, payments and stablecoins, Capital One credit card business model, US fintech landscape, exchange competition, among other things. We also summarize below our recent calls with replays in case you find it helpful. 

We hope you can join us! 
Yuri, Grespan, Fernanda, and Diego 
​

LatAm Fintech Series

*	Clip: Focus On Efficiency, New Products and Revenue Diversification <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12835603&ad=4073424274&h=ed__vY7&sig=4Fe7VQ0OI8mrk0oRN7Q25KtjJRZ&v=2&isig=Rn4gFpWyAE4lg7CUN3Uxg6Rln3Z&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/email/scx/i3qcpk06/GPS-5214991-0/ddcec79c-fb81-43c2-b8bf-c82d5fb39ff0>  (replay <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12835603&ad=4073424274&h=9mi71Z8&sig=EHDrPB3FhP1AIA4NJG5Jab6CvVr&v=2&isig=ZGOcCQ9tSISFSgh8uxNe0qo4uHj&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/email/scx/ijfgki99/KAL-1-0U1RN3OG/ddcec79c-fb81-43c2-b8bf-c82d5fb39ff0> )

events.one_liner: Webinar sobre GLP-1 no Brasil com Close-Up International; aborda tendências de mercado e patente de semaglutida.
events.body:
________________________________

De: Joseph Giordano <joseph.giordano@jpmresearchmail.com>
Enviado: sexta-feira, 20 de março de 2026 11:01:37 (UTC-03:00) Brasilia
Para: Sell Side <sellside@torkcapital.com.br>
Assunto: *STARTING NOW* Webinar with Close-Up International: GLP-1 in Brazil


 <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12833695&ad=4072284481&h=bZBR4X0&sig=6lCK3x1dsd6ezbXIAOJBeBGosZW&v=2&url=https://www.extelinsights.com/voting> 	
 	LATIN AMERICA EQUITY RESEARCH 	
YOU'RE INVITED 		
 	 
*STARTING NOW* Webinar with Close-Up International: GLP-1 in Brazil 
 	 
Hi Tork, 

You’re invited to join Joseph Giordano, JPM LatAm Healthcare Equity Analyst, for a conversation with Close-Up International’s Commercial Director, Carlos Aguiar. 

Close-Up International is a leading intelligence provider that has ~100k audited pharmacies in Brazil that enables high frequency data with TAM accuracy. Among the tracked data we highlight sell-in and sell-out data of GLP-1 drugs in Brazil. In this webinar, we will discuss the main trends across GLP-1 drugs in Brazil, semaglutide patent expiry, and the implications for pharma players. 

We encourage you to send any questions you may have in advance by replying to this email. 

 	 
	
 	 
Date & Time	 
 	 
Friday, Mar. 20th @ 10AM ET 
Pre-Registration required: Please register your attendance.

events.subject: ENC: [In One Hour] Webinar with Close-Up International: GLP-1 in Brazil
events.one_liner: Webinar sobre GLP-1 no Brasil, patente semaglutida e implicações para farmacêuticas com Close-Up International.
events.body:
________________________________

De: Joseph Giordano <joseph.giordano@jpmresearchmail.com>
Enviado: sexta-feira, 20 de março de 2026 10:01:46 (UTC-03:00) Brasilia
Para: Sell Side <sellside@torkcapital.com.br>
Assunto: [In One Hour] Webinar with Close-Up International: GLP-1 in Brazil


 <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12832883&ad=4071616678&h=bZBR4X0&sig=L7kGYeOISv3V3IwYD4Ec8DTy3ZA&v=2&url=https://www.extelinsights.com/voting> 	
 	LATIN AMERICA EQUITY RESEARCH 	
YOU'RE INVITED 		
 	 
[In One Hour] Webinar with Close-Up International: GLP-1 in Brazil 
 	 
Hi Tork, 

You’re invited to join Joseph Giordano, JPM LatAm Healthcare Equity Analyst, for a conversation with Close-Up International’s Commercial Director, Carlos Aguiar. 

Close-Up International is a leading intelligence provider that has ~100k audited pharmacies in Brazil that enables high frequency data with TAM accuracy. Among the tracked data we highlight sell-in and sell-out data of GLP-1 drugs in Brazil. In this webinar, we will discuss the main trends across GLP-1 drugs in Brazil, semaglutide patent expiry, and the implications for pharma players. 

We encourage you to send any questions you may have in advance by replying to this email. 

 	 
	
 	 
Date & Time	 
 	 
Friday, Mar. 20th @ 10AM ET 
Pre-Registration required: Please register your attendance. 
 	 
REGISTER <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12832883&ad=4071616678&h=MLC8HMf&sig=COix5KpCd0qkHsqhKCz9Sn9YdzJ&v=2&url=https://jpmchase.zoom.us/webinar/register/WN_X_T5G-X0S-OUiDKILYmH5A%23/registration>

events.subject: ENC: Agentic Commerce: a discussion with JD Sports
events.one_liner: Webinar com JD Sports sobre Agentic Commerce e AI para compras de clientes nos EUA.
events.body:
________________________________

De: Joseph Giordano <joseph.giordano@jpmresearchmail.com>
Enviado: sexta-feira, 20 de março de 2026 09:36:27 (UTC-03:00) Brasilia
Para: Sell Side <sellside@torkcapital.com.br>
Assunto: Agentic Commerce: a discussion with JD Sports


 <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12832578&ad=4071433062&h=bZBR4X0&sig=M3zWZj6QCTkoY5IeGvbzVVwnjP3&v=2&url=https://www.extelinsights.com/voting> 	
 	LATIN AMERICA EQUITY RESEARCH 	
YOU'RE INVITED 		
 	 
Agentic Commerce: a discussion with JD Sports 
 	 

We wanted to flag to you the following webinar on Agentic Commerce with JD Sports that will be hosted by our EU General Retail and Luxury & Sporting Goods team. You can also read the following note from the team for reference (Change Agents <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12832578&ad=4071433062&h=SsLaoud&sig=UPsIJ8Nj4dITcj55ybsvEGxUf3e&v=2&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/email/scx/-lh0svb6/GPS-5176181-0/65b060bd-9e62-4750-bcab-6f63e251850c> ) 

The research team will host JD Sports Chief Technology & Transformation Office, Jetan Chowk and Group Director of Product, Antonia Hansen. The JD Sports team will discuss the group’s recent announcement that it will be enabling US customers to purchase goods from JD directly through AI agents (without leaving the agents’ apps) in the coming months. It is an opportunity to better understand key considerations for retailers as they prepare for a potential consumer shift towards agentic commerce. 

Lastly, we wanted to say that Today is the LAST DAY for the LatAm Extel I.I. survey. ________________________________

 	LATIN AMERICA EQUITY RESEARCH 	
YOU'RE INVITED 		
 	 
Brazil Fuel Market Irregularities: What’s Next? - With Henry Hadid (Vibra’s VP of Legal, Compliance, and Institutional Relations) - Portuguese Only 
 	 

The debate around irregularities and regulatory enforcement continues to shape the sector’s outlook, with implications for pricing, supply chains, and competition dynamics – especially for leading companies like Vibra. In this context, we invite you to a webinar with Henry Hadid (Vibra’s VP of Legal, Compliance, and Institutional Relations) to gain a clearer view of recent industry updates and the ways in which evolving market dynamics could impact Vibra. You are welcome to submit questions in advance. We look forward to your participation. 

*We need your help ! EVERY vote counts* The Extel/Institutional Investor survey is almost closing, and our team is competing in three equally important categories (Oil & Gas / Metals & Mining / Pulp & Paper). Please vote for us in recognition of our work and our in‑depth coverage of the Middle East conflict. In the LatAm survey (not Brazil), please rate J.P. Morgan 5 stars – Rodolfo Angele / Milene Carvalho / Henrique Cunha / Tathiane Candini.

________________________________

Assunto: Convite JPM | Novonesis w/ Corn Ethanol Experts - Meeting In-person



Hi, 

 

JPM invites you to an in person meeting with corn ethanol experts from Novonesis. 

 

Novonesis is a Danish industrial biotech that makes enzyme and microbe biosolutions for food, agriculture, energy, and home care. In Brazil, Novonesis holds over 90% market share in corn ethanol enzymes and possesses extensive expertise in the sector’s competitive dynamics.

 

Monday, March 23rd @ 10am BRT.

 

Click here <mailto:cynthia.cs.sztaynbaum@jpmorgan.com?subject=JPM%20-%20Novonesis>  to confirm.

events.body:
________________________________

De: Lucas Ferreira & Guilherme Mendes <lucas.x.ferreira@jpmresearchmail.com>
Enviado: sexta-feira, 20 de março de 2026 08:01:44 (UTC-03:00) Brasilia
Para: Sell Side <sellside@torkcapital.com.br>
Assunto: *IN ONE HOUR* 2026 Protein & Grains Outlook: Webinar with Rural Ventures


 <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=12830964&ad=4070462484&h=bZBR4X0&sig=VsgqelTmaups7D3bxPlRgCQ13BK&v=2&url=https://www.extelinsights.com/voting> 	
 	LATIN AMERICA EQUITY RESEARCH 	
YOU'RE INVITED 		
 	 
*IN ONE HOUR* 2026 Protein & Grains Outlook: Webinar with Rural Ventures 
 	 

Please join Lucas Ferreira, JPM LatAm F&B and Agribusiness Lead Equity Analyst, and Guilherme Mendes, JPM LatAm Transportation Equity Lead Analyst, for a webinar with Rafael Harada, Partner and Consultancy Head at Rural Ventures, and Marco Barros Sampaio, Partner at Rural Ventures, on the 2026 outlook for US and Brazil protein and agribusiness sectors. 

Rafael Harada and Marco Barros Sampaio spent over 20 years at JBS, mainly in the US, where they held senior leadership positions including Global Head of Risk Management and Head of Risk Management and Trading. Currently, both partners work together at Rural Ventures, an agribusiness ecosystem that connects over 600 startups, 350 corporations, and 200+ investors through venture capital, consultancy, and strategic partnerships across the agricultural value chain. 
​ 

We encourage you to send any questions you may have in advance by replying to this email. 

Best,

Lucas, Froy & Lari + Guilherme & Julia

You are welcome to submit questions in advance. We look forward to your participation. 

*We need your help ! EVERY vote counts* The Extel/Institutional Investor survey is almost closing, and our team is competing in three equally important categories (Oil & Gas / Metals & Mining / Pulp & Paper). Please vote for us in recognition of our work and our in‑depth coverage of the Middle East conflict. In the LatAm survey (not Brazil), please rate J.P. Morgan 5 stars – Rodolfo Angele / Milene Carvalho / Henrique Cunha / Tathiane Candini.

events.body:
________________________________

De: Yu, Gary (Research) <gary.yu@morganstanley.com>
Enviado: quinta-feira, 19 de março de 2026 23:29:26 (UTC-03:00) Brasilia
Para: Sell Side <sellside@torkcapital.com.br>
Assunto: 今天 上午 11:00 (香港时间) 中国AI云发展趋势展望；腾讯及阿里业绩解读| 摩根士丹利 “新经济” 板块热点前瞻



 

  <https://ny.matrix.ms.com/eqr/research/portal/home/asiaPacific> 

	
 <https://ny.matrix.ms.com/eqr/research/portal/collection/d7109f07-2dce-418c-9945-d6ed176f0e18?isTheme=false> 

 

中国AI云发展趋势展望
腾讯及阿里业绩解读

 

我们聚焦中国AI云的中长期发展路径，分析其市场规模扩张、竞争格局重塑，以及利润率与价格进入上行周期的驱动因素。
同时，我们也将解读腾讯与阿里的业绩表现。

 

3月20日（周五）

上午11点 (香港时间)

 

+加入行事历 <https://morganstanley.webcasts.com/viewer/vcs.jsp?ei=1754839&reminderid=0>

中国互联网行业分析师
